author: dwelle

title: state rewrite
body:
(Writing down for discussion. May first require moving the core away from current react class implem.)

# current state

By nature of our main App component being a class component, we've been bundling all manner of unrelated state into a single `AppState`.

This causes problems because there's no clear delineation of what is a canvas state vs editor state, what is public vs private state, and what's persistent vs ephemeral state.

In many cases the categorization isn't clear, and there's a lot of overlap, but we should redesign it so that it solves these things:

1. it's more clear what's part of the canvas state. What is persitable (file/storage), and what the user/host app should be taking care of (listen on to store, and later set/update when restoring data).
2. reduce state update race conditions stemming from (1).
3. reduce state scope (for perf and complexity reasons)

One example is `appState.errorMessage`. This is ephemeral state, editor, and mostly private state (but it should still be settable from host). As a result of not differentiating it from other state right now, we have a race condition where if the host app (or the editor) sets an error before initial scene is finished loading, it gets overwritten — either by our `defaultAppState` on init, or by host app overwriting it if they restore whatever they persist to DB into a fully-qualified AppState.

# next

Unclear what exactly we should do. Most likely split state into multiple states. Some state, such as ephemeral, private, editor state, shouldn't even be exposed to host apps (apart from getters/setters where applicable). It may also be localized to components that need it. For example, an `errorMessage` only needs a setter and a presentation component that can be completely separate from the rest of the UI.

author: anatoliibakhtin

title: Full-text search in whiteboard
body:
With huge and text-heavy whiteboards, you sometimes forget where a certain piece of text is located. In such situations, a full-text search would come to the rescue.

To initiate the text search, we could use the default keyboard shortcut in browsers (ctrl+f) or a dedicated button near the zoom and undo/redo buttons. When the text is found, the corresponding text field on the whiteboard should be centred (like shift+2 does).

To be sure, I'm not proposing to have a search function to search for saved whiteboards (by their title and description) in [Excalidraw Plus](https://plus.excalidraw.com/excalidraw-plus-vs-excalidraw), rather I'm speaking about the ability to search for text within an individual whiteboard, when editing it. Although, a global text search in the Excalidraw Plus interface for all saved whiteboards, showing the found text as a snippet for context, would be handy as well.

Edit: Somehow I overlooked similar requests, linking them here for the sake of overview:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5435
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/3826

title: After socket reconnection, I am wondering if there is an intention not to send additional ExcalidrawElement to the client.
body:
Hello, I am a student who is interested in excalidraw.
I found that a client with missing ExcalidrawElement occurred while building a project with friends studying together and using it.

At first, a race condition occurred due to the pseudo-p2p model in which the element state is not saved in the server, and I thought that the method of dropping all of them in the problem client was adopted.

[https://blog.excalidraw.com/building-excalidraw-p2p-collaboration-feature/]
I read the mechanism of and confirmed that even if the problem occurs, it is handled as a versionNonce.
Also, since it is a pseudo-p2p model and not a p2p model, I thought that the race condition itself would not occur because there is a Socket io server that adopts the TCP protocol.

The phenomenon was not easily reproduced, so I further analyzed it at the code level, but I guessed that some ExcalidrawElements were not missing, but that the socket was disconnected. The reconnection default setting value is true, so socket reconnection will proceed, but if the socket is disconnected, the updated ExcalidrawElement will not be received, and the issue will be reproduced.

After socket reconnection, I am wondering if there is an intention not to send additional ExcalidrawElement to the client, so I leave an email.
(If someone doesn't take additional notes, I think it's a situation where a client with other ExcalidrawElements can exist.)
author: cshacker-web

title: Remove component rendering from Action objects
body:
We should separate action logic from UI.

In other words, remove the `PanelComponent` from the Action objects (and remove `actionManager.renderAction()`.

- there's no underlying rule for whether a component that's updating state should have an action and whether its UI should be inside an action or not. Results in inconsistency. It's also messy and adds complexity.
- we'll definitely need to do this at the point of decoupling editor from the UI layer (react), anyway
- and it's buggy #6074

That said, having a single point for determining whether a specific UI should be rendered or not (in contextMenu or elsewhere in the UI) is a cool concept so we should figure out a replacement for this.

author: vulcan-spacemage

title: render rest children to LayerUI
body:
Right now in excalidraw.com we render some app components outside `<Excalidraw/>` but use internal components.

For example, the `<Collab/>` dialog uses `<Dialog/>`, but is rendered outside. So context and atoms (potentially) won't be set correctly.

Thus, this does nothing at best:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/618442299f85229a0e5c1fdb7c3bb9aeaa850a50/src/components/Dialog.tsx#L78-L79

When used inside the `<RoomDialog/>`:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/618442299f85229a0e5c1fdb7c3bb9aeaa850a50/src/excalidraw-app/collab/RoomDialog.tsx#L178-L185

We should render app-custom `children` passed to Excalidraw to `<LayerUI/>` as is, so we can move the `<Collab/>` inside `<Excalidraw/>`.

Longer-term we'll may introduce an `<ExcalidrawContext/>`, but it's unclear how and whether it will work, and in some cases like the this issue it's unnecessary.

author: AndreychukAI

title: Rewriting Sidebar API
body:
- [ ] support for sidebar tabs
- [ ] migrate to Excalidraw child (remove `props.renderSidebar`)
- [ ] rewrite state handling

# component proposal

```jsx
<Sidebar>
  <Sidebar.Trigger>Custom togle</Sidebar.Trigger>
  <Sidebar.Tabs>
    <Sidebar.TabContent name="library">
      {/* ... */}
    </Sidebar.TabContent>
    <Sidebar.TabContent name="comments">
      {/* ... */}
    </Sidebar.TabContent>
    <Sidebar.TabContent name="frames">
      {/* ... */}
    </Sidebar.TabContent>
    <Sidebar.TabList>
      <Sidebar.TabTrigger name="library" />
      <Sidebar.TabTrigger name="comments" />
      <Sidebar.TabTrigger name="frames" />
    </Sidebar.TabList>
  </Sidebar.Tabs>
</Sidebar>
```

(component naming & structure TBD)

- we'd be passing the component always (same as `props.renderSidebar()`), and the editor would render it based on state
- `appState.openSidebar` can stay
- change `appState.openSidebar` state to `string | null` (could be narrowed down to host-enabled tabs if we figure out how to do it)
- `null` means closed sidebar
- `string` would open sidebar with the tab it's set to. E.g. `library` or `comments`
- we either remove `toggleMenu` for now, or we change its signature to `{ type: "sidebar", tab: string }`

author: meowyx

title: customizing ContextMenu
body:
We should be slowly decoupling the editor from UI layer ( https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6075), so we can't be adding contextMenu related components or icons and other things into the Actions objects themselves.

As such, we have two options for custom contextMenu:

1. have the editor take care of rendering the items from the Action objects configuration. This will prevent UI customization such as custom icons (if we introduce them to contextMenu items), and we'd also need to come up with DSL for grouping, nesting and similar.
2. build ContextMenu similar to `<MainMenu/>`.

Laying it out, I think it's actually clear we should go with (2). But we'll also need to modify Actions so that we can render all action items and they filter themselves out based on `type: "canvas" | "element"` and other predicates we use to conditionally render contextMenu items in `App._getContextMenuItems()`.

We may also consider adding support for opening contextMenu anywhere. Let's say a host app (or the editor itself) adds `onContextMenu` on an arbitrary DOM node, it should then be able to open the contextMenu with arbitrary items.
author: dwelle

title: toolbar tool grouping/dropdown
body:
We might want to support grouping toolbar tools.

### Use cases:

- hiding less often used tools by grouping with (under) others
- adding support for tools which we wouldn't otherwise consider if we had to expose them on the main toolbar
- adding tool modifiers (switching between tool modes)

### Implementations:

1. **click-to-choose**: clicking a tool will always open the dropdown. We'd definitely auto-pick the active tool so you could ignore the dropdown if you didn't want to switch tools.

    **upsides:** 100% discoverable
    **downsides:** annoying if 90% of the time you don't want to switch.
3. **click-on-arrow-to-choose**: split the tool into the button and an arrow. Only clicking on the arrow opens the dropdown. 

    This is what Figma below is using. They also do not switch tools when you only click on the arrow, which I think is bad UX because it's not often clear what you're clicking on.

    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/213866423-7d5a821a-2e19-4d4d-a675-757b0cb81f07.png)

    **upsides:** discoverable
    **downsides:** Annoying if you misclick. It takes more space. It's not as pretty.
4. **hold-to-choose**: clicking doesn't open the dropdown. You need to click-hold pointer for an interval (~500ms+).

    Below, Affinity Designer:
  
    ![affinity-toolbar-dropdown](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/213866460-e3ececbf-2155-41e6-bf2e-a2bc1c7fb60c.gif)

    **upsides:** not annoying if you rarely switch
    **downsides:** least discoverable (even if we still render an arrow for grouped tools).

5. **slide-to-choose**: modification of (3), where you can also drag-slide to open the dropdown. We should likely do this on top of (3), not exclusively, because it's rather hidden behavior. But it could be useful if you don't want to wait the delay period. 

    **upsides:** same as (3) with a bonus UX if needed
    **downsides:** same as (3)

title: Broken emoji in rectangle if open in some browsers
body:
I has been using shared canvas for a while without any problem, however, recently I've encountered a situation when (a new group of) people opening canvas/scene in some browsers broke an emoji placed in a rectangle (for all the collaborators). "😃" (and any other used "smile" as well) was replaced by "\ud83d\n\ude03" (or an equivalent).

Before:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/148013/213888337-117d3b4e-d212-4054-83df-b1caa7db752e.png)

> "text":"😃" and "originalText":"😃"

author: TvoozMagnificent

title: move from `ref` to `excalidrawAPI` prop
body:
The `ref` prop to get the `excalidrawAPI` has at least two issues:

1. Doesn't work with Next.js `lazy` (you have to revert to hacks like these .https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/3849#discussioncomment-1026558)
2. If a function is passed, the argument's type should be `excalidrawAPI` instead of `ExcalidrawAPIRefValue`. 
  
    ```jsx
    <Excalidraw
      ref={(api) => {
          // ^ should be `ExcalidrawImperativeAPI`, not `ExcalidrawAPIRefValue | null`
      }}
    />
    ```

3. Clarify naming. `ref` is not clear what you should expect.

(2) could possible be fixed by using a custom type instead of `<forwardRef>`, but (1) is still a problem.

----

We should move to `excalidrawAPI` prop.

author: dwelle

title: feature request: ability to make scale drawings
body:
I think it would be really useful to be able to create scale drawings in excalidraw. This could be done in a unit-less manner so you don't need to be bogged down by mapping pixels to physical units or anything like that. The core feature would be the ability to say that this line is twice as big as some other line, or that it is _x units_ in length. 

My use-case would be to create scale plans of rooms and furniture. I typically do this by hand: drawing the room and furniture to scale, then i cut out pieces of furniture with scissors, so that i can move things around in the room without having to physically move furniture. This can save a lot of physical effort, but it's slow to produce and easy to lose/damage paper. It would be super cool to have an excalidraw file that is a plan of each room of my house and each piece of furniture, so that I can return to whenever i decide to change a room's layout (or buy some new furniture).

This would not doesn't require absolute precision, so it's not verging into CAD territory or anything like that. I imagine you already have some logical dimensions in the app's internals? So this might be a case of exposing those values as editable in the UI

People can generally write text next to a place, but the drawing/diagram would become disrupted.
I noticed we have comments but it doesn't work for externals 

It would be cool if a person could change the drawing as a suggestion (ie. not committed) but that's going outside the point.

My pain is that right now if I share my diagram, the only way people can leave me feedback is through team members.

This behavior is converted in visual collaboration boards like Miro and Figjam.
Where they have also other collaboration features like timers and voting on elements.

I would like it if I only use one tool for visual collaboration. you currently don't fulfill a big chunk of that need

author: huzaifahj

title: split canvas rendering
body:
For performance reasons, we should have at least 2 `<canvas>` elements:

1. one for elements (shapes) themselves. Sitting below.
2. one for UI things, such as selection/bounding boxes, remote client cursors, etc. Sitting above.

UI-related things change more often than elements, which affects performance in hot paths such as displaying remote client cursors on movement.

author: harshsinghatz

title: support easing & zoom in `scrollToContent()`
body:
We should add `opts` as 2nd parameter to `scrollToContent` in `src/components/App.tsx`, and support these options:

1. `opts.fitToContent: boolean` to fit zoom to target element(s). Let's reuse `zoomToFitElements` from `src/actions/actionCanvas.tsx`.
2. `opts.animate: boolean` to animate the transition instead of it being instant. I'd probably go with an out -exponential easing function: https://github.com/kaelzhang/easing-functions/blob/1250bf0d830b1367e17eab1fa59b09d25643bc1b/index.js#L294-L305

    In other words, if `animate` is true, it should split the transition into X number of steps across Y number of `ms`, e.g. ~24fps, and move the viewport to the target element across those frames.

    We may be having perf issues on large scenes and may need to adapt, but we'll see.

    If `opts.fitToContent` is `true` at the same time, it should take this into account and animate the zoom in/out as well.

    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/221403395-34e7e837-4464-40af-b46b-afd20ddb7e7f.png)

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] support `opts.fitToContent` (reuse `zoomToFitElements` from `src/actions/actionCanvas.tsx`)
- [ ] support `opts.animate`
```

title: Feature Request: Lasso Selection (free selection)
body:
I would love to see a feature for selecting elements (vectors, arrows, images, text...) in the canvas using a lasso selection, sometimes called free hand selection tool.

## How it could work

The user would choose the tool from the toolbar, click and drag either with a mouse or stylus button on the canvas ('Start' in picture) and encircle ('path ->') the elements (here yellow boxes) they would like to select. The selection is always a closed shape which gets completed by a straight line from the current mouse/stylus position to the coordinates where the selection has begun (blue in picture). Only shapes that are completely surrounded by the lasso are selected upon releasing the mouse/stylus.

This behavior is analogous to OneNote for Windows 10, where this screenshot is taken from:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/55597910/224867065-9d479c04-b3c4-4b2d-b896-094d843c072d.png)

## What it might be used for

- Selecting multiple elements that are not aligned in a rectangular pattern
- Selecting handwritten text snippets from larger bodies of handwritten text
- Quick and accurate element selection using a stylus

## Note on styli

In addition to supporting the selection as a tool in the toolbar, it was amazing if using the lasso could also be accomplished by pressing and holding a button on the stylus while having the pen tool selected.

## Another great addition

... would be if the area a vector would take if it was an enclosed shape (connect start and end of stroke) was calculated and the vector and only selected it if the lasso area (blue) covered at least p% (p being a configurable threshold) of the whole shape area. In the picture, the area covered by the lasso is green due to the overlap of the blue selection area and the yellow shape area, so green would have to be at least p% of green + yellow (without green) for the vector to be selected.

This would especially be useful for selecting a row in a block of handwritten text as letters such as `p` or `q` might get cut off if their tails are not properly enclosed by the lasso.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/55597910/224870628-12e85504-3bb1-45d9-ba39-e1c09380fcb2.png)

To avoid cutting of the i-dots, the user has not put the selection as low as he should have had to select p, q and f.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/55597910/224871130-357a0805-93d5-4f7a-bc32-5e7d24dbec3a.png)

I understand that this is probably a tremendous amount of work but it would greatly improve the UX and make Excalidraw more usable for handwritten text.

title: Feature Request: "Scroll/panning border" API option
body:
### Usecase

- with read-only links you can embed scenes into docs, notion etc., we still allow panning on that scene but users may get lost if it pans too far. I would like to create some kind of "Border" which will not allow panning any further so users would not get lost.

### Implementation consideration
- it needs to be in dynamic nature due zooming and "edges" (be able to get into edges)
- still allow panning when you have completely zoomed out but the threshold for blocking it
- maybe some kind of feedback animation instead of "flat" block (?)
- when a user tries to repeatedly "go beyond" display some tooltip/msg (?) 

Behavior like: https://jsoncrack.com/editor

title: Full Screen Support on Tablets via `apple-mobile-web-app-capable`
body:
Absent a true iOS app, it would be great if I could open the app fullscreen on my iPad.  I believe the way we are supposed to to this is to 
* Open the page
* Go to "Share"
* Select "Add to Home Screen"

From that point, if the app supports it, you will see the app as a full screen application when you click on the shortcut.

To support this, I believe the app would need to contain this HTML `meta` tag:

```html
<meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes">
```
I would be happy to volunteer to implement this if everyone agrees.

title: Shortcut to edit shape text
body:
Proposed User Flow

1. Select a rectangle
2. Press `ctrl+enter` to focus the rectangle's text area

Currently, the only way of editing the text area of a shape is 

1. ~select the shape~
2. double click roughly in the middle of the shape

~This becomes particularly frustrating for medium and larger-sided shapes where unless the shape is already selected, the centre of the rectangle isn't part of the hitbox to select it.~ I have just found out that I don't need to have the shape pre-selected.

For cases where you've already got the shape selected (eg. after having just drawn it) `ctrl+enter` is easier than trying to click the center of the shape (especially when there's a lot of other elements nearby.

title: split current-item-defaults per element type
body:
When changing the default while a specific element is selected, or a tool type is selected, it changes the default for all future shapes.

This may be undesirable if you e.g. change a stroke color of a rectangle, you may not want that to apply to future text elements. Especially if you switch the color to a transparent color.

### proposal

Cache defaults per element types, or type groups (e.g. rectangle + ellipse + diamond could use the same defaults fine).

author: Thomas-Lv

title: behavior of changing the default shape style(s)
body:
Right now, we remember the last style (stroke or bg color, sloppiness, font size, ...) after you change it so that drawing new shapes will use those defaults.

I don't think we should remove this behavior, but we _may_ tweak it:

1. **change defaults only if you change style while you have a tool selected**, not when changing styles existing shape(s) on canvas.

    If you create shapes of certain style and then want to change one or two shapes' style, it currently changes the default. This is often undesirable.

2. also **change defaults when copying style**.

    Right now, copying a style from an existing shape (shift-alt-c) does not change the defaults. This makes it if you want to start creating shapes with specific style you used before — you need to change all the properties manually.
    
    At the same time, if you just want to copy/paste a style from one element to another without changing the defaults, this change would be undesirable.

In both cases, arguments could be made in both directions. We need to make a call on what we think makes more sense for most people for most cases.

At the same time, having different behavior for different types of actions may make the app look inconsistent or buggy, and the behavior unintuitive.

title: Generalise storage and collaboration backends
body:
I would like to propose the excalidraw app gets a abstraction layer for firebase and socket.io.

While this will also provide a good abstraction for testing and future development, my primary intent is to implement alternative backends for storing sessions/files/scenes and for real-time collaboration. 

My proposal is to refactor all mentions of firebase and socket.io into a class type and expose it via a property, that defaults to the current behaviour to preserve existing features.

A good start was made here for the storage part: https://github.com/alswl/excalidraw/pull/2/files

References:
- https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/1772
- https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4038

title: [Feature Request] Quick Search and Add Image from Google Images
body:
## Feature Request: Quick Search and Add Image from Google Images

### Description

I would like to suggest adding a feature to Excalidraw that allows users to search for and add images directly from Google Images within the Excalidraw interface. Currently, users have to switch to a separate browser window, search for an image on Google Images, and then copy/paste the image into Excalidraw. It would be much more convenient if we could perform a quick search and add images directly from Google Images without leaving the Excalidraw page.

### Use Case

As a user, I often need to include reference images or visual elements from the web in my Excalidraw diagrams, such as when creating wireframes or mockups. However, the current process of switching between Excalidraw and a separate browser window to search for and add images can be cumbersome and disrupts the workflow. Having a built-in quick search and add image feature within Excalidraw would greatly streamline this process and improve productivity.

### Proposed Solution

I propose adding a search bar or a button within Excalidraw that allows users to perform a quick image search using Google Images. The search results could be displayed within Excalidraw itself, and users should be able to select and add images directly to their Excalidraw canvas with a simple click or drag-and-drop action. This would eliminate the need to switch between Excalidraw and a separate browser window and would provide a more seamless and efficient way to add images from the web to Excalidraw diagrams.

### Additional Information

- Including this feature would enhance the usability and convenience of Excalidraw, making it a more powerful tool for creating visual diagrams and illustrations.
- This feature could be optional, and users should have the ability to enable or disable it in the settings, in case they prefer not to use it.
- It could be implemented using the Google Images API or by integrating a web view within Excalidraw to display search results from Google Images.
- Other drawing and diagramming tools, such as Lucidchart and Miro, already have similar built-in image search features, which could serve as inspiration for implementing this feature in Excalidraw.

Thank you for considering my feature request. I believe that this addition would greatly improve the user experience of Excalidraw and make it even more user-friendly and efficient for creating visual diagrams and illustrations.

title: [Feature Request] Pages
body:
Dear Excalidraw,

I am writing to request the addition of a new feature to Excalidraw - the ability to create multiple pages within a single document. Currently, users are limited to creating drawings on a single page, which can become cluttered and difficult to manage as the complexity of the drawing increases.

By allowing users to create multiple pages within a single document, Excalidraw would become a more versatile tool for creating complex diagrams and flowcharts, as well as more detailed illustrations. This would be particularly useful for designers, architects, and engineers who need to create detailed drawings with multiple layers of information.

Some potential features that could be included with multi-page functionality could be:

* The ability to add, delete, and rearrange pages within a document
* The ability to link pages together with arrows or hyperlinks
* The ability to export individual pages or the entire document as a single file
* The ability to navigate between pages with a sidebar or toolbar

I believe that this feature would greatly enhance the usability of Excalidraw and make it an even more valuable tool for a wide range of professionals. Thank you for considering my request, and I look forward to seeing what the Excalidraw team comes up with in the future.
author: alyxdow

title: id cross-contamination when inserting multiple library items at once
body:
Library items may share ids (and group ids, binding ids...). This can happen when you create a library composed of the same specific elements.

This becomes a problem if you insert multiple such items at once. Element ids are regenerated, but they still share the same group ids because at the time of insertion we've already flattened all the items into a single array and we can't determine whether an element shares a group id incorrectly.

Repro:

1. install https://libraries.excalidraw.com/?theme=light&sort=default#mayankguptadotcom-wardley-mapping-canvas
2. select all its library items (shift-click) and drag onto canvas
3. many library items share their groups

title: Excalidraw getting slow in Obsidian
body:
Hi,

I use Ecalidraw inside Obsidian (Android app v 1.4.3 with Excalidraw plugin v 1.8.17) on my Chromebook (ChromeOS 111.0.5563.118, Acer Spin 514) with a pen. I use it for handwritten Notes or small drawings. But once I write about one loosely filled screen of text, Excalidraw starts to become very slow. The sampling rate for the pen position becomes so slow that some letters are not written at all or become just a straight line (so just two pen positions are recorded by Excalidraw). 

I don't know how to give instructions to reproduce this or what information I can add to make this easier to understand or analyze, so please let me know and I'll try to provide whatever I can. I don't know what exactly the issue is here, but I have this related to the amount of text on a note just with Excalidraw in this setup. I did not experience it with other handwriting software (apps or web), only then the system load of my Chromebook is very high, which is not the case with Excalidraw, at least not when the note is pretty empty. I would also be happy with options to just "flatten" the drawing (meaning to make it just an image on which I can continue to write). I do not change stuff from the top of the nor and if I want to, I can just draw white lines above the old and rewrite, so that would be totally fine. But I didn't find an option to do that.

Regards,
Jan

title: Collaboration-Room (TUTO)
body:
Hi, 

I finally achieve to work with collaboration container (excalidraw-room).  :rocket: 
Please find below my docker compose (with RP: traefik).
Note that the websocket HAVE to be on another URL (not a PATH - It doesn't work), if you go through ReverseProxy as traefik. 


```
---
version: "3.7"
########################### NETWORKS
networks:
  t2_proxy:
    external: true
########################### VOLUMES
volumes:
  notused:
    driver: local
########################### SERVICES
services:
  excalidraw-server:
    image: xxxxxxxxxxxxx/excalidraw:latest (custom, build your own)
    restart: always
    networks:
      t2_proxy:
    volumes:
      - notused:/opt/node_app/app/node_modules
    labels:
      - com.centurylinklabs.watchtower.enable=true
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      ## HTTP Routers
      - "traefik.http.routers.excalidraw.entrypoints=https"
      - "traefik.http.routers.excalidraw.rule=Host(`draw.EXAMPLE.com`)"
      ## HTTP Services
      - "traefik.http.routers.excalidraw.service=excalidraw-svc"
      - "traefik.http.services.excalidraw-svc.loadbalancer.server.scheme=http"
      - "traefik.http.services.excalidraw-svc.loadbalancer.server.port=80"
      - "traefik.http.routers.excalidraw.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.excalidraw.tls.certresolver=xxxxxxxxxx"

  excalidraw-room:
    image: excalidraw/excalidraw-room:latest (official)
    restart: always
    networks:
      t2_proxy:
    environment:
      TZ: Europe/Paris
      PORT: 3002
    labels:
      - com.centurylinklabs.watchtower.enable=true
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      ## HTTP Routers
      - "traefik.http.routers.excaliroom.entrypoints=https"
      - "traefik.http.routers.excaliroom.rule=Host(`draw-ws.EXAMPLE.com`)"
      ## HTTP Services
      - "traefik.http.routers.excaliroom.service=excaliroom-svc"
      - "traefik.http.services.excaliroom-svc.loadbalancer.server.scheme=http"
      - "traefik.http.services.excaliroom-svc.loadbalancer.server.port=3002"
      - "traefik.http.routers.excaliroom.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.excaliroom.tls.certresolver=xxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
      - "traefik.http.routers.excaliroom.middlewares=websocket"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.websocket.headers.customrequestheaders.X-Forwarded-Proto=https"
```

**My Dockerfile for Excalidraw** 

```
FROM node:14-alpine AS download
WORKDIR /opt/node_app
# Télcharger une version de Excalidraw
RUN wget https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/archive/refs/tags/v0.15.0.tar.gz -O excalidraw.tar.gz
RUN tar -zvxf excalidraw.tar.gz
RUN mv excalidraw-* excalidraw

FROM node:14-alpine AS build

ENV PUB_SRV_NAME draw.EXAMPLE.com
ENV PUB_SRV_NAME_WS draw-ws.EXAMPLE.com
ENV NODE_ENV development
ENV TZ Europe/Paris
ENV REACT_APP_BACKEND_V2_GET_URL https://$PUB_SRV_NAME/api/v2/
ENV REACT_APP_BACKEND_V2_POST_URL https://$PUB_SRV_NAME/api/v2/post
ENV REACT_APP_WS_SERVER_URL https://$PUB_SRV_NAME_WS/

WORKDIR /opt/node_app

COPY --from=download /opt/node_app/excalidraw/package.json /opt/node_app/excalidraw/yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn --ignore-optional --network-timeout 600000

COPY --from=download /opt/node_app/excalidraw/ .
RUN yarn build:app:docker

FROM nginx:1.21-alpine

COPY --from=build /opt/node_app/build /usr/share/nginx/html

HEALTHCHECK CMD wget -q -O /dev/null http://localhost || exit 1
```

I hope this can help other people <3
author: siddjellali

title: Tag with version in docker publish
body:
Dear Excalidraw team,
Thanks for your software [delivered in permissive license](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/LICENSE)

While I'm currently writing an helm chart to deploy to self-hosted kubernetes cluster (ref #2703), I'm seeing that docker hub contains only "latest" tag, and no other tag seems to be added since ~1 year.

Is it possible to tag with version ?

Anticipated issue : 
1. I'm deploying hightly available deployment with 3 replicas
2. Node hosting replica 1 is down
3. Kubernetes is reallocating to another node, without docker image in cache

-> 2 replica have the version of step 1, the new replica download the latest, with version of the step 3, and users observe version inconsistency.

<details>
  <summary>More reasons</summary>
  Using a versioned Docker tag rather than just the "latest" tag in a Kubernetes deployment context provides several benefits that improve overall deployment stability, predictability, and manageability. Here are some key reasons:

* Reproducibility: Using versioned tags ensures that the exact same image is deployed every time, which guarantees reproducibility. "Latest" is a mutable tag that could point to different images over time, and this can introduce inconsistencies in deployments, making it difficult to identify issues caused by version changes.
* Rollback and roll-forward: In case of a failed deployment or detected issues, using versioned tags allows you to easily roll back to a known, stable version or roll forward to a new version. With the "latest" tag, you cannot guarantee which version you are rolling back or forward to, which can lead to unexpected results.
* Explicitness: Versioned tags make it clear which version of an image is being deployed. This enhances the understanding of what is running in the environment and reduces confusion among team members.
* Dependency management: In a Kubernetes deployment, different components of your application may depend on specific versions of other components. Versioned tags ensure that the right version of each component is deployed and helps to avoid breaking changes introduced by updating to incompatible versions.
</details>

Why not using excalidraw.com ?
We're in France, and excalidraw.com **break** GDPR using Google services without any opt-in.
1. [Source: CNIL](https://www.cnil.fr/en/google-analytics-and-data-transfers-how-make-your-analytics-tool-compliant-gdpr)
2. [GDPR Article 44](https://gdpr-info.eu/art-44-gdpr/)
3. [Matomo blog](https://matomo.org/blog/2022/02/france-google-analytics-gdpr-breach/)
4. [Noyb article](https://noyb.eu/en/edps-sanctions-parliament-over-eu-us-data-transfers-google-and-stripe)
author: pand-app

author: prephyte

title: [API - feature request] Be able to create text element inside a given rectangle programatically
body:
I use Excalidraw to programmatically create rectangles with text inside them. To achieve that, I use the function [updateScene](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/ref#updatescene) and I pass, as scene data, two elements for each pair rectangle-text. One element is the rectangle, whose position (x, y) and dimensions (width and height) I decide. The second element is the text. If I want to put the text centered in the rectangle, I am forced to compute multiple text parameters on my own (x, y, width, height, baseline, lineHeight, among others).

My question: is there any way to automatically compute the text properties in order to have the text properly centered in the rectangle?
author: edgar-g-q

title: Inserted images become blurry / fuzzy, exports have large filesize (image compression)
body: Image compression in Excalidraw could be improved in 2 ways: ## 1) Blurry / Fuzzy Images Images inserted into Excalidraw are compressed, causing blurriness: Original screenshot: This is mentioned over here in #4236: > @dwelle in [comment 1516594659](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4236#issuecomment-1516594659) The images are being compressed. We can try improve the compression, as well as increasing the size limits. Also in #4236, another example image was presented by @jwdinkel: > @jwdinkel in [comment 1407579257](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4236#issuecomment-1407579257): I'd like to understand if there's a way to make the images pasted into Excalidra crisp like on miro.com. > > I find the images rather fuzzy and the text is not crisp. ![215309605-ec2f2409-c936-45f0-b078-de0f9540633e](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1935696/234064121-7a36dc0a-aae6-4c1f-b562-024417ff1048.png) ## 2) Large export filesize Inserting an unoptimized screenshot with 169 KB filesize causes the Excalidraw export to become 626 KB: - https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6515#issuecomment-1522134010
author: karlhorky

title: Inserted images have incorrect colors in dark mode
body: When switching Excalidraw to dark mode, the color representation of images is off (tested browser: Chrome 112 on macOS): https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1935696/234062899-3e4c69d8-1f9a-40d6-93cd-87df9fdf2ddc.mp4 Original screenshot: This color shift issue (with dark mode) also has some explanation over here (although the author @anatoliibakhtin is actually reporting a different issue): - https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5312 It is apparently caused by the color inversion: > @dwelle in [comment 1157697888](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5312#issuecomment-1157697888): we invert the colors using CSS, and then individually apply a counter filter on image elements Also mentioned in #4616: > @dwelle in [comment ](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4616#issuecomment-1250112100): Not something we do by choice, but due to how our dark mode works right now. > > I assume you're using Safari? On other browsers that support canvas filters we selectively invert the images back (the double invert isn't 100% accurate, but for now it seems serviceable). I am in Chrome 112 macOS, and this is a noticeable, undesirable color shift. Would be amazing to improve the "counter filter" to work better in non-Safari browsers (also in Safari would be ideal)

title: [Feature Request] To be able to lock "resizing" of elements
body:
I would like to be able to lock the resize of elements, so that I can only move them when selecting them, but not resizing them unless I unlock resize again.

To provide more context on why I need this feature, I have used Excalidraw to build a planning, where I use rectangles with text to represent each item in the planning. However, when these rectangles are too small and when I use Excalidraw in a small touch screen, it is sometimes hard to move a rectangle without resizing it. For this cases, I would like to have resizing of the rectangle locked.

If you finally decide to implement this, I would like also to be able to lock deletion of specific elements (I understand the logic is similar to the case of locking resize, its just locking or unlocking a specific operation on an element)

Thanks a lot in advance

author: godrays

title: Add tests coverage threshold in pull requests
body:
We should add a minimum threshold for test coverage in PR's to make sure the PR's merged at least have some tests if not 100%.

Right now since we don't have any such threshold so its hard to say the coverage and often we need to ask the author to add test.

I think setting  to **65%** threshold would be good to go and later we can increase it.

This would make sure every PR has some tests and will help in avoid bug, better maintenance down the line and increase coverage.

title: CSS class conflict with bootstrap
body:
Version: 0.15.2

Hello, I'm integrating Excalidraw into an app which also uses bootstrap. I'm currently looking at a bug which causes the hamburger icon not doing anything upon a click:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/617641/236669947-c64ca0aa-75a6-4b2c-bc47-874d8787a15d.png)

Upon closer inspection, it looks to be caused by a class name conflict with bootstrap's "dropdown-menu" class:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/617641/236670171-5f28e6c3-a231-4752-810c-e466c2417db2.png)

Basically, the boostrap's styles keep the excalidraw's dropdown hidden. I can fix this locally by forcing a style override, but I guess it would be better to avoid these sort of problems, perhaps by using a vendor prefix for all excalidraw CSS classes?
author: zadam

title: [Feature request] : Ability to rotate arrows at an angle
body: Hello Excalidraw team, I am a user of Excalidraw and I have been enjoying using it for my diagramming needs. However, I have noticed that while it is possible to rotate shapes like squares and circles, there does not seem to be a way to rotate arrows at an angle. As someone who frequently uses arrows in my diagrams, I find this feature to be essential. I am requesting that Excalidraw be updated with the ability to rotate arrows at an angle. Specifically, I would like to be able to pick a point on the arrow and rotate it around its center, just like I can with other shapes. I believe that this feature would greatly enhance the functionality of Excalidraw and make it an even more valuable tool for users like me. Thank you for considering my request. Best regards,

title: Japanese text input inside a container does not work properly
body:
OS: Windows10 or MacOS too
Browser: Chrome 113.0.5672.64 or Edge 113.0.1774.35 too

When I input some Japanese hiraganas and covert it into kanji or katakana inside rectangle or "Wrap text in container" (that's how you input Japanese normally), the output is converted before writing is finished like below:

![JapaneseIssue](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/105631979/65cf7463-448a-4a5b-a575-946fc6102e23)

In the case above, I expect the text to be "日本語でコミュニケーション", but it is "日本語でこみゅにけーｓｙ御" or “日本語でこみゅにけーｓｙおおｎｎ” instead.

This phenomenon doesn't happen in the case below:
-convert hiraganas directly on the canvas(without any container)
-convert hiraganas into a container of sufficiently large size
Even though I can work around this issue by expanding a container to large size from the begging, I’m having trouble with conversion bugs when adding sentences gradually.

I would be very grateful if you could change it so that the container expands automatically so that it can be used with Japanese IME.
author: eri24w

title: support `scrollToContent` `opts.fitToViewport`
body:
```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] change docs for `opts.fitToContent` to clarify it contains the content to viewport (down to 10% limit), but does not zoom above 100%. Perhaps we should rename the option to something more clear.
- [ ] add an option `opts.fitToViewport` to _actually_ fit content to viewport, in other words go above 100% if the element(s) are smaller than viewport
```
author: dwelle

title: disallow grouping bound containers to themselves
body:
```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] disallow grouping text containers or labeled arrows with themselves
- [ ] if you copy such container from inside a group and paste outside that group, make sure the group ids are stripped
- [ ] repair such existing groups on restore (legacy)
- [ ] if we end up with such invalid grouping anyway, make sure that selecting such a container ignores the group
```

steps:

1. create text container or labeled arrow
2. `cmd/ctrl-G` to group
3. shouldn't be allowed
author: dwelle

title: [Feature Request] Increase or reduce space between elements
body:
A drawing tool that I use at work has a couple of really handy tools called sweeper and magnet.

The sweeper tool lets you pick a point in a drawing in between two objects and drag to create a space between the objects either side. The magnet tool does the opposite. This [YouTube video](https://youtu.be/xWurQ7-KTFU) demos the tools.

It would be great to have something similar in Excalidraw if the underlying tech permits, perhaps with a shortcut e.g. ctrl+shift+drag with the hand tool selected.

title: Drawing size limit
body:
It seems like there is a hard-coded 10 MB limit. Is this true? Once my document (mathematical notebook with basically just drawings by hand and text) exceeded this size, whenever I open excalidraw (free website version) I now find that the changes are lost. Luckily I saved a local backup so I never really lost any data, but it is still annoying to have to remember to reload the document manually from disk each time I open the app.

(I have also had a related issue when collborating, which has caused some sort of desyncing issues. I believe it was when someone accidentally pasted stuff onto the whiteboard that this used to happen. But I have not had this issue when working alone, so I don't think it is the same issue.)

I am using the latest chrome version on arch linux.

title: Provide a way to change text color of multiple selected element at the same time
body:
The only way to change the text color of an element is by double clicking it so that the properties side panel change to allow setting the "stroke" color of the text.

This prevents to be able to modify multiple selected elements text color since double-clicking only works for one element.

I propose having an additional slider in the text section to set the color instead.

body:
When I refresh a draw with live session enabled, it always initialize the board with grid disabled.

I'd like the setting to persist across all users in that session room, this allows a more streamlined experience and expand to more use case when I use Excalidraw with my team.

For example,

title: Rich Text Editor / Powerful WYSIWYG support
body:
As an extensive Excalidraw user I miss this feature specially when it comes creating presentations as I need to highlight specific parts of text or style them differently.
There is also an issue https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/1126 regarding adding the ability to colour different parts of text  

Here are some of the Rich Text Editors we can try out 

1. https://quilljs.com/ - 54.19KB (gzip) - probably this doesn''t have hightling with diff color support as well
2. https://lexical.dev/ - 29.79KB (gzip)
3. https://ckeditor.com/ - 277.17KB (gzip) 
4. https://github.com/basecamp/trix - too basic so probably not suitable for us as it doesn't have features like highlighting

There are couple of more, I will add here as I come across. Lexical seems to the best choice so far with features and bundle size as well.

author: elFreak

title: Introduce a property Text Color for Text containers and labelled arrows
body:
Right now if we want to update colour of Text inside a Text container / Labelled arrow then we select the container and update its stroke color however if we want to colour if differently from the container stroke color then the only way is to double click and select the text and update the **Stroke colour.**


https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/11256141/21a4b9a1-9bba-4019-8f70-649155bc7847

1. However often might want to colour the text differently than stroke colour and double click is not ideal always and is currently sort of hack to achieve it and sometimes I do want the container stroke as transparent as mentioned in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5863 where I end up using double click hack

2. This also won't work when we want to update the text colour of **multiple text containers / labelled arrows** at once so having a field for it separately will be helpful. 

3. The Stroke colour and Text colour are two different properties and right now its used to serve two separate purpose.

Hence having a separate field **"Text Colour"** which is responsible for only updating text colour will be helpful
author: ad1992

title: Improve documentation :)
body:
We launched new [docs](https://docs.excalidraw.com) recently but we need to make it better for user facing queries.

I am listing down the queries which we often get though we have mentioned it in docs but it needs to be improved so its more clear and helps in better communication. Feel free to add more.

- [ ] How to integrate Excalidraw in non React Projects ? Basically this is the way to do it https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/integration#browser and we need to improve the docs to communicate this better. There are couple of discussions also opened related to same https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/4100, https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/6537,https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/3849
- [ ] How to support collaboration in Excalidraw npm package ? Its mention in [FAQ](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/faq#does-this-package-support-collaboration-) but we get this query often and we need to improve the docs for the same. There are discussion opened related to this as well (apart from the one linked in FAQ) - https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/6676, https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/5902, https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/3941, https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/4924, https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/4685
- [ ] Improve docs on how one can start collaboration locally by connecting to the collab server for development. There are couple of issues opened related to this as well
- [ ] Document JSON schema https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6804

- [ ] Add code_of_conduct https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6859
author: ad1992

title: Migrate from CRA to Vite
body:
We would like to migrate from CRA to Vite due the a couple of reasons

* The Build time of Vite is significantly faster the CRA  
* Hot Module Replacement(HMR)  is faster in Vite as compared to CRA
**Vite doesn't have strict `path` rule that you cannot import anything outside `src` folder line `CRA`. Due to this restriction we have not been able to decouple a lot of code outside src directory.
*  CRA is no longer maintained and not recommended in official [docs](https://react.dev/learn/start-a-new-react-project?ref=asserts.ai)

Here is a nice [article](https://makimo.pl/blog/why-we-use-vite-instead-of-create-react-app/#:~:text=Vite%20uses%20the%20esbuild%20bundler,in%20turn%20makes%20it%20slower.) about metrics in Vite vs CRA. We should try out and check how it works for us to understand better

Initial attempt to integrate Vite in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6441 by @wesbragagt

Shippped in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6713 and https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6818
author: ad1992

title: Improve Arrow Binding UX
body:
1. Right now we auto bind arrows to the shapes as they are close by but this could be annoying at times when I don't want to bind it and still its bind so we should have a way to unbind it as well. 

This gets worse when arrow gets bind to a text and you try to move text but as a result arrow moves as well (Notice when I am trying to move the text "HELLO")

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/11256141/f2e77ffb-f70b-4ad7-8b1c-98282c1b7934



This has been one of the requests as well from users so linking all issues here and we will be discussing her how we want to approach this so we can allow unbinding as well and improve overall UX for arrow binding

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/3690, https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/2138, https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4797, https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6631

2. There is one more case as mentioned here in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4324 about not binding arrow to elements outside the group (when arrow is inside the group) which makes complete sense as well

body:
When in view mode users should be able to pan canvas using the keyboard arrows.

- [ ] arrow left/right/up/down should move around

author: dwelle

title: Text on objects in frames disappears when aligning
body:
### Steps to reproduce:
1. Open new blank canvas.
2. Draw two shapes inside a frame, add text to one of them.
3. Select both shapes and align them (top, bottom, left, right or center in either dimension) - make sure the alignment will cause the shape with the text in it to move.
4. The text disappears. 😱
5. Double clicking the shape reveals the text for editing, but clicking out of editing mode causes the text to disappear again.

This doesn't happen when aligning shapes that are not part of a frame.

Possibly related to #6686

author: melekabbassi

title: Enhancement Request/Necessary Feature - Font List Styling
body:
Hello Excalidraw team,

I would like to request the addition of a font list styling feature in Excalidraw. Currently, the application provides a great set of features for creating diagrams and sketches, but I believe adding font list styling would bring additional advantages to the users.

Advantages of Font List Styling:

Enhanced Visual Organization: With font list styling, users can easily distinguish different font families and styles within their text, making it easier to visually organize and structure their diagrams.

Improved Readability: Applying font list styling allows users to highlight important information or headings by using different font styles or weights, improving readability and making it easier to understand complex diagrams.

Consistent Styling: Font list styling ensures consistency throughout the diagram by providing a unified look and feel across the text elements. This is particularly useful when working on collaborative projects or creating diagrams with a consistent visual style.

Suggested Implementation:
It would be great if Excalidraw could provide a font list styling option in the text toolbar, where users can select a font family, font size, and apply font styles such as bold, italic, underline, and strikethrough. Additionally, a preview of the selected font style could be displayed in the font list dropdown, helping users make informed styling choices.

Example:
Here's a visual representation of how the font list styling feature could look in Excalidraw:

![pnnnnng](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/101344518/d7055941-aef5-4e23-8980-e61b33fad7a7)

I believe the addition of font list styling would greatly enhance the usability and versatility of Excalidraw, making it an even more powerful tool for creating visually appealing diagrams.

Thank you for considering this enhancement request. I look forward to hearing your thoughts on this feature and its potential inclusion in future versions of Excalidraw.

title: excalidraw UI goes blank on eyedropper open
body:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/14358394/764c6604-1ed2-4e53-b40b-971d43cf2f8b

@dwelle This was raised for the Obsidian plugin, but I can reproduce the effect on excalidraw.com as well. https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/1157

Screen recording is on a Samsung Android 13 device using Chorme 115.0.5790.40 with a bluetooth keyboard.

by the way, why are we using jotain 1.13.1 instead of 2.2.1. I see the current code would need to be migrated to 2.x.x but maybe 1.13.1 is not fully React 18 compatible? just thinking aloud
author: zsviczian

title: Rendering differences / errors in excalidraw-utils
body:
**Context**:
I am writing a script to export Excalidraw documents to SVG. For this I use the `excalidraw-utils` with the `exportToSvg` function. Here is an example drawing as PNG (the excalidraw scene is embedded) with the expected result:
![Test Drawing as PNG](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/13430588/368ebe8a-26e3-4191-8ab6-d3d92e49148d)


**What happend:**
I noticed that there are problems in some places when I use the `excalidraw-utils` that do not occur in the normal excalidraw application in the browser. Using the `exportToSvg` function from the utils I get the following svg output:
![Test Drawing as SVG using utils](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/13430588/f1e72080-8e4a-4de2-8d00-c572fc467d26)
As you can see the two texts "PROBLEM" are not rendered on the arrows but on the far left side. Also all the boxes including the background box are not rounded like in the source file.


**What should happen:**
When I export the drawing as SVG using the excalidraw.com web app, the problems do not occur:

author: yyaegash

title: [Feature] Expand support for additional sketch styles
body:
With Excalidraw using RoughJS as the sketching library, I'd love to see the additional sketch styles in the library be included for the `fill` property.

Right now the fill options are 'hachure,' 'crosshatch,' and 'solid.' It would be nice to add in the other missing styles already exposed in the RoughJS library API.

---

### fillStyle
Rough.js supports the following styles (Default value is hachure):

**hachure** draws sketchy parallel lines with the same roughness as defined by the roughness and the bowing properties of the shape. It can be further configured using the fillWeight, hachureAngle, and hachureGap properties.

**solid** is more like a conventional fill.

**zigzag** draws zig-zag lines filling the shape

**cross-hatch** Similar to hachure, but draws cross hatch lines (akin to two hachure fills 90 degrees from each other).

**dots** Fills the shape with sketchy dots.

**dashed** Similar to hachure but the individual lines are dashed. Dashes can be configured using the dashOffset and dashGap properties.

**zigzag-line** Similar to hachure but individual lines are drawn in a zig-zag fashion. The size of the zig-zag can be configured using the zigzagOffset property

![roughjs](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/24608720/0327c4c5-55d3-4812-b9ac-fe2ef890a515)

---
[^^^^from the [RoughJS docs](https://github.com/rough-stuff/rough/wiki#fill)]

Additionally, it would be nice if there was expanded support for some of the more advanced `fill` properties. I can understand not all properties may be supported as there are a ton of them which could really complicate the UI/UX for most users, but either a subset of additional options would be nice or some sort of option to toggle an option to view a more advanced "Selected Shape Editor" panel. Including an option to edit the `hachureAngle`, `roughness`, and `simplification` would be awesome.

This could tie in with your recent LaTeX support #5265 or the soon-to-be supported tablular data from paste feature #3762.

For some inspiration, the OG JS diagramming library that many current projects were based on, [JS-Sequence-Diagrams](https://github.com/bramp/js-sequence-diagrams), had a lovely hand-drawn theme:

author: alex-kim-dev

title: Enable SVG Editing in External Text Editor?
body:
We have some SVGs in our codebase which have been exported from Excalidraw with the "Embed scene" option checked.

This seems to be great - the best of both worlds: creating the SVG files in Excalidraw and also viewing them in any software that supports SVG 🙌 🎉 Sounds really amazing!

However, there is a problem: making small edits of the SVG text or shapes in a text editor will cause the visible SVG and the Excalidraw scene data to get out of date 😱 Eg:

1. Create a diagram in Excalidraw and export to SVG with "Embed scene"
2. Make a small edit of the text in the SVG
3. Review the change in an SVG viewer like the browser, works ✅
4. Open the diagram in Excalidraw to make a bigger change - previous data displayed 💥

This is a bit of a hidden problem, because it appears to be working on first glance at the SVG, but then when it is opened again in Excalidraw, an old version of the diagram is shown (which may or may not be recognized by the person editing, if the text editor change was small).

It would be great if the SVG and Scene data could stay in sync somehow, allowing editing the plaintext SVG in a text editor.

Some ideas for implementation, ordered according to desirability in my opinion:

1. Derive scene data from SVG and remove duplicated scene data - most minimal data structure, if possible
2. Make scene data also human-readable and editable in a text editor - shows that further changes need to be made in text editor, but requires 2x the edits
3. Derive scene data from SVG only if there is a conflict, don't remove duplicated scene data - simplest implementation?
5. Make SVG data also non-human-readable and non-editable - least desirable, would be great to be able to use a text editor

title: `fileSave` does not work when used inside of Electron
body: # Description of the Issue When trying to do actions that save files (like export PNG to file or export JSON to file), it fails when the application is being used from inside of an Electron application. We can get two errors: 1. (This might happen only in MacOS, I'm not sure). The default save location is set to "Macintosh HD" and when clicking on "Save", it fails silently with a "DOMException: Failed to create or truncate file" in the console: 2. When choosing a "normal" save location like "Downloads", it fails and one gets the following error: "The request is not allowed by the user agent or the platform in the current context." # Reproducing the issue Reproducing the issue is trivial: 1. Open any Electron app 2. Get to the console (Right click, Inspect Element, then go to "Console" tab) 3. Enter `window.location.href="https://excalidraw.com/"` 4. Then excalidraw opens. Try to "Export Image" # Findings from brief look into the code I see that Excalidraw uses [browser-fs-access](https://github.com/GoogleChromeLabs/browser-fs-access) which might be the place where the issue is originating from. More relevant hooks into the source code: in the source, `App.onExportImage` which uses `src/data/index`'s `exportCanvas` which uses `src/data/filesystem`'s `fileSave` # Proposed solution Since it seems to be working in browsers and because electron apps are probably a minority use-case (unless Excalidraw.com is thinking of making an electron app of their own), Simplest/easiest solution would be to expose a callback in `exportProps` similar to `onExportToBackend` : https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/ui-options#exportopts. The expectation is that, when the callback is passed by the user of the API, it will be used instead of `browser-fs-access`'s `fileSave` (it could be used in `src/data/filesystem`'s `fileSave`). That way, in Electron apps (on in other cases where current approach might not be working), one can send their own fileSave function

title: Adding Grid Background Feature like Math Notebook Background
body:
Hi Excalidraw team,

I hope this message finds you well. I have been using Excalidraw for my mathematical illustrations and diagrams, and I find it to be an excellent tool. However, I believe it would greatly enhance the user experience if there was a grid lines feature that resembles a math notebook background.

The grid lines would provide a visual aid for aligning elements accurately, making it easier to create math-related diagrams, graphs, and geometric shapes. The math notebook background would add a touch of familiarity and make the workspace feel more authentic for mathematical illustrations.

I understand that Excalidraw is designed to be a versatile and intuitive tool, and the addition of grid lines with a math notebook background would further enhance its usefulness for math-related tasks. I believe many users, including students, teachers, and professionals working in mathematics and related fields, would greatly benefit from this feature.

Thank you for considering my request. I appreciate the hard work and effort you put into developing and improving Excalidraw. I look forward to seeing the continued growth of this fantastic tool.

### Feature Details
- When enabled, the grid background should overlay the canvas and provide a visual grid pattern.
- Users should have control over the grid size, spacing, and color.
- The grid should be customizable to support different types of grids (e.g., square, isometric, etc.).
- It would be helpful to have an option to toggle the grid on/off based on user preference.

### Expected Behavior
1. When the grid background feature is enabled, a grid pattern should be displayed on the canvas.
2. The grid pattern should follow the specified grid size, spacing, and color preferences.
3. The grid should not interfere with existing drawing functionality and should not obstruct or affect user interaction with the canvas.

Thank again for considering this feature request. I believe it would enhance the usability and flexibility of Excalidraw for many users. Please let me know if you need any further information or clarification.

Thank you for your wonderful Excalidraw, it really boost my productivity with Obsidian. I have a feature request as following:

Say I'm creating a table. I drew lines, then I make contents in the cells. Suddenly a certain chunk of text is quite big, it goes outside the right border of the containing cell. So I have to pull the overlapping line (and also other parallel lines behind it) to the right. And now all the horizontal lines are quite short, they don't cover the last column. So I have to lengthen them to the right, which can only be done by manually selecting and stretching each line, because mass resize can be done only for both vertical and horizontal directions, not for a single one.

Please consider making resizing in one direction possible for multiple objects at once.

title: Idea: allow ChatGPT to create diagrams
body:
Hi,

I love Excalidraw. I am very interested in describing complex ideas with diagrams excalidraw is perfect for that task. 

ChatGPT excels in generating text that can be used to construct complex charts with for example MermaidJS. This involves asking ChatGPT to generate a MermaidJS diagram, which I then copy and paste into the MermaidJS editor at https://mermaid.live/. 

This has proven to be quite efficient and seamless. I am very eager to extend this workflow to Excalidraw, leveraging ChatGPT to generate JSON text that could be used to create diagrams in Excalidraw. For instance, I requested ChatGPT to generate a JSON version of a simple Excalidraw diagram, specifically, "two circles connected by a line". Here's what the AI came up with: 

```json
{
  "type": "excalidraw",
  "version": 2,
  "source": "https://excalidraw.com",
  "elements": [
    {
      "type": "ellipse",
      "version": 1,
      "versionNonce": 123,
      "x": 50,
      "y": 50,
      "width": 100,
      "height": 100,
      "strokeColor": "#000000",
      "backgroundColor": "#ffffff",
      "strokeWidth": 2,
      "strokeStyle": "solid",
      "roughness": 1,
      "opacity": 100
    },
    {
      "type": "ellipse",
      "version": 1,
      "versionNonce": 456,
      "x": 200,
      "y": 50,
      "width": 100,
      "height": 100,
      "strokeColor": "#000000",
      "backgroundColor": "#ffffff",
      "strokeWidth": 2,
      "strokeStyle": "solid",
      "roughness": 1,
      "opacity": 100
    },
    {
      "type": "arrow",
      "version": 1,
      "versionNonce": 789,
      "x": 100,
      "y": 100,
      "width": 100,
      "height": 0,
      "strokeColor": "#000000",
      "backgroundColor": "transparent",
      "strokeWidth": 2,
      "strokeStyle": "solid",
      "roughness": 1,
      "opacity": 100
    }
  ]
}
```

While it looks like excalidraw format. I put it in a test.excalidraw file and tried to upload it to the editor. Sadly I got an error:

![error](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/1375475/3e5f5c69-da4f-41f1-8391-a709a58f1dd7)


But there was not any explanation of what was wrong in the markup. Also I couldn´t find any JSON markup documentation of excalidraw, that would have been enough to give it to ChatGPT to generate valid markup.

> Unfortunately, as a text-based AI, I'm unable to generate valid Excalidraw JSON files due to the limitations mentioned earlier. The JSON format of Excalidraw is likely to be more complex than the example and potentially contains other information about the drawing, such as scene metadata.
> 
> I would recommend creating a drawing in Excalidraw and exporting it as a JSON file to understand the exact structure and format that it requires. Once you have an example of an Excalidraw JSON file, you can use it as a template to create new JSON files that represent different drawings.

Given this, I kindly request the following:

- Could you provide detailed documentation about the exact structure and format that Excalidraw's JSON files require? This would significantly help in generating valid JSON markup for creating diagrams.
- If it's not already in place, could you consider more descriptive error messages when a JSON import fails? This would greatly assist in troubleshooting problematic JSON.

Thank you for considering my suggestions. I believe these enhancements could potentially open up new ways for users to interact with Excalidraw, particularly in combination with text-based AI models.

body:
Hi!
Thank you for your awesome software!

author: alexaatm

title: Corrupted multi-point arrow causes shape drag-n-drop to never release
body:
To reproduce problem, follow these steps:

1. draw a corrupted arrow by
- select arrow drawing tool
- double click somewhere on the page
- click somewhere else to create the arrow
- press ESC or ENTER to exit arrow drawing

2. draw a rectangle

3. drag the arrow until the start of the arrow attaches to the border of the rectangle

4. try to drag the rectangle. it drags but never releases, despite having unclicked the mouse button

the problem appears to be caused by double-clicking to start drawing the arrow.
it creates a points array with two coordinates with values [0,0].

Example:
"points":[[0,0],[0,0],[307,5],[321,214]]
author: robnorthpower

title: Dev build broken if  package - @excalidraw/excalidraw deps installed
body: Currently the dev build is broken if you try to install the dependencies inside `src/packages/excalidraw` due to multiple versions of `react` getting installed. `react 17.x` gets installed due to `@size-limit/time@8.2.6` - I have opened an issue in the repo as well [here](https://github.com/ai/size-limit/issues/331) To fix this you will need to remove the `react` package - `src/packages/excalidraw/node_modules/react` in local and then it will work fine

author: ryan-di

title: Feature allotment between the various packages and applications
body:
It is unclear to me the extent to which the set of excalidraw features advertised as already implemented in the _npm package_ (which, I understand, constitutes the "golden set" of excalidraw features(*)) is reflected in (a) the _VSCode extension_ and (b) the _app hosted at excalidraw.com_. Put differently:

1. What would I be missing by using the VSCode extension instead of the npm package?
2. What would I be missing by using the app hosted at excalidraw.com instead of the npm package?
3. Is it the intention of the devs, to completely(*) harmonize the sets of features between the three "versions"?

(*) Except, perhaps, for some of the features advertised for the excalidraw.com app, such as PWA and real-time collaboration, that do not prima facie make sense for the other two versions ...

title: Saving in Linux (ubuntu) creates a new file instead of overwriting an existing file.
body:
I have been working **on windows** where suppose for the first time if I save the drawing, with `file-1.excalidraw` then it saves in the destination folder say **downloads**.

## 🤔 What is desired?
Now, I am constantly making the changes and when I save the changes, **it simply overwrites the existing** file `file-1.excalidraw`. Which is desired.

## 👀 What is happening?
I am trying in **ubuntu** where if I save the changes, it **creates a new file** `file-1(1).excalidraw` and on the second save, it does the same `file-1(2).excalidraw`. *(using firefox - haven't tested on chrome in Linux)*

Now, I am not sure if this is the problem with linux or what.
Please fix, this is annoying.

author: jonathanbell

title: Suggestion: snap to nearby shapes when dragging / resizing
body:
It would be really useful if I could easily align shapes by dragging them around. If I want some shapes to be left / right / top / bottom aligned I can select them and then align. This works, albeit less convenient than just dragging them and have them snap to an automatically detected guide, as in this video:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/776358/14cc448f-a09f-4563-acfb-920072c20aee

However, when trying to resize shapes to be the same width / height, there's currently no way to do that. See this video to get a sense of what I mean:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/776358/1b8248d1-3b50-4cd1-935a-d7190233c7ac

This is pretty standard in many visual editing tools (these videos are from Google Slides) but if it's too hard to implement or undesired for some other reason, I would really appreciate if there was some other way to make existing shapes the same height / width as other shapes and/or align one side of them by resizing rather than moving the whole shape.

title: Preloading libraries
body:
I was unable to find a previous issue on the subject so I'm creating anew one.

I'm selfhosting Excalidraw and a collab server for a friend so we can learn DevOps and AWS. 

Our use case requires the same set of libraries and I was wondering if there is a way to preload them so every user that connects to my Excalidraw instance has the same library, ready to use.

title: Moving items in multiple frames removes them from their frames

## Steps

1. Make two frames with shapes inside
2. Select a shape in each frame
3. Move the selected shapes within their frames

The shapes stay included in their respective frames.

## Actual behaviour

Only one shape stays included.

In the video below, the circle in the stop frame stops being included in the frame.

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/1884486/6f8ac79b-1b0f-44a2-90a7-59475716b172

I believe I have seen other behaviour which ends up with similar results: shapes being excluded from frames they were in.

title: [Feature Request] Add identifiers to the SVG export
body:
Hello!

I would like to do some SVG animation with what I export from excalidraw as SVG.
I love that it gives me lots of info about the elements, even support for custom data if I read the documentation correctly.
But I would love to be able to use the exported SVG and get the element data associated with each shape.
Something like an id attribute on the DOM element that matches the id on the excalidraw element would be great.

Seems like a small change as well, so if it is agreed it makes sense I could look into the code and do a PR for that.

title: box-selection while holding shift doesn't undo selection
body:
When box selecting while holding shift, retracting the box selection should remove the elements previously selected.

Note that this should only apply to elements that were added to selection within the currently active `pointerdown`. IOW, once you `pointerup` and then do another shift box-selection, it should keep the previous selection no matter what you (un)box-select.

![excal-bug-shift-box-selection-undo](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/5153846/eae4ed52-b01f-41b0-8c1e-779dbf88e1ca)

author: Tsukeyo0

title: `onInit`
body:
Two options:

1. `props.onInit: (api: ExcalidrawImperativeAPI) => void`
    - invoked once component is initialized. If prop not added before initialization, won't be invoked.
2. `excalidrawAPI.onInit: (subscriber: () => void) => void`
    - pub/sub
    - invoked for each subscriber. If editor already initialized at time of subscription, invoked immediately.

body: When using a dark mode in Firefox the drawing lags and delays with respect to the movement of the mouse cursor but in the light mode drawing is very smooth
author: amirc901

title: stop updating labeled-arrow text's position on updates
body:
Right now we're updating labeled-arrow text's position when we drag the arrow across the canvas. Since we're not relying on text position when rendering the labled-arrow, updating its position is unnecessary and and results in deoptimizations.

- [ ] when the arrow is inside a group and you move the group
- [x] when the arrow is inside a frame and you move the frame
- [x] when dragging plain labeled arrow
author: dwelle

title: don't rely on container text position when rendering bound text
body:
Currently rendering container's bound text relies on its position, thus on its position being properly updated on updates such as when moving the container across the canvas.

This unnecessarily increases the complexity and prevents easy optimizations such as not trashing the cached canvas of the text element on shape non-destructive operations such as position change.

With labeled text, we're not relying on text position with labeled-arrows already (though [incorrectly still updating the position in some cases](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6882)), so we should align with that.
