author: ghost

title: Japanese x Chinese unicode issue
body:
Japanese Kanji characters have a common origin with Chinese Hanzi, but they are not completely the same, as they have slightly different keystrokes. The problem is that they share unicode codepoints (likely unicode simplified too much here), which essentially means that a single font has to prefer one variation over the other. With #8530, most Kanji will be displayed as Hanzi (as Xiaolai is biased towards Chinese), which might be unreadable for some Japanese or even unusable in certain Japanese use cases/domains. See the following example:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9da0517a-f8ba-4441-919f-7460967175cc)

Further discussion https://discord.com/channels/723672430744174682/1291770040764465244/1293553830251860030.

To solve this we likely have no other option than to introduce a separate Japanese fallback font with Kanji and "active it" (~push above Xiaolai in the fallback chain) once Japanese variation is preferred/detected. 

**Technical challenges**
- Japanese font should be fairly similar to Xiaolai
  - could we use Kanji from `Kose font`?
    ~ the font Japanese variant of Xiaolai, https://github.com/lxgw/kose-font
- How shall we decide when Japanese variation should be activated?
  - consider having a scene/workspace toggle (defaulting to `false`) to always prefer Japanese variation
  - consider autodetection (toggle on) based on client-side location/ app language? (not bullet-proof, but could work for most cases)
  - consider autodetecting Japanese based on Hiragana / Katakana in the workspace > scene > element (with element allowing combining Kanji/Hanzi codepoints in one scene, but likely being more complex and with a small character set for auto-detection)
- Introducing a new Japanese font should not cause a breaking change for existing Japanese diagrams
  - consider specifying fallback fonts on the element level
    - i.e. `fallbackFamilies: "SetoFont"` or `fallbackFamilies: "SetoFont, Segoe UI Emoji"`
- Both client-side and server-side exports shall receive the flag to include the Japanese fallback font
  - could be especially challenging for server-side PNG / PDF in E+, as we would have to have "two sets" of merged fonts (one with just Chinese, other with just Japanese; possibly per each family x weight variation) and decide in runtime which one to use
    - that would also lock us into one option per scene, due to the limitations of `Resvg` and `PdfKit` (unless we find a workaround, auto-detection on the element would be worthless)

**Description:**
I’m trying to embed Excalidraw as a modal in my application, but I’ve encountered a couple of issues with the behavior of the context menu near the window edges:

1. **Right edge:**
When left-clicking near the right edge, the context menu should open towards the left, but it still opens towards the right, causing it to be hidden off-screen.

 When left-clicking near the bottom edge, the menu generally opens correctly upwards, but in some spots, it opens downwards, which causes the window to scroll unnecessarily.

author: backpropogated

title: feature request: allow watching youtube videos/playlists and local videos/m3u8 while drawing
body:
i would like to request a feature that enables users to watch youtube videos, playlists, local videos, or m3u8 streams while using the drawing tool in excalidraw. 

#### current limitation:
currently, users must switch between different applications or tabs to watch videos and draw simultaneously. this can disrupt the creative flow and make it challenging to take notes or illustrate concepts while engaging with video content.

#### use case:
allowing users to view videos while drawing would greatly enhance productivity, especially for educators and content creators. for instance, a teacher could play a tutorial video in the background and annotate key points or concepts in real time, making lessons more interactive and dynamic. many people already take conspects in excalidraw, and this feature would further enhance that capability.

#### proposed solution:
- integrate a video player within excalidraw that supports youtube links, local video files, and m3u8 streams.
- allow users to resize and position the video player within the drawing canvas, so they can easily refer to the video content while drawing.

#### why this matters:
this feature would significantly improve the user experience by combining video content consumption with real-time drawing. educators, students, and content creators often use videos as learning tools; being able to annotate or illustrate alongside would enhance comprehension and retention. 

by implementing this functionality, excalidraw can cater to a broader audience and become a versatile platform for interactive learning and collaboration, ultimately leading to more engaging and effective sessions.

thank you for considering this request! i believe that adding this feature would not only enhance individual workflows but also elevate the overall value of excalidraw as a powerful educational tool.
author: backpropogated

title: feature request: voice annotations in excalidraw
body:
i would like to propose a feature that allows users to add voice annotations directly to their drawings in excalidraw.

#### current limitation:
currently, users must rely on text notes to convey ideas and context within their drawings. this can be time-consuming and may not effectively capture the nuances of spoken explanations, especially when trying to convey complex concepts or emotional nuances.

#### use case:
the ability to record and attach voice annotations would greatly enhance the usability of excalidraw for educators, students, and professionals. for instance, a teacher could record a brief explanation of a diagram while creating it, providing students with immediate context. similarly, a team could record discussions about specific elements of a project as they design collaboratively, ensuring that everyone is on the same page.

#### proposed solution:
- integrate a voice recording feature that allows users to record audio directly within the excalidraw interface.
- users should be able to attach these audio notes to specific elements or areas of their drawings.
- provide playback controls for users to listen to their recordings while reviewing or presenting their drawings.

#### why this matters:
implementing voice annotations would significantly improve the user experience by combining visual and auditory elements in a single workspace. this is particularly beneficial for:
- **enhanced learning**: voice annotations can provide additional context that text alone cannot convey, aiding comprehension and retention for students.
- **dynamic presentations**: presenters can use voice annotations to explain their diagrams or ideas more effectively, creating a richer experience for their audience.
- **accessibility**: voice notes can help those who may struggle with writing or typing to express their ideas more freely.

by adding this feature, excalidraw can become an even more powerful tool for interactive learning and collaboration, making it appealing to a broader audience. 

thank you for considering this request! i believe that voice annotations would greatly enhance the functionality of excalidraw and improve the overall user experience.

author: zsviczian

title: [✨] Remove traditional slides sidebar and use frames instead to organize slides on the canvas itself
body:
_note: frames here means frames from the frames tool._

Slides on Excalidraw have the potential to be so much better than conventional PPT or Google Slides.

## Problem
Imho, the current model of having frames on the canvas and organizing slides on a sidebar is fundamentally flawed and leads to a poor UX. With this model, it is simply impossible to organize slides in the same way on the canvas and on the sidebar. 

Currently, the way it is handled is far from ideal:
- Adding a new slide on the sidebar adds a frame quite randomly on the canvas
- Adding a frame on the canvas adds a new slide to the bottom of the sidebar

With this model it is not really possible to have a canvas that reflects the order of the slides, or slides that reflect the way things are organized on the canvas. You could chose another model, e.g. to [put frames in a strict position on the canvas depending on slides order](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/8130), but then you would lose the beautiful freedom that the canvas offers.

## Current state
To that, we need to take into account that the current model is missing quite a lot of important features for efficient slides management:
- Reorganizing/removing slides can only be done one slide at a time -> maybe not so easy fix
- frames cannot be removed without removing content inside -> maybe not so easy fix
- slides cannot be removed multiple at a time -> maybe not so easy fix
- it is not possible to edit the canvas while on presentation mode -> maybe not so easy fix
- frames title grows when de-zooming on the canvas and can take up a lot of space -> easy fix, just make it a fixed size
- frames cannot be hidden which can create a lot of visual noise when editing the content -> easy fix, could be done with zen mode 
- probably other things I've overlooked

## Proposal
What I would do instead in the spirit of Excalidraw would be to entirely remove the slides sidebar, but instead make it so that frames are the only way to manage slides. New frames would come with a pre-defined number and changing the order would be just a matter of a right-click on a certain slide or selected slides group. Adding a new slide could be done at the end or "after" another slide with a right click. Logically, adding a slide or changing a slide's number would automatically change the number of the other slides.

### 🚨 Bug/Feature Request

#### Problem
Currently, there’s no way to access recent drawings or any history in Excalidraw. If I close my browser, I lose my work and can’t pick up where I left off. This affects workflow, especially when working on multiple drawings.

#### What I’d Love to See
- A **Recent Drawings** or **History** option to access my previous sessions quickly.
- This would help users continue or reference past work without recreating or saving everything manually.

#### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open Excalidraw, create a drawing.
2. Close the browser/tab.
3. Reopen Excalidraw – there’s no option to access previous work.

---

### 🌟 Suggestion
Implement a basic **Recent Drawings** feature that could:
- Use local storage for recent sessions.
- (Or even better!) Use cloud storage if supported, so work is accessible across devices.

Thanks for considering this!

title: Feature Request: Increase Zoom-Out Ratio for Better Board Overview
body:
**Description:**

I would like to request an enhancement to the zoom functionality of the tool to allow users to zoom out further. This adjustment would make it easier to view the entire board in one frame, especially when working with larger boards.

### Problem

Currently, the maximum zoom-out limit restricts users from seeing the full extent of large boards. This limitation makes navigation and board management cumbersome, as users have to scroll frequently to see different sections of the board.

### Proposed Solution

Increase the zoom-out ratio to enable users to view the entire board on a single screen. This could be done by:
- Allowing an additional level of zoom-out that accommodates larger boards.
- Adding finer control over the zoom scale to adjust according to board size.

### Benefits

- Enhanced user experience by enabling a complete view of large boards.
- Improved efficiency in navigating and managing content on extensive boards.

### Use Cases

- Project managers and team members need to quickly scan large boards without excessive scrolling.
- Users want to see the overall structure of complex boards for planning and collaboration.

### Additional Notes

If any feedback or specific examples would be helpful for development, I’d be happy to provide more details.

author: dwelle

title: Feature Request: Enhanced File Management and Workspace Features
body:
## Feature Request: Enhanced File Management and Workspace Features

### Description
I commonly make notes and concept diagrams in **Excalidraw** , using it as a **PWA** . Though accessing and viewing files from the default file system of my machine becomes tedious and time consuming. I am aware of **Excalidraw+**  but it is not built to work with the local storage.
I think that a better navigation and file access management can be implemented to work with the current PWA , without relying on any other database.

---

### Broad Outline for Proposed Features

1. **Folder Selection**:
   - Allow users to select a specific folder on their local file system as a workspace using the File System Access API.

2. **File Listing**:
   - Display all files and subfolders within the selected folder in a sidebar for quick access and organization.

3. **Load Saved Folder**:
   - On startup, check Local Storage for a previously selected folder and prompt the user to either use that folder or select a new one.

4. **Change Folder Option**:
   - Provide functionality for users to change the selected folder, updating the Current Workspace with the new selection.

5. **Multiple tab interface**:
   - Implementation of multiple tabs for viewing different files from the local storage, if such is possible.

6. **User-Friendly Interface**:
   - Create an intuitive user interface that integrates these file management features seamlessly with the existing drawing functionalities of Excalidraw.

### Benefits
- **Improved Organization**: Users can keep their projects organized within specific folders, making it easier to manage multiple drawings.
- **Enhanced User Experience**: Quick access to recently used files and folders improves overall usability.
- **Increased Efficiency**: Dynamically accessing files without needing to upload or manage them on a server streamlines the workflow for users.

---

I acknowledge that I know less than little of the application architecture and overall storage system . Therefore, please let me know if the features I’ve mentioned are feasible to implement.  Thank you for your consideration!

title: Feature request: predefined frame sizes
body:
Not sure if there is a better place for feature requests - if there is, please let me know so I can move this there!

## Predefined frame sizes

It would be great to be able to create frames at predefined sizes and proportions, such as 16:9 1080p or 4k

Basically allowing us to select a ratio and a size in pixels for frames.

## Auto align

A feature I also feel is missing is the ability to align elements while dragging them around the canvas.

In similar software, lines show up indicating alignment between close elements while dragging.

I feel this is critical for most use cases, from sketching UIs (where things will certainly be aligned) to graphs and all other kinds of canvases I can imagine wanting to build.

I basically don't think I'd ever want not to align things.

author: mtolmacs

title: Feature Request: Spaced node-style connections on shapes
body:
I work a lot in node-based animation and VFX programs like Houdini, Blender, Unreal Engine, etc, and I often sketch out node networks in my notes. In Excalidraw I've built up a library of node shapes for these programs, each consisting of the node shape grouped with circular connectors.  Here's a shot of my shapes library and how I use them in practice.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d3b13259-615c-4a22-9237-fe60770c2c4e)

While the library shapes method works, it's a bit of a time investment to set up. So this request is for shapes that auto create spaced input/output 'ports' as the arrow is connected. In Houdini for example, multiple connections looks like this:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/08e7f0ba-1f1d-4003-b8b9-51ae0902b181)

The above example shows a merge node taking inputs to one big port, though ideally there'd be an option for a port to be created with each connector, like the in/out ports on the top row of nodes.

title: FR: Easy to  grid selected images
body:
### Need for Image Organization
In my work, I often handle large volumes of images that need to be arranged in grids for clarity and visual appeal. An efficient way to organize images in a grid would save time and enhance readability.

### Solution Found in Miro
In Miro, an online whiteboard, I discovered a helpful feature: after selecting multiple images, a control button appears on the right side of the selection box. Dragging this button allows quick grid adjustments, creating a clean, organized layout.

### Request for Excalidraw
However, I rely on Excalidraw for most of my work. Adding a similar grid feature to Excalidraw would greatly improve its usability, allowing for professional, organized layouts with ease.

title: Increase text element padding in rectangle
body:
As of now, the text padding inside the rectangle is way too small, so it's often necessary to manually add the spaces to achieve a more pleasant look.

Instead, we shall increase the padding, so that no manual "alignment" is necessary.

Things to keep in mind:
- it might be a regression -> double-check
- do not create a breaking change for existing diagrams (after a manual redraw, like entering wysiwyg, it's likely fine)
- don't forget about padding while editing (wysiwyg)

author: BearDad

title: [Feature Request] Add Master Frame/Template functionality similar to PowerPoint master slides
body:
### Problem Description
When creating multiple frames in Excalidraw+, there's currently no way to maintain consistent elements (like logos, headers, footers) across all frames. Each element needs to be manually copied and placed in each frame individually.

### Proposed Solution
Implement a master frame/template feature similar to PowerPoint's master slides, where:
- Users can define a master frame with common elements
- All new frames automatically inherit these elements
- Updates to master elements reflect across all frames
- Option to override master elements in specific frames if needed

### Use Cases
1. Adding company logos to all frames in a presentation
2. Maintaining consistent header/footer information
3. Applying common background elements or watermarks
4. Ensuring brand consistency across multiple frames

### Benefits
- Saves time in creating consistent multi-frame documents
- Reduces manual work when updating common elements
- Ensures consistency across all frames
- Makes it easier to maintain brand guidelines

title: Lock position of elements
body:
I often use Excalidraw with a digital pen, and I’ve noticed that accidentally moving elements is a recurring issue. Whenever I touch elements on the interface with the pen, they tend to move unintentionally.

To address this, I suggest implementing a "Lock Position" option that prevents elements from being accidentally moved while still allowing other interactions.

This option could be added to the context menu (right-click menu), alongside the existing "Lock" feature. Unlike the current "Lock" option—which completely disables interaction with the element—this new feature would allow users to:

- Edit text blocks without risk of moving the element.
- Add text to elements or other elements in a natural workflow.
- Resize small elements intuitively without unintentional displacement.

author: scalenow

title: UI Enhancement: Resolve Tool Panel Overlap with Canvas Content
body:
# UI Enhancement: Resolve Tool Panel Overlap with Canvas Content
#UIEnhacement
## Issue Type
🎨 UI Enhancement

## Current Behavior
The pen and text tools panel currently overlaps with the canvas content, creating several usability issues:
1. The fixed position of the tools panel can obstruct content being edited
2. When working with text or drawings near the left side, the tools panel interferes with the workspace
3. Users need to constantly move their cursor around the panel to access content behind it

## Expected Behavior
The tools panel should:
- Not interfere with canvas content
- Remain easily accessible
- Can be drag able or collapse able
- Maintain full functionality while preventing content obstruction

## Proposed Solution
Consider implementing one or more of these solutions:

1. **Collapsible Panel**
   - Add a toggle button to collapse/expand the tools panel
   - Show only icons when collapsed
   - Expand on hover/click to show full options

2. **Draggable Panel**
   - Allow users to drag the panel to their preferred position
   - Remember position between sessions
   - Include a "reset position" option

3. **Context-Aware Positioning**
   - Automatically adjust panel position based on:
     - Active canvas area
     - Current cursor position
     - Available screen space



## UI/UX Considerations
- Maintain tool accessibility
- Preserve current functionality
- Ensure smooth transitions
- Consider mobile responsiveness
- Account for different screen sizes

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Panel collapse/expand functionality
- [ ] Position persistence
- [ ] Mobile compatibility
- [ ] Cross-browser compatibility
- [ ] Touch device support
- [ ] Performance impact

## Screenshots
Current UI with conflict 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f4e1ba4-d00d-4eee-8372-591750ffdc3e)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2cb35899-7bb7-45ca-bb75-561c074df770)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b231975-57c8-44fc-9372-ad2dea3c869f)


## Environment
- **Browser:** All modern browsers
- **OS:** Cross-platform
- **Screen sizes:** Desktop and mobile


## Labels
- UI/UX
- Enhancement
- User Experience
- Priority: High


Would you like me to modify or expand any part of this issue report?

title: Feature Request: Can Excalidraw Create Visualizations in Stepwise Format?
body:
### Desired Format

1. **Structured Workflows:**
   Stepwise representation of processes.
2. **Hierarchical Diagrams:**
   Visualization of layered concepts effectively.
3. **Interactive Components:**
  Editable diagrams allowing customization and collaboration.

**Reference Material**

A detailed example of the desired format is available here: [Reference File on Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1r87ZqG0ZiBJbuOLQO-15fDR0g6AlWMVe/view?pli=1)

**Current Limitations**

Excalidraw currently has:

Limited templates for structured and hierarchical diagrams.
Minimal support for complex workflows with interactive components.

By introducing these features, Excalidraw could expand its usability to cater to a broader range of users, making it a versatile tool for various workflows and collaborative tasks.
Thank you for considering this request! For further details or clarifications, feel free to reach out.

title: Option to Lock Sizes within a Group
body:
Currently, when a group of items is scaled, all items inside the group are scaled proportionally. There is no feature to lock the dimensions of specific items within the group.

Feature Request:
Add an option to lock the dimensions of individual items in a group so they remain unchanged during group scaling. This would provide more flexibility when working with grouped elements, especially when certain items need to maintain their original size.

author: handsome-xiaowang

title: Performance Issue on Huawei devices
body:
Dear Excalidraw Team,

I hope this message finds you well. I am a fan of Excalidraw, particularly because of its unique doodle style of drawing and text. However, I am experiencing significant performance issues when using Excalidraw on both of my Huawei MatePad 10.4" running Harmony OS 2.0.0 and MatePad Pro 13.2" (regarded as the best hardware of huawei at the moment) running Harmony OS 4.0.0.

During my testing, I found that the performance of Excalidraw is quite poor compared to tldraw. While using Excalidraw, I can barely draw on the canvas due to lag and unresponsiveness, whereas tldraw performs smoothly without any issues. This discrepancy in performance is quite disappointing, especially since I enjoy using Excalidraw for its intuitive interface and drawing capabilities.
To help illustrate the problem, I have included a video demonstrating the performance issues I encountered while using Excalidraw on my device. 
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a9757476-d6d2-46f8-b0c2-685bc1a50f9e

If there is anything I can do to assist in resolving this issue or if you need further information from my end, please let me know. I appreciate your attention to this matter and look forward to any updates you may have.
Thank you for your hard work on this fantastic tool!

Best regards,
Anthony
author: antlaw

title: FR: Change no-arrowhead buttons to a greyed out arrowhead (in place of a regular line)
body:
A small UX improvement request on my side: it is currently unclear the two buttons work for arrowhead setting. I literally had to google it and only after arriving to https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/616 had I realized, toggling back and forth between github and the whiteboard that the little straight line is indeed a setting for the other arrowhead.

One example of how this could be solved would be to display a greyed out arrow head in the other icon, in place of a black straight line. This would achieve two objectives
1. Inform that clicking this button leads to something related to arrowheads
2. Indicate that it's currently inactive (grey)

Currently, the buttons focus on how the whole line looks (a straight line) rather than on focusing on the end points (pointy arrows)

title: Issue with Double-Tap Text Input on Excalidraw (Windows Tablet with Stylus)
body:
When using a stylus on a Windows tablet, there's an issue with the double-tap shortcut for entering text. If the double-tap is performed quickly, the text input field appears momentarily and then closes automatically before any text can be written. However, if the double-tap is slower, the input field stays open, allowing text entry. Interestingly, this problem does not occur when performing the double-tap with a finger, where the text input works as expected.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Open Excalidraw on a Windows tablet with a stylus.
2. Attempt to create a text input field by double-tapping the screen quickly with the stylus.
3. Observe that the text input field appears and then closes immediately.
4. Repeat the action, but this time perform the double-tap more slowly.
5. Notice that the input field stays open, allowing text entry.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7b4af39-7ce7-47a0-b689-642b60325b57

Additionally, the same issue has been observed when using the Excalidraw plugin in Obsidian https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/1827

title: Issue with Image Embedding in SVG Output
body:
Hello,

Excalidraw is an awesome and easy-to-use tool! However, in my opinion, the main drawback is the way it handles image embedding for user-imported images. It embeds imported images as Base64 in the SVG output, which is not standard for SVG files and makes the output incompatible with programs like Illustrator and PowerPoint.

I hope you will consider addressing this issue.

Thank you!

title: Cursor reappearing while writing on tablet
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/8922
Originally posted by **happyfunction** December 17, 2024 When using the pen tool, the crosshair is always displayed when using the mouse, whether you are writing (pressing the left mouse button) or just moving the cursor. However, when using a digital pen and a graphics tablet, the cursor disappears when the digital pen touches the graphics tablet. When the digital pen leaves the graphics tablet, the cursor reappears. If you write relatively fast, the cursor will appear and disappear from time to time, and this kind of experience is not good.

author: hartmut-co-uk

title: [Feature Request] Flowchart is almost there...
body:
The flowchart is great! It's what I use to quickly break down text and information into fragments of knowledge and relate them together. To properly digest and make that knowledge mine.

There are a few features that would immensely increase the efficiency and quality of life though. 

1. Automatic rectangles placement/modifcations - As of now you can initially create new flowchart boxes, but once you have the need to add another box to a node with children, the nodes overlap, and you have to manually drag it. Sometimes this can cause misalignment if you don't use the grid. It'd be nice if nodes were smart enough when doing this operation and move other nodes above and below it to make space. There could be a function to recursively 'ripple' and push nodes away.

2. Similar to above, but for nodes where you add in more text and expand it such that by the end of the operation it covers another node. Covered nodes should be pushed away so the diagram is readable. This would definitely save time in editing flowcharts.

In summary, when a node is placed or expands such that another node is covered, then there should be a recursive macro that ripples through all the nodes and ensures readability and optimal placement of nodes.

3. Flowchart integration - As of now, flowcharts are agnostic to all other elements, it'd be nice to drag an image or element to a flowchart and immediate have it connected to a node. Manually placing arrows is ugly and not as so nice and uniform as the flowchart, at least with my skills.

4. Tying into the above. Commonly I have lots of ocr'd text that I edit and refine into fragments of knowledge. Dragging them onto a node and making these automatically into nodes full of text would be great.

title: Embed pages from Inkdrop

[Inkdrop](https://www.inkdrop.app/) is a note-taking app. It also allows you to share your notes [publicly](https://docs.inkdrop.app/reference/share-notes-on-the-web).

author: MahiroCh

title: Remote element drag updates are slow
body:
**Reproduction**
1. Connect two excalidraw clients (A and B) through a shared scene 
1. Create freedraw element on client A
1. Drag the freedraw element on client A
1. Observe the updates being reflected in slow-mo speeds on client B

**Technical details**

The issue seems to lie in shape cache misses, likely due to remote elements being always new instances and hence always missing the cache & re-generating from scratch.

**Notes**
- Probably does not affect just freedraw, though the difference there might be the most noticable.
- See the related screenshots, capturing the bottleneck (freedraw shape re-generation) which repeats on every single remote update.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e173ec79-82e6-4467-94b1-3a970d3a8e06)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/318a7006-3b0e-45fb-a59c-30296c26fe99)
author: Mrazator

title: Feature: both a background color and a scratch fill type?
body: I've noticed that quite often I need to stack shapes on top of one another for some fancier constructions. I use the non-solid fill types to highlight various things, but given that their background is transparent (apart of the shading), stacking them leaks the thing underneath. My current solution is to have 2 of every shape. An "underlay" with a solid white color, and then my actual shape on top with the needed shading. As seen on the above image, this does work, but it needs a lot of effort to duplicate all shapes and handle overlaps between them, so sometimes I end up forgetting or just not wanting to bother (see the outer red shape's 4 corners, each bleeding the black strokes from underneath). Wondering if it would be an interesting idea to add a non-transparent option for the shaded fill type? I'm aware that this would all of a sudden require *2* background colors, so happy to be told no, just thought I'd bring it up as food for thought.

title: creating an elbow arrow from a frame adds it to the frame incorrectly
body:
When creating an elbow arrow by clicking a bit inside the shape (in this case the frame), it binds it to the shape as expected, but in case of a frame it also adds it to the frame as a child because you initially clicked inside the frame when creating the elbow arrow. This puts the arrow completely outside the frame and doesn't render it.

Instead, if we detect the the arrow is being bound to the frame during creation, we should never add it to the frame as a child (-> `frameId: null`)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bc9cef4c-1e1c-4804-849d-164d35f2c0d5)

author: robmcphers0n

title: [Feature Request] Custom File Name for New Embedded Drawing
body:
# Description

Currently, users can insert a static string between the note name ({NOTE_NAME}) and the date when saving Excalidraw files. However, different users prefer different naming conventions based on their workflows. A more flexible approach would allow users to define custom naming patterns, similar to how the *Paste image rename* plugin enables structured image file names.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/884d49a0-c2a2-4bff-9380-6fd29155d5c2)

# Proposed Enhancement

Enable dynamic file name formatting for Excalidraw files, allowing users to define a custom pattern using placeholders. This would ensure that Excalidraw file names align with users’ preferred naming and organizational conventions.

## Example Use Case

I use the *Paste image rename* plugin to rename images in the following format:

`{DATE:YYYY-MM-DD_HH-mm-ss}_{imageNameKey}`

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fa0c159b-dada-41c1-9e32-2f143092ec23)

Where:
- `{DATE:YYYY-MM-DD_HH-mm-ss}` ensures chronological sorting.
- `{imageNameKey}` links the image to the file it belongs to.

A similar approach for Excalidraw files would allow users to define custom file names using placeholders:
- `{NOTE_NAME}` – the name of the attached note
- `{imageNameKey}` – a user-defined property of the file
- `{DATE:YYYY-MM-DD_HH-mm-ss}` – a timestamp

## Expected Behavior

Users should be able to configure their preferred naming convention in settings, using a customizable template such as:

- `embed_{DATE:YYYY-MM-DD_HH-mm-ss}_{NOTE_NAME}`
- `{DATE:YYYY-MM-DD_HH-mm-ss}_{imageNameKey}`

This would enable:
- Better association between Excalidraw files and their parent notes – With an imageNameKey, the association remains intact even if the file name changes.
- Chronological sorting.
- Consistency with existing attachment/file naming conventions.

# Conclusion

Implementing this feature would give users greater flexibility in defining their own file naming conventions, ensuring better organization, consistency, and seamless integration with their existing workflows.

title: "Unsupported File Type" Error dialog shows up when embedding images on Samsung devices after a recent ONEUI update
body:
This issue is not specific to Obsidian or excalidraw. Samsung's recent updated messed up the image embedding mechanism. The image embedding mechanism may need to be changed for Samsung devices. The original issue reported: [(Issue)](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/2245#issue-2828428289)


# My Findings [(comment)](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/2245#issuecomment-2633730166)


> This happened to someone I know as well. From the troubleshooting I did, it turns out that this is not an Excalidraw issue. I tried downgrading, but importing images still results in an "Unsupported File Type" error dialog.
> 
> The issue occurred after the person updated their Samsung tablet. It seems that Samsung may have changed the image importing mechanism in a recent update, causing incompatibility. This is also not an Obsidian-specific issue because importing images from the Excalidraw free whiteboard web interface also doesn't work. This appears to be entirely on Samsung, though the Excalidraw developers might need to change the image selection method.
> 
> ## List of troubleshooting steps I followed:
> * [ ]  Updated Obsidian  (fail)
> * [ ]  Downgraded Excalidraw to v2.7.5 (fail)
> * [ ]  Tried uploading an image to the web interface (fail)
> * [x]  Tried uploading an image from the web interface on IOS, Windows, and Samsung's older ONEUI 5.1 (all work)
> 
> > **Conclusion**: This issue needs to be reported upstream to Excalidraw's main repository

author: knackstedt

title: Single script import does not work out-of-the-box
body:
With new ESM bundle, we should still support a single script import (with react & react-dom), so that the editor is easily embeddable with a `<script>` element, without the need for additional bundlers, but also without us providing a huge `browser` bundle with all dependencies inside.

The idea is to rely on a smart CDN such as `esm.run` from `jsdelivr`, which would bundle our dev / prod bundles for us. However, as of now, it does work out-of-the-box, which we should fix as part of the issue. We will likely need to adjust few problematic imports.

> Error: "Uncaught SyntaxError: The requested module '/npm/pako@1.0.11/+esm' does not provide an export named 'inflate'"

https://jsfiddle.net/4f3c6q8s/9/

In the future, let's consider publishing `@excalidraw/browser` or similar, as suggested in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/8104.

author: retro-dwsz

title: Finalize our new ESM bundle structure
body:
The main bundle should be `dist/prod` and `dist/dev` respectively:

- `dist/browser` should go, reasoning in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/9137
- types should be separated into a `types` subfolder
	- it seems we will need to have `excalidraw`, `utils` and `math` sub-subfolders inside, due to our mono repo structure
- fonts should be separated into a separate `fonts` subfolder
	- transforming the woff2 imports into "file" name as default exports (our esbuild plugin)
- locales should be separate into a `locales/` subfolder
	- transforming the json imports into esm (ideally), as json imports need to be processed by a bundler and so that the esm import works without one (even though it does not work yet out-of-the-box)
- all docs & related examples should be updated to use the new bundle, so there is no confusion of which bundle to use and how

Issues to investigate:
- [x] `utils` are more server-oriented, though they might deserve `browser` bundle as well (I.e. without the fonts being embedded, chunked into tree-shakable sub-modules, etc.)
- [x] `math` package does not seem to be built / published

author: benberli

title: Prefer dist over src inside our yarn workspace
body:
So that we:
- don't build from source
- don't import anything from any subpath (~don't import things that are not not exported in the final bundle)
- don't have to duplicate build related specifics in vite & esbuild (defining woff2 plugins, aliases, chunks / assets)
- don't have to hack ts aliases to go into src (vite, esbuild, tsc)
- don't have to hack types to go into dist/types/{excalidraw,utils,math}/
- can have `@excalidraw` scoped packages referencing each other, without each being a standalone unit & bundling everything on it's own
- can test the built packages with OSS app (shortens feedback loop, allows to discover library specific bugs sooner -> less bugs in library, less bugs in E+)
- pushes us to think deeper about the public API of packages, so that we can't just import anything from any subpath

---

- [ ] Consider using references & composite in tsconfig
- [ ] Share most of the build* scripts logic, instead of the duplicated scripts
- [ ] Share a common tsconfig between packages instead of the duplicate configs
- [ ] Rewrite all the exports so that there are not "subpath" exports (there are no subpaths in the bundle)
- [ ] Get rid of circular dependencies between packages (i.e. utils & excalidraw)
- [ ] Get rid of duplicate plugins/settings in vite (i.e. woff2 won't be needed anymore, as esbuild will already do the job)
- [ ] Building app should build all the packages automatically
- [ ] Changing a package should automatically rebuild the package (watch mode) & the app

title: Add Support for Bilibili (B站) Integration
body:
I’d like to request the addition of Bilibili (commonly known as B站), a popular video-sharing platform in China, to the supported services/features of this project. Integrating Bilibili could expand the project’s reach and functionality, especially for users in the Chinese market or those interested in content hosted on this platform.
Proposed Features:  
Support for embedding or linking Bilibili videos.  
Ability to fetch metadata (e.g., title, description, thumbnail) from Bilibili URLs.  
Optional: Compatibility with Bilibili’s API (if available) for enhanced functionality.
Use Case:
Many users rely on Bilibili for video content, similar to how YouTube or other platforms are used globally. Adding this support would make the project more versatile and appealing to a broader audience.
Additional Notes:  
Bilibili’s official website: https://www.bilibili.com/  
https://www.bilibili.com/ 
If needed, I can assist in providing more details or testing the integration.
Looking forward to hearing your thoughts on this! Thank you!

title: Scalidraw + code generation + LLMs
body:
Guys, I am new in the community, but i have an idea:
If we integrate scalidraw with a javascript code generation tool that generate code based on scalidraw templates and connects the inputs and outputs of connections similar to the n8n tool, we could make a plugin that transforms scalidraw and obsidian in a IDE.

The benefit of this is to allow the rapid prototyping. If the templates are connected with react or svelte components code for example, you could generate the code for the frontend from the site wireframe.

Connect the scallidraw to templates of a backend framework like nestjs and you create an api.
Connect to teraform teplates, and you have the cloud infrastructure.
Connect to some webgl integration lib in frontend, like PIXI and you have a game
Connect to python tensorflow templates and you have an AI model
Connect to blender+python blender scripts templates and you have a 3d model building tool, perfect for engineering
Conect to some animation framework like manim for mathematics and you can easily create animation videos

Connect this whole system to an speciallized LLM and you have the ability to create almost anithing through natural language, but with much more agility, precision and a documented firs approach (the documentation would alway be sincronized with the code because de documentation IS the code)

Maybe this already exists. If so, could you please send me a link? If not, lets build it?

title: Text elements created with `convertToExcalidrawElements` have incorrect metrics
body:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe26b556-1e0e-444e-b5b8-e27653d50b35

https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/github/excalidraw/excalidraw/tree/mrazator/release-v18/examples/with-script-in-browser

This happens likely due to `measureText` being executed before the fonts even load, while subsequently we don't trigger another redraw (so that we don't break existing diagrams).

What we could do instead is:
- wait for the fonts to load, before starting `convertToExcalidrawElements` and `measureText` (note that in an edge case, fonts might not load at all)
- schedule a redraw for the particular elements created with `convertToExcalidrawElements`, so they get redrawn once the fonts are available (including redraw of text container)

title: Feature Request: Auto-convert Hand-drawn Shapes to Predefined Shapes in Freehand Mode
body:
### Proposed Feature
Add an optional toggle to ​**freehand drawing mode** that automatically converts hand-drawn shapes into precise predefined shapes when detecting:
- Perfect circles ○  
- Squares □  
- Triangles △  
- Diamonds ◆  
- Arrow lines →  

### Benefits
This would allow users to:
1. Quickly select shapes through natural drawing gestures
2. Reduce reliance on keyboard shortcuts
3. Maintain creative flow while ensuring geometric precision

### Implementation Suggestion
- Add a toggle button in the toolbar (🔘 "Shape Assist")
- Support adjustable recognition sensitivity
- Optional visual feedback during conversion

---

*This is an incredibly powerful tool for organizing complex ideas. Implementing this feature would make shape selection even more intuitive than using shortcuts. Thank you for creating such a valuable tool for the world!*

### Description:

### Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open Excalidraw in a split-screen view (e.g., alongside another app/browser).
2. Observe that the drawing toolbar takes up a large portion of the screen without an option to hide it.

A toggle button to hide and show the drawing toolbar should be available to improve the screen space.

### Actual Behavior:
The drawing toolbar remains open, limiting the workspace in split view.

title: No longer possible to bind by moving non-arrow object, but it's still handeled in the code.
body:
If I go back about 400 commits I can do this: 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09bc2b9f-e9ed-4b2f-a51f-0157020baf11

But in more recent versions it's no longer possible (this is from staging.excalidraw.com):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1f525e7e-a9ad-47b0-8b19-da6353a38f7b


In the code if I remove the other option for the `SuggestedBinding` like this,
```
export type SuggestedBinding = NonDeleted<ExcalidrawBindableElement>;
  // | SuggestedPointBinding;

export type SuggestedPointBinding = [
  NonDeleted<ExcalidrawLinearElement>,
  "start" | "end" | "both",
  NonDeleted<ExcalidrawBindableElement>,
];
```
and then run `yarn test:typecheck` it runs without throwing any errors.


If I am correct, then this part:
```  const renderHighlight = Array.isArray(suggestedBinding)
    ? renderBindingHighlightForSuggestedPointBinding
    : renderBindingHighlightForBindableElement;
``` 
and the `renderBindingHighlightForSuggestedPointBinding` are redundant, and should be removed from the codebase.

I didn't spend too much time on this, so I'm not sure when this feature was removed and why, please let me know. I'd also be happy to work through the pull request for this issue.

author: kindermoumoute

title: move to `iTXt` png chunks
body:
When encoding scene data to PNGs (see https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/data/image.ts)

turns out `tEXt` chunks support latin1 only, but we're encoding the data as utf8. Browsers don't care, but it may throw off other PNG decoders.

Instead, we should use `iTXt` chunks which [support utf8](http://www.libpng.org/pub/png/spec/1.2/PNG-Chunks.html).

~~https://www.npmjs.com/package/png-itxt~~ [png-chunk-itxt](https://www.npmjs.com/package/png-chunk-itxt)

We still need to keep `tEXt` support for backwards compat.
author: dwelle

title: Bug: Element Duplication Issue in Excalidraw
body:
### **Bug: Element Duplication Issue in Excalidraw**  

#### **Description:**  
When duplicating any element in Excalidraw, the duplicated element is not appearing on top. Instead, it moves down and remains hidden. Even when moving the cursor, the element does not come to the front, making it difficult to use the duplication feature effectively.  

#### **Steps to Reproduce:**  
1. Open Excalidraw.  
2. Create any shape or element.  
3. Use the duplicate function (Ctrl + D for Duplicate).  
4. Observe that the duplicated element moves downward instead of appearing on top.  
5. Try to move the cursor over the duplicated element—it does not come to the front.  

#### **Expected Behavior:**  
The duplicated element should appear directly above the original element and remain fully visible without being pushed down.  

#### **Actual Behavior:**  
The duplicated element moves downward and remains hidden, making it hard to locate and use.  


#### **Attachments:**  
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ff99ab19-c295-4db6-abba-ff491f305e02)

#### **Priority:**  
🔴 **Major Issue** – This affects usability, especially when working with multiple elements.  

#### **Possible Fixes/Suggestions:**  
- Ensure that duplicated elements appear at a higher z-index.  
- Adjust the duplication logic to place new elements directly above the original.  
- Fix the cursor interaction issue so the element appears as expected.

author: sgorman1

title: FR: Maintain Line Angle When Modifying Existing Lines
body:
**Description:**
Currently, Excalidraw allows users to draw lines at fixed angles using the **Shift** key (every 15°). However, when modifying an existing line by dragging one of its endpoints, there is no way to constrain the adjustment to maintain the original angle. This makes precise modifications difficult, especially for technical drawings.

**Proposed Feature:**
Introduce a keyboard shortcut that allows users to preserve the original angle of a line when modifying it. For example:
- Holding **Shift** while dragging an endpoint should constrain the movement to maintain the initial angle of the line.
- Alternatively, a combination like **Alt + Shift** or **Ctrl + Shift** could be used if Shift alone is reserved for other functions.

**Benefits:**
- Ensures consistency in structured diagrams.
- Improves usability for users working on precise drawings.
- Aligns with existing behavior of constrained angle drawing but extends it to modifications.

**Possible Implementation:**
- Detect when a user is modifying a line and pressing the defined shortcut.
- Calculate the closest point along the original angle where the endpoint can be repositioned.
- Snap the endpoint accordingly.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38301399-7e03-4897-9c78-7ac5a2fb3916
author: HoodyEz

title: FR: Per-Segment Stroke Style for Lines
body:
**Description:**
Currently, Excalidraw allows users to change the stroke style (solid, dashed, or dotted) for entire lines. However, when creating 3D geometric shapes or technical diagrams, users often need to represent hidden edges with dashed lines while keeping visible edges solid. The only current workaround is to create separate independent line segments, which reduces efficiency and makes modifications more cumbersome.

**Proposed Feature:**
Allow users to apply different stroke styles to individual segments within a single line. For example:
- A user could select a line and adjust the stroke style between any two points.
- A new UI option in the stroke settings could enable per-segment styling.
- Users could right-click a segment to toggle between solid, dashed, or dotted styles.

**Benefits:**
- Enhances flexibility for technical drawings, architecture, and 3D illustrations.
- Reduces the need to create multiple separate lines just to represent different visibility states.
- Improves consistency and editability of complex diagrams.

**Possible Implementation:**
- Extend the existing line data structure to store stroke styles per segment.
- Modify the rendering engine to respect per-segment styling.
- Introduce a UI element (e.g., context menu or properties panel) to allow easy modification.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e81f7c8-6c0e-4c23-8691-8c0ce54da49a

author: rbavery

title: Webpack 5 Compatibility: Missing .js Extensions in Imports for roughjs modules.
body:
I’m using the @excalidraw/excalidraw package in my React project, and I encountered an issue when using Webpack 5. The project is failing to compile due to missing file extensions in the imports for roughjs modules.


`Module not found: Error: Can't resolve 'roughjs/bin/rough' in 'node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/dev'
Did you mean 'rough.js'?
BREAKING CHANGE: The request 'roughjs/bin/rough' failed to resolve only because it was resolved as fully specified.
`

This issue occurs because Webpack 5 requires imports to be fully specified in strict EcmaScript modules (ESM). The imports for roughjs in the Excalidraw codebase (e.g., import rough from 'roughjs/bin/rough';) are missing the .js extension.

2) Make use of this new more idiomatically named function by refactoring the files where this formula is used to use the common function. 

Some of the files I found as good candidates for review:
- packages/excalidraw/element/binding.ts
- packages/excalidraw/element/collision.ts

author: mtolmacs

title: Refactor point types ` { x, y }` to the proper unified point type in `@excalidraw/math`
body:
Goal: Have a unified point type system across the codebase. 

- Create missing point type `ViewportPoint` = [x, y] in `@excalidraw/math`
- Create unified point type `GenericPoint` in `@excalidraw/math`
- Refactor point generic function type parameters to extend `GenericPoint` where applicable
- Refactor object point types (i.e. `{ x: number, y: number }` to use GlobalPoint, LocalPoint, ViewportPoint or a combination as applicable

Note: Watch out for `rough-js` point types, which are also `[x,y]` but cannot be refactored. Avoid type casting as much as possible.

title: Subtext in shapes

title: FR:would you consider to add task components for elements?
body:
Hey there,
With AI being so popular these days, most whiteboard tools are starting to integrate AI features like text processing, image enhancement, etc.
Given this trend, I think it would be really cool to add some kind of task component system that lets users define and process tasks on their elements. This could be super useful for things like enhancing images, formatting text, or other element-specific operations.
I'd love to see something like this in Excalidraw - maybe as an optional plugin that users can choose to install based on their needs?
What do you think? Would you consider adding something like this to future versions?
Thanks!
Below is generate by AI,maybe it could let you unstand me more.
# Feature Request: Task Processing Center for Elements

## Overview
I'd like to propose adding a "Task Center" component that would allow users to perform various processing tasks on selected elements. This would enable powerful element transformations and AI-powered enhancements directly within Excalidraw.

## Use Cases
- **Image Elements**: AI-powered enhancements, background removal, style transfer, etc.
- **Text Elements**: Smart formatting, translation, summarization, style consistency
- **Shapes/Lines**: Auto-alignment, style normalization, distribution
- **Groups**: Batch processing with consistent styling

## Proposed Implementation
A task center could be implemented as:
1. A context-aware panel that shows available tasks based on selected element types
2. A modal interface for task configuration and execution
3. Support for both synchronous and asynchronous processing with visual feedback

## User Experience
When elements are selected, users would see available tasks in a dedicated panel. Tasks would be categorized by element type and processing type. After selecting a task, users could configure parameters and execute the process, with visual feedback on progress and results.

## Technical Considerations
- Task processors could be pluggable to allow for community extensions
- Processing could happen client-side for simple tasks, or connect to external services for AI capabilities
- Results should be non-destructive (original elements preserved or easily reverted)

## Benefits
- Enhances Excalidraw's capabilities without complicating the main interface
- Provides a consistent framework for advanced element manipulation
- Opens possibilities for community-developed task processors
- Maintains Excalidraw's simplicity while adding power-user features

Would the team be open to considering such a feature for future development? I believe it would significantly enhance Excalidraw's utility while maintaining its elegant simplicity.
author: T-Curiosity

title: Losing Text Field Focus When Switching Tabs
body:
## Summary  
When editing a text element in Excalidraw, switching to another application or browser tab causes the text field to lose focus. Upon returning, I must manually click the text field again to continue editing.

## Steps to Reproduce  
1. Open Excalidraw and create a new drawing.  
2. Add a text element and start typing.  
3. Use **Alt + Tab** (or equivalent shortcut) to switch to another application or browser tab.  
4. Switch back to the Excalidraw tab and try to continue typing.  

## Expected Behavior  
The text field should retain focus so that I can continue typing immediately after returning to Excalidraw.  

## Actual Behavior  
The text field loses focus, requiring a manual click to re-select the field before continuing to type.  


## Additional Context  
This issue disrupts the workflow when referencing content from other apps while creating text elements in Excalidraw.

title: Bug : arrows bind themselves to a shape they were previously unbound of
body:
When toggling arrow binding, be it by pressing Ctrl when moving an arrow tip, or by using the toggle in Obsidian's comment palette, the other end of the arrow binds itself back to the adjacent shape. I was able to reproduce the issue on Obsidian as well as on the official website.

Here is a screen capture of this issue : 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/043224e2-9766-4db1-8d26-e6081632a01f

The steps to reproduce are as follows : 

1. Create a new diagram. Draw two rectangles a few units apart. Let's call the first rectangle A and the second B.
2. Draw an arrow from A to B and let it bind to the shapes on both of its ends. Check that the two ends are bound to their respective shapes by moving both shapes around.
3. Unbind the arrow from A by left-clicking and holding its end, dragging it then repositioning it while holding the Ctrl key. Check that this end is not bound to A anymore by moving the A shape around. Then reposition A. Do the same with B to confirm that B is still bound to the other arrow tip.
4. Repeat the same operation on B side : unbind the arrow from B by moving its tip and repositioning it while holding Ctrl. Check that B is not bound anymore to this tip by moving B around.
5. **The other end adjacent to A is now bound to A, instead of staying unbound from step 3**

title: Allow disabling web workers
body:
When upgrading Excalidraw in TriliumNext we noticed that there is a new error displayed in the console:

```log
Failed to use workers for subsetting, falling back to the main thread. SecurityError: Failed to construct 'Worker': Script at 'file:///C:/Users/elian/Projects/TriliumNext/Notes/node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/dev/subset-worker.chunk.js' cannot be accessed from origin 'http://localhost:8080'.
```

The error itself does not cause any issues, since it falls back to using the main thread. However we would prefer to get rid of it to avoid it being reported by users.

From what I saw, the error comes from:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/0cd5a259ae6e6db87fa5af2b0466426d6920bb45/packages/excalidraw/subset/subset-main.ts#L63

The check if web workers are possible is done via:

```typescript
let shouldUseWorkers = typeof Worker !== "undefined";
```

The issue seems to be that web workers are trying to be used, but our server is on HTTP in this case.

Would it be possible to allow configure whether web workers are allowed or not, to bypass this error? Or adjust `shouldUseWorkers` to check whether the protocol is HTTPS.

title: ALLOW_SAME_ORIGIN does not inherit from the validateEmbeddable logic in custom apps
body:
Unless I am mistaken in my usage of "validateEmbeddable" variable when instantiating a new custom Excalidraw app, then, unfortunately the `ALLOW_SAME_ORIGIN` const in `embeddable.ts` does not inherit from the logic passed to validateEmbeddable when evaluating "allowSameOrigin" here:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/c79e892e558ca88de6623fd60517af7bb2086e77/packages/element/src/embeddable.ts#L104-L106

This can create situations where you are able to customize which domains you allow, but could still face CORS/Same-Origin issues for these

author: vicradon

title: Build fails in Docker container due to PWA file size limitation
body:
## Bug Description
When attempting to build Excalidraw using Docker (via `docker-compose up -d`), the build process fails due to a file size limitation in the workbox PWA configuration. The main JavaScript bundle exceeds the default 2 MiB limit for precaching.

## Error Message
Configure "workbox.maximumFileSizeToCacheInBytes" to change the limit: the default value is 2 MiB.
Check https://vite-pwa-org.netlify.app/guide/faq.html#missing-assets-from-sw-precache-manifest for more information.
Assets exceeding the limit:
 - assets/index-DlRwqTH8.js is 2.3 MB, and won't be precached.
39.80
39.80     at logWorkboxResult (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite-plugin-pwa/dist/chunk-G4TAN34B.js:44:13)
39.80     at generateServiceWorker (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite-plugin-pwa/dist/index.js:229:3)
39.80     at async _generateSW (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite-plugin-pwa/dist/index.js:362:5)
39.80     at async Object.handler (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite-plugin-pwa/dist/index.js:526:13)
39.80     at async PluginDriver.hookParallel (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite/node_modules/rollup/dist/es/shared/node-entry.js:21757:17)
39.80     at async Object.close (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite/node_modules/rollup/dist/es/shared/node-entry.js:22741:13)
39.80     at async build (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/dep-9A4-l-43.js:67191:13)
39.80     at async CAC.<anonymous> (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite/dist/node/cli.js:845:9)

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Clone the Excalidraw repository
2. Run `docker-compose up -d`
3. Observe the build failure with the error above

## Environment
- Docker version: 4.25.0
- OS: Windows 10
- Repository commit: 6fc85022ae6ad14e74271e071afc51a60b7dc3c5

## Suggested Fix
Based on the error message, the build is failing because the main JavaScript bundle (`assets/index-DlRwqTH8.js`) is 2.3 MB, which exceeds the default 2 MB limit for precaching in the workbox configuration.

This could be fixed by either:
1. Increasing the `maximumFileSizeToCacheInBytes` limit in the vite-plugin-pwa configuration
2. Implementing code splitting to reduce the main bundle size
3. Configuring the specific asset to be excluded from precaching

title: [Bug]: Zoom resets unexpectedly during annotation/drawing on whiteboard
body:
Description:
There is a frustrating issue with the whiteboard where the zoom level resets to a general view instead of staying at the same position. This typically happens in the middle of annotating or drawing, which is very annoying and interrupts the workflow.

Steps to Reproduce:
	1.	Zoom in on a specific area of the whiteboard.
	2.	Start annotating or drawing.
	3.	At some point during interaction, the view suddenly resets to a general zoom.

Expected Behavior:
The zoom should remain fixed at the user-defined level and position unless explicitly changed by the user.

Actual Behavior:
The zoom unexpectedly changes to a general overview, disrupting ongoing annotation or drawing work.

Impact:
This behavior makes it difficult to focus on specific areas while working and causes loss of precision and flow during tasks.

Environment:
	•	Device/OS: [e.g. Windows 11 / macOS Sonoma]
	•	Browser: [e.g. Chrome, Firefox]
	•	App version: [if applicable]

Request:
Please fix the zoom behavior so that it remains consistent during interaction. This would greatly improve usability and reduce frustration for users relying on detailed whiteboard work.
author: etplusco

title: [Bug]: Images disappear and show gray squares instead on whiteboard
body:
Description:
There’s a serious issue on the whiteboard where images that were previously added become invisible and are replaced by gray squares. This seems to happen after some time or after refreshing/reopening the whiteboard. It’s extremely frustrating and makes it nearly impossible to rely on the whiteboard for anything involving images.

Steps to Reproduce:
	1.	Add an image to the whiteboard.
	2.	Wait some time or reload/reopen the session.
	3.	The image is no longer visible—only a gray square appears in its place.

Expected Behavior:
Images should remain visible and correctly loaded in their original position, even after refreshing or reopening the whiteboard.

Actual Behavior:
Images randomly disappear and are replaced by gray placeholders. The image link appears to be broken or lost.

Impact:
This breaks the whiteboard’s usefulness for image-based work, such as diagrams, visual annotations, or reference material. It significantly affects productivity and makes the tool unreliable.

Environment:
	•	Device/OS: [e.g. Windows 10, macOS 13]
	•	Browser: [e.g. Chrome 123, Safari]
	•	App version: [if available]

Request:
Please investigate and fix the issue causing image links to break or disappear. Ensuring persistent image visibility is essential for users who depend on visuals in their work.
author: etplusco

title: Move Undo/Redo Buttons to Top Toolbar
body:
# Context
Currently, the Undo/Redo buttons are located in the bottom left corner of the Excalidraw interface. This placement can be less convenient for users who frequently use these functions, as it requires moving the mouse cursor away from the tools menu where it spends a lot of its time.

# Proposition
I propose moving the Undo/Redo next to the tools menu. This is a common placement for these essential functions in many design and drawing applications for a reason.

# Benefits
* Reduced mouse travel.
* Consistency with other applications (for improved familiarity).

I believe this small UI change could significantly improve the user experience for many Excalidraw users.
author: cos4ni2s

title: Event Recording and State Storage for Excalidraw Board
body:
Hi everyone!

I'm looking to implement a feature that records changes made on an Excalidraw board. I’ve recently integrated Excalidraw as microfrontend into my platform, which already supports code recording, and I’d love to extend the experience by capturing and replaying interactions with the board as well.

Could you help me understand the minimal setup or API hooks I’d need to start recording events and storing the board’s state effectively?

Thanks in advance!

author: sfu2008

title: Implement Mouse Scroll Wheel Zooming
body: # Context Currently, zooming the Excalidraw canvas with a mouse requires holding down the `Ctrl` key (or `Cmd` on macOS) while using the scroll wheel. While functional, this differs from the behaviour in many other visual tools (e.g., map software, image editors, design tools) where the scroll wheel directly controls zoom without a modifier key. For users accustomed to that interaction model, or those who prefer minimal modifier key usage for ergonomic reasons, the current requirement adds an extra step and can feel less intuitive. The default scroll wheel action is vertical panning, which might not be the primary desired interaction for all users when using the scroll wheel. # Proposition I propose adding an option in Excalidraw's settings to allow users to choose the behaviour of the mouse scroll wheel: - `Scroll Wheel = Zoom` Default | `Ctrl + Scroll Wheel = Vertical Pan` (and perhaps another panning modifier like `Shift + Scroll Wheel` for horizontal pan could be considered) - `Scroll Wheel = Pan Vertically` Legacy | `Ctrl + Scroll Wheel = Zoom` This would allow users to configure Excalidraw to match their preferred workflow or the behaviour they are used to from other applications.

As you can see in my attachment, the S + I shortcut succeeds 4 times but then switches to "Edit HEX" in the subsequent attempts. A possible fix could be:

1. Set "I" as the default shortcut for changing stroke color.
2. Ensure the color picker behaves consistently.

The "I" shortcut is useful for switching stroke (pen) with a palette that has more than 5 colors, instead of changing the fill color.

author: ThayalanGR

title: Feature Request: Enhanced Selection Behavior (Ctrl+Click & Directional Box Selection)
body:
Hi team, thank you for the great work on this project!
﻿
I’d like to suggest a couple of improvements to the selection functionality that would significantly enhance usability:
﻿
Ctrl + Click to Add to Selection:
Many modeling and graphics software support holding the Ctrl key and clicking on individual elements to add them to the current selection. It would be great if your tool could support this behavior as well.
﻿
Directional Box Selection:
Another useful feature would be directional box selection. For example:
﻿
Left to right drag (mouse left-click and drag from left to right) selects only elements completely within the box.
﻿
Right to left drag selects all elements that intersect with the box (even partially).
﻿
These enhancements would make the selection experience more intuitive and efficient, especially when working with complex diagrams or large numbers of elements.
﻿
Thanks for considering this!

title: [Bug] Elbow arrow editing bug
body:
# What
Attempting to adjust an elbow arrow to create a single 90 degree turn causes the arrow to glitch and revert to default shape.

I am quite new to Excalidraw, so there might be something about the app or its usage that I don't understand, however this does look like a bug to me.

# Steps to recreate
I am not exactly sure how reliably this can be reproduced so I am including here an Excalidraw file in which the bug appears, GitHub won't let me upload it as a file, so i will just copy the JSON data.

```
{
  "type": "excalidraw",
  "version": 2,
  "source": "http://localhost:3000",
  "elements": [
    {
      "id": "ezh_U_h9NCqWYk63RWhN_",
      "type": "arrow",
      "x": 500,
      "y": 260,
      "width": 540,
      "height": 60,
      "angle": 0,
      "strokeColor": "#1e1e1e",
      "backgroundColor": "transparent",
      "fillStyle": "solid",
      "strokeWidth": 2,
      "strokeStyle": "solid",
      "roughness": 0,
      "opacity": 100,
      "groupIds": [],
      "frameId": null,
      "index": "b1n",
      "roundness": null,
      "seed": 1746165717,
      "version": 1030,
      "versionNonce": 788735764,
      "isDeleted": false,
      "boundElements": [],
      "updated": 1745513867779,
      "link": null,
      "locked": false,
      "points": [
        [
          0,
          0
        ],
        [
          520,
          0
        ],
        [
          520,
          20
        ],
        [
          540,
          20
        ],
        [
          540,
          60
        ]
      ],
      "lastCommittedPoint": null,
      "startBinding": null,
      "endBinding": null,
      "startArrowhead": null,
      "endArrowhead": "triangle",
      "elbowed": true,
      "fixedSegments": [
        {
          "index": 2,
          "start": [
            520,
            0
          ],
          "end": [
            520,
            20
          ]
        },
        {
          "index": 3,
          "start": [
            520,
            20
          ],
          "end": [
            540,
            20
          ]
        }
      ],
      "startIsSpecial": false,
      "endIsSpecial": false
    }
  ],
  "appState": {
    "gridSize": 20,
    "gridStep": 5,
    "gridModeEnabled": true,
    "viewBackgroundColor": "#ffffff"
  },
  "files": {}
}
```

Note that this was originally a larger drawing, from which I removed elements until only the buggy arrow was left. I checked that its behavior was still consistent and still broken. If you need the original file I can make that available as well.

If you open the file, you will find the offending arrow as shown below:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3076fd2-6adc-42ce-a51d-d00b05959ded)

After grabbing one of the white control points next to the cursor and dragging it up while holding the mouse button down, you will see the intended final shape of the arrow:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65efa826-bd64-4a01-a720-cd067dd4860b)

However after the mouse button is released, the arrow snaps into an entirely different, default, shape as shown below:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd95f9a9-1454-4851-be46-2e20e807de4b)

I tested this using a local instance of Excalidraw, both at 817d8c553c3389650f8b4503984a6d4a5d2f0c11 (v0.18.0) as well as a18b139a60a50f1db73444140443254961655a55 (master).

I tested in both Firefox and Chromium, just in case, as well as with object snapping and background grid turned on and off.

In all cases behaviour persists.

title: Embed Emoji fallback font for consistent emojis across platforms

- so that we have consistent emojis across browsers and windows / mac / linux
- so that we have consistent emojis during server-side render (SVG / PNG exports in E+, PDF black & white Noto Emoji)

title: Inconsistent viewport size behavior across monitors with different resolutions
body:
When opening the same Excalidraw scene on monitors with different screen resolutions or DPI settings, the visible canvas viewport size varies. This leads to inconsistent user experiences: on one screen, more of the drawing is visible; on the other, it appears zoomed in or cropped. The actual drawing content is unchanged, but users see different portions of the canvas depending on the display characteristics.

**Steps to Reproduce**:
Open the same Excalidraw file in two different monitors:

Monitor A: e.g., 1920x1080 

Monitor B: e.g., 2560x1440 

Compare the visible portion of the drawing canvas on each screen without manually adjusting zoom.
Observe the difference in how much of the canvas is visible by default.

I don't know whether this is the issue or expected behavior.
If this is expected behavior, In collaborative environments or when switching devices, how does users losing the content know this is happening ?
If multiple people are drawing,  users may unknowingly miss content drawn by others or believe elements have been lost, simply because they are outside the visible canvas area. There can be accidental deletion or overwrite of off-screen content.

Explaining screenshots:

I logged into a session with 2 users.
User 1 drew a box bordering his visible canvas.
User 2 can see ample space outside the box.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61396fbd-4a5f-45bd-8f9b-76be95cd763e)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/74641e2d-6a13-425f-9f3e-bec59792b6eb)

author: ryan-di

title: [Feature Request] Support Multiple Slide Decks per Canvas
body:
#### Is your feature request related to a problem?

I am a heavy Excalidraw+ user and maintain large canvases that often contain multiple slide decks for different stakeholders, each focusing on a distinct scope of a feature or implementation. Currently, Excalidraw only supports one slide deck per canvas, which makes it challenging to manage different presentations for various audiences without duplicating or fragmenting canvases.  

My current workaround is to split the canvases, allowing me to build multiple presentations. However, this approach complicates the organization and makes managing content significantly harder, as related information becomes scattered across multiple canvases.  

#### Describe the solution i'd like

I would like to suggest the ability to manage multiple slide decks within a single canvas. This could involve creating and naming multiple slide decks, allowing users to structure content based on audience or context and switch between them in presentation mode.  


#### Describe alternatives i've considered

Currently, the only workaround is to split the canvases, but this quickly becomes unwieldy and harder to manage as the number of decks grows.  

---

Implementing multiple slide decks would significantly enhance the usability of large canvases with diverse content, enabling more structured and audience-specific presentations without duplicating content.  

Thank you for considering this feature! Let me know if further clarification or examples would be helpful.
author: iamfj

title: Improve Feedback: Display Error Message for Invalid Hexadecimal Color Codes
body:
## Description
Currently, Excalidraw accepts some invalid or malformed hexadecimal color values in the color input field without providing any visual or textual feedback to the user. This may cause confusion as users may not realize why the color hasn’t changed.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Excalidraw.
2. Select the pencil tool (or any tool that allows color selection).
3. Open the custom color input field (hex code).
4. Enter the following invalid values one at a time:
- 123456789 → more than 8 characters
- 1 → only 1 digit
- 12 → only 2 digits
- 12345 → 5 digits
- 1234567 → 7 digits
- zzzzzz → contains invalid hexadecimal characters
- blue → non-hex text input

## Expected Behavior
A clear and visible error message should appear when an invalid hexadecimal value is entered, such as:

- Error: Hex code must be 3, 4, 6, or 8 characters (excluding #)
- Error: Invalid characters in hex code
- Error: Not a valid hex color

## Actual Behavior
The input is ignored silently and the pencil color remains unchanged. No error or feedback is shown to the user.

## Why This Matters
- Improves usability by helping users understand why their input didn’t work.
- Prevents frustration or confusion when using custom colors.
- Follows good UX practices for input validation and error feedback.

## Suggested Fix
Implement front-end validation in the color input field and display an error tooltip or inline message when an invalid value is detected.

title: Improve Feedback:  Shortcut Ctrl + Shift + [  and Ctrl + [ brings element to front instead of sending to back
body:
## Description

Currently, in Excalidraw, under the Help tab where available shortcuts are listed, the shortcuts `Ctrl + Shift + [` and `Ctrl + [` are described as sending the selected element **to the back**. However, instead of moving the element to the back, they actually bring the element **to the front**.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Excalidraw.  
2. Create several overlapping geometric shapes.  
3. Select the shape that is in the middle.  
4. Press `Ctrl + Shift + [` or `Ctrl + [`.

## Expected Behavior

The selected shape should be **sent to the back**.

## Actual Behavior

The selected shape is **brought to the front**.
author: Leticia-Arisa-K-Higa

title: Improve Feedback: Ctrl + Shift + ]  and Ctrl + ] shortcut not working
