## Description

Currently, in Excalidraw, under the Help tab where the available shortcuts are listed, the shortcuts `Ctrl + Shift + ]` and `Ctrl + ]` are described as sending the selected element **to the front**. However, when either shortcut is pressed, **nothing happens**.


## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Excalidraw.  
2. Create several overlapping geometric shapes.  
3. Select the shape that is in the middle.  
4. Press `Ctrl + Shift + ]` or `Ctrl + ]`.

## Expected Behavior

The selected shape should be **brought to the front**.

## Actual Behavior

Nothing happens when the shortcut is pressed.
author: Leticia-Arisa-K-Higa

title: Improve Feedback: Locked element cannot be selected again to unlock after deselection
body:
## Description

Currently in Excalidraw, after locking an element using the shortcut `Ctrl + Shift + L` and then interacting with another element or creating a new one (which deselects the locked element), it is not possible to unlock the element.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Excalidraw.  
2. Create any geometric shape.  
3. Select the shape.  
4. Press `Ctrl + Shift + L` to lock it.  
5. Create a new geometric shape or select another shape.  
6. Try to select the locked shape again to unlock it.

## Expected Behavior

It should be possible to select the locked shape again in order to unlock it.

## Actual Behavior

It is not possible to select the locked shape to unlock it.

title: Embed Scene option not working correctly when re-importing exported file
body:
Description:
The "Embed scene" option during image export does not appear to function as expected. When exporting a drawing with "Embed scene" enabled and then re-importing the resulting PNG or SVG file back into Excalidraw, the file behaves like a normal image instead of restoring the full editable scene.

Expected Behavior:
When the "Embed scene" option is selected during export, the file should include the scene data. Upon re-importing the image into Excalidraw, it should restore the original editable elements (shapes, text, positions, etc.).

Actual Behavior:
The exported file (PNG/SVG) does not restore the scene. Instead, it is treated as a static image and cannot be edited as an Excalidraw drawing.

Steps to Reproduce:

Create a drawing in Excalidraw.

Go to Export > Image.

Enable "Embed scene".

Export as PNG or SVG.

Import the exported file back into Excalidraw.

Observe that the drawing cannot be edited — only the image is shown.

title: Embed Scene option not working correctly when re-importing exported file
body:
##Description:
The "Embed scene" option during image export does not appear to function as expected. When exporting a drawing with "Embed scene" enabled and then re-importing the resulting PNG or SVG file back into Excalidraw, the file behaves like a normal image instead of restoring the full editable scene.

##Expected Behavior:
When the "Embed scene" option is selected during export, the file should include the scene data. Upon re-importing the image into Excalidraw, it should restore the original editable elements (shapes, text, positions, etc.).

##Actual Behavior:
The exported file (PNG/SVG) does not restore the scene. Instead, it is treated as a static image and cannot be edited as an Excalidraw drawing.

#Steps to Reproduce:

- Create a drawing in Excalidraw.
- Go to Export > Image.
- Enable "Embed scene".
- Export as PNG or SVG.
- Import the exported file back into Excalidraw.
- Observe that the drawing cannot be edited — only the image is shown.
author: JoaoPC10

title: Improve feedback: notify user when no shape tool is active
body:
#**Description:**

When a shape tool (e.g., rectangle, ellipse, diamond, arrow) is selected and then immediately deactivated by pressing Esc, the UI still gives the impression that the tool is active. However, attempting to draw on the canvas results in no action, and the user receives no feedback. This lack of feedback may confuse users who believe the shape tool is still active and functional.

#**Expected Behavior:**

If the shape tool has been deactivated (e.g., via Esc), either:
The UI should clearly indicate that no tool is selected, or, when attempting to draw without a valid tool, a message or visual cue (like a tooltip or toast notification) should inform the user that no tool is currently active.

#**Actual Behavior:**

The user can click and drag on the canvas, but nothing happens.
No feedback is given to indicate that the tool was deactivated, leading to confusion.

#**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Open https://excalidraw.com/.
2. Select any shape tool (e.g., rectangle, ellipse, diamond, arrow).
3. Immediately press the Esc key (this deactivates the tool).
4. Try to draw on the canvas by clicking and dragging.
5. Observe that no shape is created and no message is shown.
6. Repeat the test with other shape tools.

#**Suggested Improvement:**

Add user feedback when a draw action is attempted with no active tool — e.g., a tooltip or temporary message like:
"Please select a shape tool to draw."

author: dwelle

title: Missing CSS import in documentation for NextJs Integration
body:
In a Next.js project, Excalidraw does not render correctly out of the box unless its CSS is explicitly imported. This is due to the fact that, unlike some frameworks, Next.js does not automatically include styles from third-party packages unless they are manually imported.

To ensure Excalidraw renders properly, you must add the following import statement in your application (wherever you are dynamically* importing it):

```ts
import '@excalidraw/excalidraw/index.css';
```

Without this import, the Excalidraw canvas will not display or function correctly, as it relies on specific styles for layout and interaction.

Action: Please update the documentation to explicitly mention this requirement for users integrating Excalidraw with Next.js or similar frameworks that do not auto-load package-level CSS.

author: zsviczian

title: Refactor: Improve browser detection reliability in ShareDialog
body:
The ShareDialog component's getShareIcon() function (lines 35-47) currently uses User Agent string parsing to determine which share icon to display based on the user's operating system. While this provides a native-feeling experience by matching the platform's visual language, the current implementation using User Agent detection has reliability concerns.

Current Implementation
The function currently relies on parsing the User Agent string to detect the operating system and display the appropriate share icon for the platform.

Problem
User Agent string parsing is inherently unreliable.

Proposed Solution
Replace the current User Agent string parsing with a more robust browser detection library.

title: Dark Mode: Visual flicker and momentary switch to Light Mode when dragging an object into a Frame Tool
body:
**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Go to [https://excalidraw.com/](https://www.google.com/url?sa=E&q=https%3A%2F%2Fexcalidraw.com%2F).
2. Enable Dark Mode for the interface.
3. Add a "Frame" tool to the canvas.
4. Create any object (e.g., a diamond, a rectangle).
5. Drag the created object and attempt to drop it or move it over the previously added Frame.

**Actual Behavior:**
When dragging the object over the Frame, the screen experiences a very quick flicker. During this flicker, it appears as if the interface momentarily attempts to switch to Light Mode before immediately reverting to Dark Mode. This happens within milliseconds, but is noticeable.

**Expected Behavior:**
The interface should remain consistently in Dark Mode without any flickering or theme-switching attempts when dragging and dropping objects into a Frame Tool. The interaction should be smooth and visually stable.

title: Feature Request: Layer/Element Visibility Toggle Within Frames
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/9306
Originally posted by **rapozoantonio** March 26, 2025 I'd like to propose adding a display/hide functionality for elements within the same frame. This would enable users to create interactive diagrams with toggleable layers of information, all contained within a unified frame structure. ## Use Case As someone who creates technical diagrams and presentations within Excalidraw, I often need to show different states or layers of information in the same spatial context. Currently, this requires: - Creating duplicate frames - Copying elements between frames - Ensuring perfect alignment A visibility toggle would simplify this workflow tremendously, allowing: - Single frame maintenance - Toggle between different element combinations - Creation of interactive diagrams for presentations ## Implementation Suggestion 1. Add a "visibility" property to elements 2. Create a sidebar panel for managing visibility groups/layers 3. Implement keyboard shortcuts for quick toggling 4. Allow exporting with visibility states as separate images/slides ## Benefits - **Better Presentations**: Create step-by-step reveals without file complexity - **Enhanced Interactivity**: Enable richer diagrams for educational/presentation purposes ## Questions for the Community - Would this feature be valuable to your workflows? - Any additional use cases I'm not considering? - Preferences on UI implementation for managing visibility? - How would this interact with existing features like embed/link frames? I'm happy to elaborate further or contribute to discussions on implementation approach if there's interest!

title: Bug: Drawings not rendered on iPhone Safari/Chrome (touch coordinates off)
body:
Description
When using Excalidraw in Safari on iOS, strokes drawn with a finger do not appear on the canvas. Network inspection shows that the x and y values sent in the canvas-sync payload do not align with the visible touch position:

"x": 735,
"y": 750


The same document renders correctly on desktop versions of Chrome (Windows) and Chrome (macOS).

**Steps to Reproduce**
Open Excalidraw in Safari on an iPhone (iOS 17.5.1).

Create or join any board.

Select the Pencil tool and start drawing.

Observe that no stroke appears on-screen, even though network traffic shows a canvas-sync event.

Expected Behaviour
The stroke should appear at the exact location of the touch input, just as it does on desktop browsers.

Actual Behaviour
The drawing is displayed outside of the shown area of the canvas. The captured payload indicates the pointer coordinates are offset from where the user touched.

title: Feature request: Built-in Calculator for Text Tool
body:
## Feature Request: Built-in Calculator for Text Tool

**Summary:**  
Please add a built-in calculator to Excalidraw’s text tool. When typing a mathematical expression (like `1+2`) and entering an `=`, Excalidraw should automatically display the answer (e.g., `1+2=3`) directly in the text box.

**Key Features:**
- **Inline Calculation:**  
  Typing an expression such as `1+2=` should automatically evaluate and display `3` after the equals sign.
- **Unit Support:**  
  The calculator should recognize and compute expressions with units, e.g., `5m + 200cm = 7m`. It should handle both metric and imperial units.
- **Conversion Options Popup:**  
  When a unit is used (e.g., `5m=`), a popup should offer alternative representations, such as:
    - `5m = 500cm`
    - `5m = 50dm`
    - `5m ≈ 16.4ft`
  The user can select which format to insert.
- **Settings Option:**  
  Add a setting to choose the preferred measurement system (metric, imperial, or auto-detect), which controls default output for conversions.

**Example Workflow:**
1. User types `12ft + 3m =` in a text box.
2. Excalidraw evaluates and displays `12ft + 3m = 21.84ft` (or the equivalent in meters, depending on settings).
3. Popup offers alternatives like `21.84ft = 6.66m`.
4. User selects the preferred format to insert.

**Benefits:**
- Makes quick calculations and unit conversions seamless while drawing.
- Especially useful for technical, educational, and engineering diagrams.
- Reduces the need to switch between Excalidraw and external calculators.

Thank you for considering this feature!

title: Security issue: Use of postMessage("*") with wildcard origin
body:
While reviewing the codebase with SonarQube, we noticed that window.postMessage is used with the wildcard origin "*" in several places – specifically in communication with embedded YouTube and Vimeo iframes. 

---

### Problem:

Using "*" as the targetOrigin is insecure because it allows the message to be received by any origin. This introduces potential security risks, even if the current messages are not sensitive:
	•	Cross-origin attacks via malicious iframes
	•	Unintentional data exposure to third-party origins
	•	Vulnerabilities introduced by browser extensions or injected scripts
	•	Increased attack surface for future features or changes

### Affected areas
•	Vimeo iframe messaging (pause, play)
•	YouTube player commands (playVideo, pauseVideo)
•	Initial communication with YouTube (event: "listening")

(source: packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx)

---

_Current implementation (example):_

`iframe.contentWindow.postMessage(message, "*");`

_Recommended fix:_

Replacing "*" with the iframe’s actual origin, extracted from its src:

`const origin = new URL(iframe.src).origin;`
`iframe.contentWindow.postMessage(message, origin);`

---

**We would be happy to contribute a pull request to address this if the change is welcome.
Please let us know if you’d prefer the safer logic to be implemented inline or via a utility function.**

author: AmesyLi

title: Command palette - show the "recently used" command based on the selection context
body:
Enhance the command palette to store at least 5 last-used commands and display the most recent one based on the current selection.

### Goals
- Store the last 5 used commands (instead of just the most recent one).
- When the command palette is opened, show the most recent command that is applicable to the currently selected element(s).
- If none of the recent commands are applicable, fall back to the current behavior (show generic "recently used" section).

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command usage history stores up to 5 recent commands.
- [ ] The history list is filtered to show only commands that are valid for the currently selected element(s).
- [ ] When opening the command palette:
  - If one or more recent commands apply to the current selection, show the most recent applicable one at the top.
  - If none apply, display the default "recently used" section.
- [ ] Recent commands are updated in order of use (most recent first).
- [ ] Selection-aware filtering works for:
  - Single element selection
  - Multiple element selection (intersection of available commands)
  - No selection (default behavior)

### Edge cases

- No selection: Show the most recent command as usual.
- Multiple elements: Only include commands available for *all* selected elements.
- Unavailable commands in history: Skip them, fall back gracefully.
- Empty command history: Fall back to current behavior with no "recents".

title: Azure DevOps breaks HTML link
body: When entering an excalidraw link into azure devops, the URL will be rendered as: ```html https://excalidraw.com/#room=9573b44b5d6cb0f65c8c,_5ExrR7hfqAKtW_1J4xe1A ``` When you look closely, the `,` is replaced with `%2c`. This leads to the following issue: When clicking the link, excalidraw will not recognize the URL and the link seems to be broken. A workaround is to copy the link manually and paste it into the browser. Although this is obviously also a bug on Azure DevOps side, they are notoriously slow to fix things. Therefore, maybe excalidraw can change there router configuration to allow for URL-safe encoded URLs? That would be awesome! **Edit**: Weird, I just noticed that clicking the same `https://excalidraw.com/#room=9573b44b5d6cb0f65c8c,_5ExrR7hfqAKtW_1J4xe1A In Azure DevOps it's still an issue.

title: [Feature Request]: Ghost Preview of Shape Before Placement (like Eraser.io)
body:
When a user selects a shape from the toolbar (e.g., rectangle, ellipse), a translucent “ghost” version of that shape could appear on the canvas following the cursor before placement. This preview would allow the user to place the shape with just a single click, instead of clicking and dragging.

### ✨ Why this would be helpful

* **Single-click placement**: Much faster to use, especially for diagramming workflows.
* **Improves usability**: Users get visual confirmation before placing an element.
* **Reduces friction**: No need to click-drag-release for standard shapes.
* **Precision**: Easier to align or preview before committing.

### 📌 Expected Behavior

1. User selects a shape (e.g., rectangle).
2. A ghost version of that shape (in default size and style) follows the cursor.
3. The user clicks once to place it on the canvas and it becomes a real element.
4. Optional: drag to manually size the shape instead of using the default.

### 🧩 Optional Enhancements

* Add ghost opacity/outline styling to make it clearly distinguishable.
* Allow customizing default ghost shape size via settings or previous shape dimensions.
* Snap to grid even during ghost phase.

### 💬 Reference

This feature works really well in [Eraser](https://eraser.io/) and improves flow, especially when creating diagrams or wireframes.

body:

Issue: The gradient function uses a fixed delta regardless of the function's scale, which can cause numerical instability for very large or very small coordinate values.
Fix: Use adaptive delta or scale-aware epsilon

title: Sticky notes feature is not discoverable enough
body:
## Problem

"Sticky notes" function is not discoverable. 
While it's currently possible to create a sticky note by pressing enter when any shape is selected, this user flow is not immediately discoverable and one has to read the documentation to figure out how to do it.

## One possible solution 

Add a sticky notes button in the toolbar that creates a sticky note object upon click. The difference between a sticky note and any other shape would be that a text field would be focused in it upon click, without the need of pressing enter. It could also be styled slightly differently compared to other shape

----

Ref for previous discussions on this topic https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/1428
author: marcoambrosini

title: Feature Request: Rasterized Pencil Tool for Improved Tablet Performance and Handwriting Experience
body:
**Description:**  
Hello Excalidraw team,

First of all, thank you for your amazing work on Excalidraw. I use it extensively within Obsidian, and I truly appreciate its simplicity and flexibility.

I would like to propose a feature that could greatly enhance the experience for users who take handwritten notes on tablets.

**Context and Motivation:**  
While Excalidraw was originally designed for use with mouse and keyboard on desktop computers, its intuitive interface has attracted many users who also want to use it for handwritten notes on tablets—similar to apps like GoodNotes, Samsung Notes, or OneNote.

However, there are some performance challenges when using the current pencil tool on tablets, especially on devices without a powerful GPU. Specifically:
- The current pencil tool creates editable vector lines, which can cause significant GPU usage and battery drain.
- As the number of pencil strokes increases, the canvas becomes heavier, leading to noticeable lag and a less responsive drawing experience.
- In contrast, the laser pointer tool feels much more responsive, and raster-based drawing apps (like Clip Studio Paint) handle large numbers of strokes smoothly.

**Feature Proposal:**  
To address these issues, I suggest introducing a new **"Rasterized Pencil Tool"**. This tool would:
- Draw directly onto a raster (bitmap) layer, rather than creating editable vector lines.
- Store all raster pencil strokes in a single "raster layer," separate from the existing vector layer.
- Greatly reduce GPU load and improve performance, especially for users with less powerful hardware.
- Make handwriting and sketching on tablets much smoother and more battery-friendly.

Additionally, to complement the rasterized pencil, I propose:
- A **"Raster Area Eraser"** tool, which would erase parts of the raster layer (similar to the eraser in MS Paint).
- A **"Raster Lasso Selection"** tool, allowing users to select and move rasterized strokes.

**Benefits:**  
- Significantly improved performance and responsiveness for tablet users.
- Lower battery consumption during handwriting sessions.
- A more comfortable and fluid note-taking experience, making Excalidraw a true alternative to dedicated handwriting apps.

I believe these additions would make Excalidraw even more versatile and accessible, especially for the growing number of users who rely on tablets for their workflow.

Thank you for considering this suggestion! I hope it aligns with your vision for Excalidraw and helps make it even better for the community.
author: HoodyEz

title: [BUG] 404 Errors for Assistant font files due to incorrect path in CSS
body:
[AI Generated Response, as I am not a developer, but had this issue]
Hi, 

I'm deploying Excalidraw using the official Docker image behind a reverse proxy. While most of the application works fine, I noticed 404 errors in the logs related to missing font files: 
 
 
```
GET /assets/Assistant-Bold.woff2 HTTP/1.1" 404  
GET /assets/Assistant-Medium.woff2 HTTP/1.1" 404  
GET /assets/Assistant-Regular.woff2 HTTP/1.1" 404  
```
 
 

It seems the CSS (referenced by `/assets/index-*.css`) is pointing to the fonts under the `/assets/` path, but the actual font files (`Assistant-*.woff2`) exist in the root of the `/public/` folder instead of `/public/assets/`. 

As a result, during runtime, those fonts are not found, and Nginx returns 404s. 

Expected behavior:
CSS should either reference the correct path where the fonts actually exist (e.g.,` /Assistant-Bold.woff2`) or the font files should be placed under /`public/assets/` to match the current URLs. 

Steps to reproduce: 

     Deploy Excalidraw using the official Docker image or `docker-compose.`
     Serve via Nginx (e.g., behind a reverse proxy).
     Load the app and inspect network tab or logs — 404s will appear for the above font files.
     

Potential fixes: 

     Move the Assistant font files into `/public/assets/` so that the CSS reference works as-is.
     Or update the font URL paths in the CSS to match their actual location (`/Assistant-*.woff2`).

title: Touch Device Support (Meta/collection issue)
body:
The issues collects issues and demonstrates that while Excalidraw functions on touch devices, it's currently primarily optimized for desktop use and lacks many features that tablet users expect from modern drawing applications. 

_For anyone reading this. If any of the issues resonate with you, there is always the option to give the issue an upvote by placing a 👍 on the issue's description. This can potentially create a better understanding of the severity of specific issues._

##  Major Feature Gaps

  | Gap                      | Description                                                                |
  |--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
  | No Native Multitouch     | Lacks support for collaborative multi-finger drawing                       |
  | Limited Stylus Features  | Missing standard stylus button functionality and consistent palm rejection |
  | No Smart Tool Switching  | Doesn't automatically differentiate between pen and finger input           |
  | Performance Optimization | Needs optimization for heavy tablet use with many strokes                  |
  | Missing Tablet UX        | Lacks standard tablet features like single-finger pan in drawing mode      |
  | No Gesture Framework     | Missing common tablet gestures (undo/redo, tool switching)                 |
  | Platform Inconsistencies | Different behavior across iOS, Android, and Windows tablets                |

##  Multitouch & Touch Input Issues

  | Issue                            | Description          | GitHub #     |
  |----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------|
  | Multitouch Drawing               | Users requesting support for multiple simultaneous touches for collaborative drawing on interactive whiteboards | #9595        |
  | Touch Coordinate Offset          | On iOS Safari/Chrome, drawings appear offset from actual touch position          | #9603        |
  | Palm Rejection                   | Palm rejection doesn't work properly on certain devices/browsers, causing unwanted lines          | #6088, #9549 |
  | Delayed Palm Rejection           | Palm rejection only activates after drawing starts          | #4808        |
  | Two-touch Lines                  | Lines drawn between palm and pen when both touch          | #9549        |
  | Hand Placement Interference      | Hand on screen interferes with pen drawing          | #7597        |
  | Two-finger Zoom Creates Drawings | Zoom gesture creates unwanted drawings          | #5176        |
  | Touch Handling                   | General touch handling improvements needed          | #966         |

##  Performance Issues

  | Issue                  | Description                                                               | GitHub #     |
  |------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------|
  | Lag with Many Elements | Significant performance degradation with 1000+ strokes on tablets         | #5788, #7237 |
  | GPU Stress             | Vector-based rendering causes high GPU usage and battery drain on tablets | #9697        |
  | Slow Touch Input       | Touch input can be slow on some devices, possibly due to low VRAM         | #3693        |
  | Ink Performance        | Freedraw smoothing performance problems                                   | #5192        |
  | iPad Freezing          | Program freeze when loading 1000+ strokes                                 | #5788        |

##  iPad-Specific Problems

  | Issue                     | Description                                                                    | GitHub # |
  |---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|
  | Dark Mode Export          | Can't export images in dark mode on iPad                                       | #9129    |
  | Pen Interruptions         | Pen randomly stops working while writing                                       | #9508    |
  | Missing Copy/Paste        | Copy/paste options missing from context menu                                   | #3949    |
  | File Upload Issues        | Can't upload images or select libraries on iOS 18                              | #8960    |
  | Scroll Animation Blocking | Menu scroll animation blocks button clicks                                     | #9204    |
  | Continuous Line Issues    | Last segment of continuous line not added with stylus                          | #9032    |
  | PWA Missing Features      | Can't use two-finger tap to undo or zoom with fingers while using Apple Pencil | #7979    |
  | Trackpad Issues           | iPad trackpad input recognition problems                                       | #4582    |
  | Copy Only in View Mode    | Shortcut menu "Copy" only available in View mode on iOS                        | #5504    |

##  Stylus/Pen Support

  | Issue                       | Description                                                     | GitHub #     |
  |-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|--------------|
  | No Eraser Button            | No support for stylus eraser button functionality (58+ upvotes) | #5166        |
  | Surface Pen Eraser          | Surface Pen eraser end not supported                            | #5281        |
  | S-Pen Eraser                | Samsung S-pen button eraser support                             | #6144        |
  | Wacom Pen Eraser            | Wacom pen eraser end support request                            | #6335        |
  | Cursor Flickering           | Cursor appears/disappears rapidly while writing                 | #8981        |
  | Overly Smooth Strokes       | Pen strokes become too smooth when zoomed in                    | #8622        |
  | No Pen vs Finger Separation | No automatic tool switching between pen and finger input        | #7764, #8785 |
  | Wacom Issues                | Various bugs with Wacom tablets including stuck zoom/pan        | #8476, #3794 |
  | Apple Pencil Double Tap     | Support for 2nd gen pencil double tap to switch tools           | #7961, #7996 |
  | Pen Glitches                | Drawing glitches and point changes while drawing                | #6254        |
  | Auto Pen Mode               | Option to disable automatic pen mode activation                 | #8742        |
  | Cursor Visibility           | Cross pointer concealing issues on Surface                      | #7596        |
  | Left-handed Drawing         | Streaking lines for left-handed Surface users                   | #7689        |

##  Mobile-Specific UI/UX Issues

  | Issue                 | Description                                           | GitHub #            |
  |-----------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|
  | Links Not Working     | Links on objects don't open on mobile devices         | #9637               |
  | Mobile Shortcuts      | Request for mobile users to access keyboard shortcuts | #7392               |
  | Zen Mode Access       | Need GUI button for zen mode on mobile (no keyboard)  | #5185               |
  | Shape Menu Wrapping   | UX improvement to reduce vertical space on mobile     | #3641               |
  | Context Menu Problems | Multiple issues with context menu on mobile           | #2456, #5567, #1908 |
  | Mobile Safari Paste   | Paste option not visible in context menu              | #2679               |
  | Color Change Bug      | Changing color exits text editing mode on mobile      | #6202               |
  | Rotate Constraints    | Discrete rotation steps without SHIFT key             | #1351               |
  | Gap Between Words     | Mobile view spacing issue                             | #9056               |
  | Color Rendering       | Different colors between mobile and desktop           | #7221               |

##  Gesture Support

  | Issue                  | Description                                         | GitHub #     |
  |------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|--------------|
  | Undo/Redo Gestures     | Two-finger double tap undo, three-finger redo       | #5714, #5913 |
  | macOS Swipe Conflict   | Swipe-to-go-back gesture conflicts with canvas      | #5736        |

  | Radial Menu            | Pie menu suggestion for touch interfaces            | #5651        |
  | Browser Back Triggered | Two-finger touchpad navigation conflicts            | #9388        |
  | Double-click Issues    | Double-click gesture problems with shape tools      | #740         |

##  Pen/Finger Separation Requests

  | Issue                | Description                                        | GitHub #            |
  |----------------------|----------------------------------------------------|---------------------|
  | One-finger Pan       | Request for single-finger panning in pen mode      | #8785, #7832, #6259 |

  | Pan While Drawing    | Allow panning with finger while using Apple Pencil | #6259               |

##  Android/Tablet Specific

  | Issue             | Description                                 | GitHub # |

  | Drawing Offset    | Position offset when not at top of page     | #4917    |
  | Element Unlock    | Can't unlock elements on Android            | #5569    |

  | SiYian Navigation | Android tablet navigation/exit issues       | #6645    |

##  UI/UX Feature Requests

  | Issue                    | Description                                             | GitHub # |
  |--------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|----------|
  | Rasterized Pencil Tool   | Bitmap-based drawing tool for better tablet performance | #9697    |
  | Thinner Lines            | Current minimum line thickness too thick (65+ upvotes)  | #3693    |
  | Lock Position            | Prevent accidental element movement while editing       | #8864    |
  | Better Handwriting       | Improved smoothing algorithm for natural handwriting    | #4802    |
  | Hide Pen Menu            | Option to hide pen customization menu                   | #6257    |
  | Native Mobile App        | Request for Play/App Store versions                     | #6826    |
  | Infinite Zoom            | With adaptive pen size                                  | #3956    |
  | Edit Freehand Points     | Restore ability to edit drawing points                  | #3916    |

author: lucasahartmann

title: Excessive document size on Excalidraw Share
body:
When using excalidraw share, the size of the firestore seems to be limited to 1mb. Related: #8552
> Yes, the database we're using on excalidraw.com is a 1MB limit. Also the collaboration here isn't meant for long-term/large diagrams.

It is easy to hit this limit even on seemingly small documents after just working on something for an hour or two (like studying for exams together in our case).  
I understand that the size has to be limited for persistent storage, but the speed at which the limit is reached seems unreasonably fast.

Having investigated this a bit, it seems like
- **Deleted elements are still committed to the store** with `"isDeleted": true`
  - This means pasting something and then deleting it again will permanently take up space in the committed size of the document
  - This notably does not happen when performing undo instead of delete, so I can't imagine that keeping the deleted elements is needed for syncronization?
  - This does not seem to happen when saving the `.excalidraw` file locally, deleting parts shrinks the size and the file contains no `"isDeleted": true`-entries
- The committed ciphertext data is effectively `base64(encrypt(JSON.encode(elements)))`
  - **The document data is not compressed before encrypting it**
    - Compression should be very effective here as the stringified JSON has a lot of repetition from: the normal JSON delimiters like `],[`; many points of any given freedraw element sharing the (excessively long) decimal parts; and just generally numbers being encoded as ASCII strings.
    - As a test, I tried running the pre-encryption data through Gzip DHC. This changed the size from `2132113bytes` (2.1mb) to `350810bytes` (350kb), 16% of the original size
  - The commited ciphertext is base64, which adds another 33% to the stored size (base64 stores 8 bits per 6 bits of data). Surely the backend can support storing non-ascii data?
  - All this can be seen when setting a breakpoint at [`firebase.ts:179`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/4eadb891f87ffb8d2b2761b7fff843389cec8100/excalidraw-app/data/firebase.ts#L179): `const { ciphertext, iv } = await encryptElements(roomKey, elements);`.
`JSON.stringify(elements).length * 8/6` will be nearly equal the HTTP request body size.

Is any of this a technical limitation by the sync system or firestore?  
Otherwise a 80%+ reduction in committed document size should be easily possible, plus preventing documents from balooning in size when someone (accidentally) copies and pastes large parts of the document, just to delete them again.  

*A more involved change like switching from JSON to protobuf to avoid the JSON/numbers-as-strings overhead and possibly reducing the accuracy of the freedraw point coordinates to something more sensible could likely save even more space, but shouldn't be necessary and would be more work.*

title: ESM build includes require() due to internal dependencies — breaks browser use with <script type="module">
body:
Hi

I'm trying to build Excalidraw as a pure ESM bundle that can be used directly in the browser with <script type="module">, externalizing react, react-dom, and react/jsx-runtime. 

The generated file still contains:

```
var require = ...;
require("react");
or
typeof require < "u" shims from esbuild.

```
This breaks when loaded in a browser:

> _Uncaught Error: Dynamic require of "react" is not supported_

Interestingly, when importing Excalidraw directly from [esm.sh](https://esm.sh/),
for example:
`import { Excalidraw } from "https://esm.sh/@excalidraw/excalidraw@latest?bundle";
`...it serves a file like:

> https://esm.sh/v135/@excalidraw/excalidraw/es2022/excalidraw.mjs

This file also includes fallback require() code like:

`var mr = (e => typeof require < "u" ? require : typeof Proxy < "u" ? new Proxy(e, ...
`
However, this version still works in the browser because esm.sh wraps it in a way that prevents the require() code from ever being executed. In contrast, my locally built version immediately executes require("react").

When I look into index.js generated locally, it more of looks plain JavaScript code with "require", "prototype", "__var", however excalidraw.mjs received from esm.sh contains esm only outputs like 

`import{nanoid as wy}from"/nanoid@3.3.3/es2022/nanoid.mjs";`


 I’m not an expert in ESM/CJS internals or advanced bundler setups.  I'm just trying to generate a clean Excalidraw bundle that works in the browser via <script type="module">. 
Please help.

author: Risiamu

title: MCP Server Integration: Real-time Claude + Excalidraw Canvas Sync
body:
## Project Overview

Built an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that bridges LLM provider like Claude with Excalidraw, enabling real-time diagram creation and editing through natural language.

## Key Features

- **Real-time sync**: MCP operations instantly appear on live web canvas
- **Bidirectional updates**: Changes in browser sync back to MCP client
- **WebSocket integration**: Live collaboration between AI and manual edits
- **Complete REST API**: External integrations beyond MCP
- **Batch operations**: Create complex diagrams in single calls

## Technical Implementation

```typescript
// Example MCP call creates live diagram
await mcp.call('create_element', {
  type: 'rectangle',
  x: 100, y: 100,
  text: 'AI-Generated Box'
});
```

## Use Cases

- **AI-powered diagramming**: Claude creates flowcharts, wireframes, system diagrams
- **Educational tools**: Interactive learning with AI-generated visuals
- **Documentation**: Auto-generate diagrams from text descriptions
- **Collaborative design**: AI assists in real-time design sessions

## Resources

- **Demo**: [https://youtu.be/RRN7AF7QIew](https://youtu.be/RRN7AF7QIew)
- **GitHub**: [https://github.com/yctimlin/mcp_excalidraw](https://github.com/yctimlin/mcp_excalidraw)
- **MCP Spec**: Built following official MCP standards

## Community Feedback

Would love feedback from the Excalidraw community! Interested in:
- Integration improvements
- Additional use cases
- Performance optimizations
- Potential collaboration opportunities

This project showcases how Excalidraw can be integrated into AI workflows while maintaining its core collaborative experience.

title: Excessive re-renders on pointer move during line editing
body:
There are two things that cause unnecessary re-renders while moving the pointer while in the line editor:
- A new `editingLinearElement` is returned each time [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/678dff25eddfc43319299495556ab9f6029bc719/packages/element/src/linearElementEditor.ts#L1043-L1047) even if nothing's changed. The new reference of `editingLinearElement` is then checked against the one in appState [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/678dff25eddfc43319299495556ab9f6029bc719/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L5860-L5863).
- Always executing `setState` [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/678dff25eddfc43319299495556ab9f6029bc719/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L5878-L5883) even if nothing's changed.

title: Bug: Trying to move another linear element while a line editor is open leads to a weird state
body:
The "weird state" is that the the app is in line editor state, but no line editor points are rendered.

This happens because `renderLinearPointHandles` [expects `appState.selectedLinearElement` to exist](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/678dff25eddfc43319299495556ab9f6029bc719/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L439-L441), but `appState.selectedLinearElement` is wrongly changed to another element on pointer up [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/678dff25eddfc43319299495556ab9f6029bc719/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L9413-L9431), then gets nullified as a result of this inconsistency [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/678dff25eddfc43319299495556ab9f6029bc719/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L2877-L2884).

author: Saidul-1

title: [Bug] Inconsistency in focus management inside Dialog and didn't comply with ARIA standards
body:
Hi,

### Issues:

- When the Dialog is opened, focus is incorrectly set to the second focusable element instead of the first.
- The Dialog does not respect the tabIndex attribute when determining which element should receive focus.
- Upon opening the Dialog (after clearing the canvas), focus is placed on the second element ("Confirm"), which violates ARIA guidelines by directing focus to a destructive action.

ARIA standard document: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/

**When Dialog is opened the focus returns to the second focusable element instead of first element.**

Occurs in 
- Help Dialog
- Share Dialog
- Save to Dialog

### How to reproduce?

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/46ecbaf6-4a21-4442-82a1-4d0a5ed9168c

**Help Dialog**
The Help Dialog, which includes external links and keyboard shortcuts, can be opened by clicking the ? icon located at the bottom-right corner of the screen.
When the dialog opens, focus is incorrectly set to the second focusable element ("Read our blog").
Additionally, when navigating using the Tab key, the focus always starts from the second element rather than the first.

**Share Dialog**
The Share Dialog can be opened by clicking the Share button in the top-right corner of the screen.
Upon opening, the focus is incorrectly directed to the "Export to link" button instead of the "Share session" button.

**Save To Dialog**
To open the Save To Dialog, click the hamburger menu in the top-left corner and then select the Save To option.
When the dialog appears, the focus is incorrectly placed on the "Export to link" button instead of the intended "Save to" button.

> **Press enter after the modal is opened to find the focused button/element.**


.

I have investigated the problem on why it is happening and it comes down to this line of code in Dialog.jsx which makes the second element to be in focus instead of the first element. 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/d6a934ed196874e50dac498c63e9f2af7c90296d/packages/excalidraw/components/Dialog.tsx#L63-L68


### **Reasons?**
This issue may stem from the Confirm Dialog that appears when the Clear Canvas button is clicked. In this dialog, focus is intentionally set to the Confirm button, which follows the Cancel button in the DOM. However, this behavior violates ARIA guidelines by directing focus to a destructive action instead of a neutral or non-destructive option.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b18c907-c618-4b19-81fb-19a143e434a9

After further investigation I also found out that tabIndex property is not taken into consideration for elements inside Dialog.


I have also started working on fixing it. If needed I can raise a PR.

title: Bug: align child frame elements to a non-frame member
body:
# Issue
When selecting both children of a frame and an element outside the frame, if alignment is performed relative to the outside element, the frame's children will visually align outside the frame. However, it seems as if they still retain their frameId, making them unselectable unless the frame is manually extended to include their new positions.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b25e6b0b-aaa5-4e84-9603-678e0beb66fc

## Steps to reproduce:
1. create a frame
2. create a rectangle within the frame
3. create a rectangle to the far right positioned outside the frame
4. select both rectangles and apply the align-right action
5. attempt to select rectangle initially in frame

title: Corrupted element causes Select, Eraser and Text tools to break.
body:
```
System: Mac OS Sequoia 15.6 on an Apple M3
Browser: Chrome (v138)
Excalidraw version: 0.18.0 (latest on npm)
```

### Issue:
While doing a particularly large drawing on a separate project where i'm using excalidraw as the base drawing platform, the board suddenly stopped reacting to the select tool, eraser tool and the text tool. I could still do everything else. The issue seems to be on this [line](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/817d8c553c3389650f8b4503984a6d4a5d2f0c11/packages/utils/collision.ts#L28). It says:

```
Can't read type of undefined

```

I know this relates more to the shape than the type, but the only relevant element i could find which could be causing this issue, was this element in my entire 4.5MB drawing:

```
{
  id: 'w',
  x: 2324.227230124059,
  y: 10334.426844633379,
  width: 141.41024275979544,
  height: 0,

    [ 95.31496707109272, 0 ],
    [ 124.8143204387743, 0 ],
    [ 141.41024275979544, 0 ],
    [ 141.41024275979544, 0 ]
  ],
  pressures: [],
  simulatePressure: true,
  lastCommittedPoint: [ 141.41024275979544, 0 ]
}
```

### Expected Behaviour:
Generally I wouldn't expect an element to exist without a type since the typescript type doesn't allow one. Am I wrong in this assumption?
author: hakotaco

title: Feature request: onZIndexChange callback
body:
maintaining backend state of zorder via the existing onChange is tricky and wasteful because onChange is triggered for all changes. We don't want to be checking to see if the zorder has changed every time a freedraw change event happens.
An onZIndexChange callback would allow the reordered state to be sent efficiently to the backend as it would only be done when the zindex changed.
author: gthomas2

title: Feature Request：Allow Controlled Playback and Pause of Handwriting Process Animation
body:
Dear Excalidraw Developers,

I am a frequent user of Excalidraw, and I truly appreciate your excellent work on the project.

I would like to suggest adding an "animated handwriting replay" feature—meaning Excalidraw can record the exact stroke order of my handwriting or drawings, and during lectures or presentations, I can replay the entire writing process as an animation, with options to play, pause, and fast-forward (not just undo/redo or history snapshots).

This would be extremely helpful for teaching STEM subjects, mathematical derivations, and recording public lectures, making explanations much clearer and more interactive.

I sincerely hope you will consider implementing this feature. Thank you very much for your outstanding work!

Best regards

author: Libpzzz

title: Type Resolution Fails for @excalidraw/excalidraw in (Node v22, Next.js 14, TS 5)
body:
Hello Excalidraw Team,

I'm encountering a persistent TypeScript module resolution issue with `@excalidraw/excalidraw@0.18.0` in a modern environment (Node.js v22.6.0, Next.js 14.2.4, TypeScript 5.x). The TypeScript compiler fails to resolve types like `ExcalidrawElement`, reporting errors such as: `Module '"@excalidraw/excalidraw"' has no exported member 'ExcalidrawElement'. (ts2305)`.

### Environment
- `@excalidraw/excalidraw`: 0.18.0
- `next`: 14.2.4
- `react` / `react-dom`: 18.x
- `typescript`: 5.x
- `node`: v22.6.0
- `npm`: v10.9.0
- OS: macOS

### `tsconfig.json`
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "esnext",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "incremental": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "plugins": [
      { "name": "next" }
    ],
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}

Problem DescriptionI'm building a custom Excalidraw wrapper with Next.js, dynamically importing the Excalidraw component and using types like ExcalidrawElement. However, imports like the following fail:typescript

import type { ExcalidrawElement } from '@excalidraw/excalidraw';

Error: Module '"@excalidraw/excalidraw"' has no exported member 'ExcalidrawElement'. (ts2305)Steps to ReproduceCreate a Next.js 14.2.4 project with TypeScript 5.x.
Install @excalidraw/excalidraw@0.18.0.
Use the above tsconfig.json.
Try importing ExcalidrawElement:typescript

import type { ExcalidrawElement } from '@excalidraw/excalidraw';

Run npx tsc --noEmit or build the project (npm run build).
Observe the type error.

What I've TriedImported from various paths:typescript

import type { ExcalidrawElement } from '@excalidraw/excalidraw';
import type { ExcalidrawElement } from '@excalidraw/excalidraw/types';
import type { ExcalidrawElement } from '@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/types/excalidraw/types';
import type { ExcalidrawElement } from '@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/types/excalidraw/element/types';

Cleared caches and reinstalled dependencies (rm -rf .next node_modules package-lock.json && npm install).
Inspected node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/package.json and confirmed the exports map:json

"exports": {
  "./*": {
    "types": "./dist/types/excalidraw/*.d.ts"
  }
}

Verified that ExcalidrawElement is re-exported in dist/types/excalidraw/types.d.ts from ./element/types.

Additional ContextThe issue may be related to Node.js v22.6.0's module resolution behavior or TypeScript 5.x's stricter checks.
A CodeSandbox example https://codesandbox.io/s/excalidraw-types-9h2dm uses ExcalidrawElementSkeleton successfully, but ExcalidrawElement still fails in my local setup.
Downgrading to @excalidraw/excalidraw@0.17.0 may resolve the issue, suggesting a v0.18.0-specific problem.

QuestionsWhat is the canonical way to import ExcalidrawElement and other types in a Next.js 14 + TypeScript 5 project with Node.js v22.6.0?
Is there a known compatibility issue with Excalidraw v0.18.0 in this environment?
Could the exports map in package.json cause issues with modern Node.js/TypeScript?

ReproductionLocal: Set up a Next.js project with the above environment and try the import.
CodeSandbox: https://codesandbox.io/s/excalidraw-types-9h2dm (modify to include failing import).

Thank you for your help! This issue is blocking our project.

title: Feature Request: Option to make laser drawings persistent (like iPad laser)
body:
### Feature Request: Option to make laser drawings persistent (like iPad laser)

**Description**  
Right now, when using the laser tool, the drawn line disappears automatically after a few seconds.  
It would be useful to have an **option to keep the laser visible until manually cleared or toggled off**.  

This would mimic the behavior of the iPad’s laser pointer, where users can choose between a temporary (disappearing) laser and a persistent one.

---

**Why this is useful**  
- Allows presenters to highlight something for a longer time without it fading too quickly.  
- Gives users control over whether the laser is **temporary** (current behavior) or **persistent** (proposed option).  
- Makes it easier in teaching, demos, and collaborative sessions where emphasis needs to stay visible.  

---

**Suggested approach**  
- Add a toggle/checkbox in the laser tool menu:  
  - *Temporary (default)* → laser disappears after a few seconds.  
  - *Persistent* → laser remains on the canvas until disabled or cleared.  
- Keep current behavior as default for backward compatibility.

---

**Example**  
- Select laser tool → draw line → it stays visible until manually cleared.  
- Toggle back to temporary mode → laser fades after a few seconds (same as now).
author: Ayushjhawar8

title: Feature Request: Animated Contextual Tooltips & Storyboard Preview Strip
body:
### Summary
Add two complementary features:
1. **Animated contextual tooltips** that appear when hovering over grouped or complex elements, providing instant explanations or labels.
2. A **storyboard preview strip** at the bottom of the canvas that shows thumbnails of frames or key steps, allowing quick navigation and overview.

### Motivation
- Enhances user understanding by offering context on-demand without cluttering the diagram.
- Makes storytelling/presentation workflows smoother—users can see a visual “timeline” of frames and jump directly to any step.
- Helps both creators and viewers navigate complex diagrams or multi-frame flows with ease.

### Proposed Design
#### Animated Contextual Tooltips
- When the cursor hovers over a group or selected element cluster for ~300 ms, show a hand-drawn style callout bubble near the cursor with a label/note.
- Bubble could have entrance animation (fade/slide).
- Tooltip content editable via right-click or double-click.

#### Storyboard Preview Strip
- A horizontal strip docked at the bottom of the canvas (toggleable).
- Displays mini-thumbnails of frames or manual “key steps.”
- Clicking a thumbnail navigates to that frame.
- Hovering over thumbnails could show a larger temporary preview.

### Alternatives Considered
- Manual labels and text on the canvas: cluttery and static.
- Duplicate frames vs. storyboard strip: duplicative and harder to manage.
- External presentation apps: disconnected context and not seamless.

### Additional Context
- A lightweight, hand-drawn visual flair suits Excalidraw’s aesthetic.
- Could build upon existing frame-thumbnail generation logic.

author: kaiaiz

title: Feature request: add optional AI assistant that turns natural-language prompts into diagrams
body:
Creating clean flow-charts, sequence diagrams or architecture sketches from scratch is slow and repetitive. You often end up googling for icons, dragging boxes and aligning arrows instead of thinking about the actual system.

Describe the solution you’d like

Add an optional AI assistant that:
  1. Lives behind a new button or keyboard shortcut (e.g. ⌘/Ctrl + K → “Generate diagram”).
  2. Accepts plain-English prompts like
    • “Show me a login flow with email + Google OAuth”
    • “Three-tier web app: React front-end, Node API, Postgres DB, add load balancer”
  3. Instantly drops fully laid-out Excalidraw elements (boxes, arrows, labels, icons) onto the canvas.
  4. Lets users iterate: select any generated set and re-prompt—“make this micro-service architecture” or “add error handling paths”.

Configuration & privacy

To keep the core project 100 % client-side and open-source:
  • No hard-wired cloud provider.
  • Expose a simple settings pane:
    Table
    Copy
    | Field | Example | | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | | AI endpoint URL | `https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions` | | Auth token (Bearer) | `sk-…` | | Model name | `gpt-4-turbo` |
  • The call is made directly from the browser to the endpoint the user specifies; Excalidraw servers never see the prompt or the response.
  • If the user leaves the fields empty, the AI button is disabled and no extra code is loaded → zero impact on bundle size or privacy.

Non-goals / out of scope

  • Hosting or paying for an official AI service.
  • Storing prompts or diagrams anywhere outside the user’s browser.
  • Requiring any backend changes to Excalidraw.

Acceptance checklist

  • [ ] Add “AI Assistant” toggle in settings (default off).
  • [ ] Minimal UI: prompt input, generate button, cancel button.
  • [ ] Parse OpenAI-style JSON responses into Excalidraw elements (shapes, text, arrows, groups).
  • [ ] Surface network / auth errors in a toast.
  • [ ] Unit tests for prompt → elements parser.
  • [ ] Docs updated: how to use your own OpenAI, Azure, Claude, local Ollama, etc.
This keeps Excalidraw itself free, open and private while letting anyone opt into powerful AI diagram generation

title: Feature Request: Eraser Tool Option to Target Only Pen Strokes
body:
Hi Excalidraw team 👋
I'd love to suggest a feature enhancement for the eraser tool in Excalidraw.
Currently, the eraser removes all elements indiscriminately—including freehand pen strokes, highlights, shapes, and embedded images. It would be incredibly helpful to have an option that allows the eraser to target only freehand pen strokes, while ignoring other elements like highlights or imported images.
✨ Use Case
When sketching diagrams or taking handwritten notes, I often want to erase only the pen marks without affecting highlights or visual references. Accidental deletion of important elements like images or highlights can disrupt the workflow and lead to unnecessary rework.
💡 Suggested Options
- Eraser mode: “Pen strokes only”
- Option to exclude highlights from erasing
- Option to protect embedded images from the eraser tool
If there’s already a way to achieve this via settings or scripting, I’d appreciate any pointers or keywords to help locate it. Otherwise, I hope this idea can be considered for future updates!
Thanks for building such an amazing tool—Excalidraw has truly transformed the way I visualize and organize ideas.
author: kanghangsu

title: Feature Request: Option to Restrict Canvas Panning to One Direction
body:
Hi Excalidraw team 👋
I'd like to propose a feature enhancement related to canvas navigation.
Excalidraw currently offers an infinitely expandable canvas in all directions, which is fantastic for freeform sketching and brainstorming. However, in certain use cases—such as annotating a series of document images—it would be helpful to restrict canvas panning to a single direction (either vertical or horizontal).
🧭 Use Case
When working with vertically stacked documents or horizontally arranged visual references, limiting panning to one axis can improve focus and reduce disorientation. For example, when annotating PDFs or scanned pages, vertical-only panning helps maintain a consistent flow and prevents accidental drifting across the canvas.
Zooming in and out should still be fully supported to allow detailed pen work and precise annotations.
💡 Suggested Feature
- Canvas Panning Mode:
- Default: Free (current behavior)
- Vertical-only
- Horizontal-only
- This could be a toggle in the UI or a setting in the canvas preferences.
This feature would enhance usability for workflows that are more document-centric or linear in nature, while preserving the flexibility that makes Excalidraw so powerful.
Thanks again for building such an intuitive and versatile tool—looking forward to seeing how it evolves!

title: Stylus stuck in move or zoom mode after lowering stylus whilst a finger is on the touchscreen
body:
Device: Microsoft Surface Go 2
OS: Linux Mint 22.1 Gnome
Environment: excalidraw.com on Firefox

Steps to reproduce:
- Select the draw tool (pen mode can be on or off)
- Put a finger on the touchscreen
- Without lifting the finger, try to draw something with a stylus
- The stylus will now be stuck in zoom-mode if the pen mode was off. If the pen mode was on, it will be stuck in move-mode except when I press the eraser button, which triggers zoom-mode again. (This might take two or three attempts, but it's not uncommon)
- This persists even if I remove the finger, lift the stylus and put it back down, or use the stylus to select other tools (but using my finger to select other tools gets the stylus unstuck)
- Another way to get the stylus unstuck is to click or double click the canvas with the finger (might take a few attempts)

This bug can be annoying when using the stylus, because the app will quite often recognize my hand as a touch screen input whilst I temporarily lift the stylus, leading to it getting stuck like this.

I don't think this issue is the same as the other pan & zoom issues mentioned in #9705

title: Excalidraw Hindi language code not working.
body:
Excalidraw **Hindi language code not working**.

I can see in [Excalidraw i18n.ts](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/i18n.ts#L15) that `hi-IN` should be supported.

![Excalidraw screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/471356e8-8752-473a-be7b-d0bc6584d4fc)

It is passed as a prop in `langCode`:

```tsx
<Excalidraw  
  langCode="hi-IN"
/>
```

However, it falls back to the language `"en"` and does not load its corresponding JSON.

author: anon1789

title: Feature Request: Rotate Image Functionality
body:
**Summary**
Currently, Excalidraw allows inserting images into the canvas, but there is no way to rotate them once placed. Users often need to adjust the orientation of images (e.g., scanned documents, diagrams, screenshots taken sideways).

**Proposed solution**

* Add a **Rotate Image** option, either via:

  * A rotation handle (similar to how shapes are rotated).
  * Or a toolbar/context menu action (e.g., "Rotate 90° left/right").

**Why is this useful?**

* Prevents users from having to rotate images externally before inserting them.
* Improves workflow and keeps everything editable directly in Excalidraw.
* Aligns with common UX expectations in other design tools (Figma, Miro, PowerPoint, etc.).

**Optional enhancements**

* Support free rotation (with snapping to 15°/45° increments).
* Quick actions for 90° rotations.

**Alternatives considered**
At the moment, the only workaround is to rotate the image outside of Excalidraw and re-upload it, which is inconvenient.

title: [Feature] Add support for multiple series in charts
body: ## Context When pasting two columns of CSV, a chart is being rendered using the first column as labels, the second column as data and the first row as headers. ## Requirement I would need more than two columns to be supported, so that multiple series of data could be displayed: in case of a bar chart, they would be displayed next to each other; in case of a line chart, they would be displayed on top of each other. The color of the series would be different, using the colors in that palette. ## Example ``` Month | Alice | Bob | Cecil ----- | ----- | --- | ----- Jan. | 1 | 5 | 2 Feb. | 1 | 5 | 1 March | 2 | 3 | 5 ``` ## Note This would only work with text labels and headers only, so the existing logic would be changed slightly: if there are two columns with number values, they would be displayed as two series, instead of the current implementation, which would use the first column as labels.

body:

author: imanzaker

title: Hot-reload support for .excalidraw files with viewport preservation
body:
Is it possible to hot-reload of .excalidraw file from disk while the diagram is open in the browser?
This would allow real-time visualization of changes made to diagram files through external tools (such as AI agents) without requiring manual browser refresh or file re-import.

I've built a simple React app that can reload .excalidraw files, but reloading the entire file resets the zoom level and diagram position, which breaks the user experience. The ideal solution would be an API that can apply partial changes by diffing two versions of the file, preserving the current viewport state.

I'm willing to implement this feature myself if the maintainers are open to it and can provide guidance on:

• Preferred approach for implementing element diffing and partial updates
• Existing APIs or patterns within Excalidraw for updating elements without viewport reset
• Code architecture and integration points within the existing codebase
• Testing requirements and browser compatibility considerations
• Any specific patterns the project follows for similar features

title: race conditions with drag-dropping library items on canvas
body:
1. install a larger library (20-30 items at least)
2. select all (hold shift and click on first and then the last)
3. drag them quickly over to the canvas

If you're fast enough and the library items are large enough, they won't be inserted on canvas. Also depends on CPU speed.

I haven't checked, but I believe it's because of the delay between starting to drag and how much time the `dataTransfer.setData()` call takes (it's synchronous):

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/f55ecb96cc8db9a2417d48cd8077833c3822d64e/packages/excalidraw/components/LibraryMenuItems.tsx#L176-L184

TODO

- [ ] investigate if this is indeed the root of the problem
- [ ] if so, only encode a lightweight data that serializes quickly (e.g. list of library ids)
- [ ] on drop, parse the ids and resolve to the full library items

title: Feature request: improve shareable links customizability
body:
I wanted to add a diagram to my readme. Exporting it as an image is of course an option, but it means the diagram cannot be changed easily, and I would have to store a  share link to diagram somewhere anyway, yada yada.

So I thought: it would be really cool to embed the link itself as an `iframe` in markdown (I guess it actually may be a horrible idea, smack me if it is):
you get full interaction, can zoom in on any part of the diagram and move stuff around. You can change anything right inside the readme, and to apply the change you just reexport the share link and commit it. So it seems like a cool feature.

It went quite well, but for now, this approach is not really usable for a couple of reasons: 
1. It's kind of hard to predict the default position of the diagram, so a consumer of my readme would have to pan the image by themselves (although I suspect this might be a skill issue on my side).
2. The link opens in edit mode, which is unnecessary for my scenario, I'd like to be able to share a link in view mode.
3. Excalidraw gives a warning that it's going to replace existing content with the content from link. This renders my approach completely unusable. It would be really cool to have a query param or something that would tell excalidraw to not mess with existing content. I have no idea how possible it  is to implement, but if it is, it would be great

title: Feature Request: Import Flowchart Images and Auto-Convert to Excalidraw Shapes
body:
### Problem

Many users work with pre-existing flowcharts saved as PNG images (from whiteboards, scanned drawings, or exported diagrams). Manually redrawing these diagrams in Excalidraw is time-consuming and error-prone.

### Solution

A feature that allows users to upload a PNG image of a flowchart, which an AI-powered backend would process to extract diagram structure and convert it into editable Excalidraw elements (shapes, arrows, text, styles, positions).

### Flow

1. User uploads a PNG image of a flowchart.
2. AI model detects flowchart components (e.g., shapes, connectors, text).
3. Elements are reconstructed and imported into the Excalidraw canvas with editable properties.
author: AparAgarwal

title: [Feature Request] Improve navigation between same-level rectangles after editing with Ctrl+Enter
body:
**Description:**

I've noticed that in Excalidraw, after creating a rectangle, using Alt + Arrow Key quickly adds a child rectangle with an arrow connection. Pressing Enter then allows me to add text immediately. This is an incredibly useful feature, and I love using it to create mind maps while still enjoying the freedom of a blank canvas.

However, I’ve noticed a behavior that feels a bit counter-intuitive:

Currently, to focus on the next sibling rectangle (at the same level), I have two options: 
1) Use the mouse to click on it, which forces me to switch frequently between my keyboard and mouse.
2) Press Alt + Arrow Key (any direction) to move up to the parent, and then immediately repeat the shortcut to jump to the next child. 

The first option disrupts the keyboard-centric workflow, while the second feels counter-intuitive and inefficient for a simple sibling navigation.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea4a8527-f541-4c18-957e-507a2f60a800)

**Suggested Improvement:**

It would be much more efficient if, after finishing editing a rectangle at the same level, I could use something like Ctrl + Arrow Key to directly focus on the next sibling rectangle at the same level—without having to return to the parent first. Alternatively, a different keyboard shortcut could be introduced to achieve this.

This has been a minor pain point in my workflow for a while, and resolving it would significantly improve my efficiency when building mind maps.

Thank you for all your hard work—Excalidraw is an amazing tool that I use and appreciate daily!

author: dwelle

title: [Feature Request] Native Code Block Support with Syntax Highlighting
body:
**Problem Statement**
Currently, Excalidraw does not have native support for displaying code blocks with syntax highlighting. When users paste code into Excalidraw, it appears as plain text without any formatting, requiring manual styling to create the appearance of a code block.

**Current Limitations**
- Code pasted into Excalidraw loses all original formatting and syntax highlighting
- Users must manually adjust fonts (e.g., switching to monospace), create background shapes, and format text
- No built-in syntax highlighting for different programming languages
- The workaround through Obsidian plugin (transclusion) is only available to Obsidian users, not the core web application

**Proposed Solution**
Implement native code block support in Excalidraw with the following features:
1. Rich Text Paste Support: Detect when code is copied from modern editors (like VS Code with editor.copyWithSyntaxHighlighting enabled) and preserve the RTF formatting from the clipboard.
2. Code Block Element Type: Create a new element type specifically for code blocks with:
  - Automatic monospace font application
  - Preserved indentation
  - Optional background container (similar to cards)
  - Line numbers (optional)
3. Syntax Highlighting: Support basic syntax highlighting for popular languages:
  - JavaScript/TypeScript
  - Python
  - Java
  - C/C++
  - HTML/CSS
4. Theme Preservation: When pasting from editors, attempt to preserve the color theme (light/dark background with corresponding syntax colors)

**Inspiration from Similar Tools**
Google Docs successfully interprets rich text data from VS Code when pasting, preserving syntax highlighting and themes. Excalidraw could implement similar clipboard detection and rendering.

**Expected Behavior**
1. User copies code from their editor (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.)
2. User pastes into Excalidraw (Ctrl+V / Cmd+V)
3. Excalidraw detects rich text formatting and creates a code block element
4. Code appears with syntax highlighting and proper formatting preserved
5. User can resize, move, and style the code block like other Excalidraw elements

**Alternative Approach**
If full rich text interpretation is complex, consider a simpler initial implementation:

- Add an "Insert Code Block" option in the menu
- Provide language selection dropdown
- Apply basic syntax highlighting using a library like Prism.js or Highlight.js
- Allow users to paste plain code that gets automatically formatted

**Additional Context**
This feature was mentioned in Issue #9456  and would be particularly valuable for developers and technical writers who frequently combine visual diagrams with code examples.

When running a presentation, the current behaviour is to show only content within frames, but not just for the current slide/frame: all frames are visible if the current frame does not fit onto the screen exactly.

* Display contents of all frames (but not other content) [current behaviour]
* Display all content

Thanks for consideration.

title: Canvas Rendering Performance Optimization
body:
## Summary
Optimize canvas rendering pipeline for better performance with large element counts. Current separate static/interactive rendering causes redundant calculations.

## Current Issues
- Separate `renderStaticSceneThrottled` and `renderInteractiveSceneThrottled` calls
- O(n) viewport checking with `isElementInViewport` for every element
- Multiple RAF throttling competing for frames
- No spatial indexing for element lookups

## Proposed Solution
1. **Unified Rendering Pipeline**: Merge static/interactive rendering
2. **Spatial Indexing**: Implement quadtree for O(log n) viewport culling  
3. **Dirty Region Tracking**: Minimize unnecessary redraws
4. **Object Pooling**: Reduce GC pressure during interactions

## Expected Impact
- 30-50% rendering performance improvement
- Better performance with 1000+ elements
- Smoother animations and interactions
- Reduced memory usage

## Implementation Plan
- Phase 1: Rendering pipeline unification
- Phase 2: Spatial optimization (quadtree/R-tree)
- Phase 3: Memory and performance optimizations

Labels: enhancement, performance, canvas, rendering

title: [Feature Request] Make Text Within Linked Shapes Clickable
body: ### Problem Statement Currently, when creating a shape (e.g. a rectangle) and assigning it a URL, the shape itself becomes clickable, redirecting the browser to the defined link. However, if text is inserted into the shape (by double-clicking it), that text **does not inherit the shape’s clickability**. As a result, clicking directly on the text does nothing, even though the surrounding shape is interactive. Without using code, the only workaround is to: 1. Create a separate text element. 2. Manually assign the same URL to both the shape and the text. This approach is inefficient and error-prone, especially when editing or updating links. ### Expected Behavior Text that is embedded within a shape that has a defined URL should automatically be **clickable** behaving as part of the same interactive area. In other words, clicking either the shape or the text should trigger the same navigation action. ### Proposed Solution A simple way to implement this behavior would be to automatically apply `pointer-events: none` to the text elements that are inside shapes, **unless** the text itself has its own URL. For example, I use the following global CSS rule as a workaround: ```css text{pointer-events:none} a text{pointer-events:initial} ``` A better solution I think is when exporting to SVG, the shape’s text could be automatically wrapped or styled like this: **Option 1: Add inline style** ```html Example ``` **Option 2: Or even better (I think) is to wrap text in an anchor** ```html Example ``` This ensures consistency between the shape’s interactivity and its contained text, improving both usability and expected behavior during export.

author: Flinner

title: Feature Request: Offline Mode for Collaboration
body:
Description

Excalidraw's real-time collaboration feature is great, but it stops working if a user loses their internet connection. This can interrupt workflow and cause frustration, especially for those with unreliable internet.

Solution
I propose an "Offline Mode for Collaboration" that would allow users to continue working on a shared canvas even without an internet connection. Once the connection is back, their changes would automatically sync with the other collaborators.

Approach
Local Storage of Changes: When a user goes offline, any changes they make should be saved locally in their browser's IndexedDB.

Conflict Resolution: When the user is back online, the app needs to merge their local changes with the changes made by others. We could use one of the following strategies:

Last-write-wins: The most recent change to an element is saved.

Operational Transformation (OT): A more advanced method that merges changes from multiple users without losing data.

User-driven conflict resolution: Ask the user to manually decide which changes to keep.

UI/UX Considerations:

Offline Indicator: Show an icon or banner to let the user know they are working offline.

Syncing Status: Display a visual indicator when the app is syncing local changes.

Conflict Resolution UI: If we choose user-driven conflict resolution, we'll need to create a simple and clear interface to help users resolve conflicts.    

@dwelle  Assign this to me
author: Prateekiiitg56

title: [UI Issue] Bottom panels obstructed by Android interface on mobile
body:
### Preface
I am unsure if this should be an excalidraw issue or an obsidian issue, because while technically this issue occurs because of how obsidian interacts with the Android interface, it is only a real issue with excalidraw, because obsidian doesn't have any bottom panels natively on mobile


### System specs
Tablet Samsung Tab S6
OS: One UI 7.0


### Issue
The lower part of the interface (where the tray, the undo/redo button and the zoom are located) is obstructed by Android's navigation bar both in normal mode and Dex mode.

I am pretty sure this is because how obsidian works in fullscreen on mobile. Most apps either adjust the size of the fullscreen window to leave space for the navigation bar or toggle off the navigation bar. Obsidian does neither, it just fullscreens to the entire screen. Since the navigation bar is hierarchically higher, it makes everything inacessible that's behind the navigation bar.

Unfortunately that makes excaildraw effectively unusable on my tablet, because I can't access the undo button.


### Current workaround 
Use obsidian in windowed mode. This is not a viable solution, because the screen real estate sacrificed to this issue is too high.


### Proposed solution
Add toggle to interface options to leave a sufficient gap between the bottom of the obsidian window and the tray/bottom panels.

title: Style changes should affect the last created shape like they do for text elements
body:
Currently, when changing a style (e.g., fill color, stroke color, stroke width), the update is only applied to future shapes, not the one that was just created.

However, text elements behave differently:
If I create a text and then change its color or font weight, the change is applied immediately to the existing text.

But for other shapes (e.g., rectangle, diamond, line), the new style is applied only to shapes created afterward, not the one I just drew.

This feels inconsistent and unintuitive.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Draw a rectangle, diamond, or line
2. Change stroke or fill color in the toolbar
3. The previously drawn shape keeps the old style
4. Draw another shape → the new style applies only to this new one

Now try the same with a text element:

- Create a text
- Change the color → the existing text is updated immediately


## Expected Behavior (Suggestion)

- If there is a selected or just-created element, apply the style change to it immediately (same as current text behavior)
- If nothing is selected, apply the style as default for future shapes (current behavior)

This would:
- Make behavior consistent across element types
- Reduce extra clicks
- Match user expectations from design tools


## Why It’s Useful

- Text already supports this UX, so shapes should too
- Avoids unnecessary select → restyle steps
- Increases workflow efficiency and consistency

## etc

If this is okay, can I implement it?
