title: Add Bengali (bn) translation to Excalidraw
body:
## Summary
I would like to contribute Bengali (bn) language support to Excalidraw as part of Hacktoberfest. This enhancement will make Excalidraw more accessible to Bengali-speaking users worldwide.

## Details
- Add a new Bengali translation file (`bn-BD.json`) under `packages/excalidraw/locales/` with comprehensive translations for all UI elements, actions, tooltips, and dialogs.
- Update the i18n configuration to register Bengali as a supported language.
- Ensure Bengali appears in the language selector and all translated strings display correctly across the UI.
- Test for proper layout, text rendering, and fallback to English for any missing translations.

## Motivation
Bengali is one of the most spoken languages globally. Adding Bengali support will benefit millions of users in Bangladesh, India, and Bengali-speaking communities around the world. This also supports the spirit of Hacktoberfest by promoting open-source localization and inclusivity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All UI strings are translated and formatted correctly in `bn-BD.json`
- [ ] Bengali language appears in the selector and is usable throughout the app
- [ ] No UI or rendering issues caused by new translation
- [ ] Proper fallback to English for any missing strings

## Additional Notes
I am submitting this issue as part of Hacktoberfest and I am committed to following the contribution guidelines and translation standards of the project.

title: [UI Issue] Poor visibility and inconsistent warning behavior when attempting to start a live collaboration session offline
body: ### Description When attempting to start a live collaboration session with no internet connection, the application displays a small warning in the upper-left corner that is barely noticeable. After a few seconds, another message appears about a database communication error. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open Excalidraw 2. Disconnect from the internet 3. Try to start a “Live Collaboration” session 4. Observe the warning messages ### Expected Behavior A clear, visible error message should appear immediately. The message should indicate that live collaboration requires an active internet connection. ### Actual Behavior A small, low-contrast warning appears in the top-left corner. After a few seconds, another generic “communication with database failed” message appears. ### Screenshot ### Environment Browser: [e.g. Chrome 141.0.7390.54] OS: [e.g. ArchLinux 6.16.8-arch3-1]
author: BrunoBReis

title: Users can access and modify content from a terminated live collaboration session
body: ### Description When a user tries to access a collaboration session after it has been terminated, the system still allows access using the old link. The connected client (Browser B) can see previous content, even though the session was supposedly closed by the host (Browser A). Additionally, when Browser A reopens a new session, it sometimes reuses the same URL, creating two separate, unlinked instances that share an identical session link, which may lead to data confusion or potential security issues. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Browser A: Start a live collaboration session and copy the link. 2. Browser B: Keep the link ready but do not connect yet. 3. Browser A: End the session. 4. Browser B: Try to join the session using the old link. 5. Observe that Browser B can still access and view the old content. 6. Browser A: Start a new session, note that it may use the same URL as before. ### Expected Behavior When a session is terminated, its link should become invalid. Attempting to access a terminated session should show a clear error message such as: “This session has been closed by the host.” ### Actual Behavior When a session is terminated, its link should become invalid. Attempting to access a terminated session should show a clear error message such as: “This session has been closed by the host.” ### Screenshot *Browser A* *Browser B* ### Environment Browser: [e.g. Chrome 141.0.7390.54] OS: [e.g. ArchLinux 6.16.8-arch3-1]
author: BrunoBReis

title: Artificially generated room keys can create valid collaboration sessions without authorization
body: ### Description By manually generating a session key that follows the same format as Excalidraw’s collaboration keys, it is possible to create or access a session without using an officially shared link. This behavior suggests that the app accepts arbitrary keys as valid session identifiers, allowing users to create sessions programmatically without explicit authorization. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Generate a key using the following Bash command: `echo "$(openssl rand -base64 17 | head -c 22),$(openssl rand -base64 17 | head -c 22)" | wl-copy` 2. Paste the generated key into the Excalidraw collaboration URL structure (e.g.`https://excalidraw.com/#room=`). 3. Access the URL in the browser. ### Expected Behavior The system should validate keys and reject any artificially generated or unauthorized keys. No new room should be created without explicit user interaction.“This session has been closed by the host.” ### Actual Behavior A new collaboration room is created automatically using the artificial key. The user gains access to a session without any authorization or prior link sharing. ### Screenshot ### Environment Browser: [e.g. Chrome 141.0.7390.54] OS: [e.g. ArchLinux 6.16.8-arch3-1]

author: relfrank007

title: Color Picker should display the visually-inverted hex code in dark mode
body:
### Problem
Currently, when using Excalidraw in dark mode, canvas and element colors are visually inverted via a CSS or canvas filter, but the hex code shown in the color picker input remains the same as in light mode (e.g., setting background as `#333` shows as a light color but still displays `#333` in the input).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2559dd06-f9be-4379-ae61-4b572b817aa4

This is confusing for users, as the displayed hex code does not match the visible color in dark mode.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Set a canvas or element color to a dark value like `#333` in light mode.
2. Switch to dark mode.
3. The canvas color is visually inverted (appears light), but the color picker input still shows `#333`.

### Expected Behavior
- The color picker input should show the visually-rendered (inverted) hex code when in dark mode so that the hex value matches what users see.

### Proposed Solution
- Add a utility to invert hex values programmatically.
- Update `ColorInput` and pass the current theme as a prop.
- When in dark mode, display the inverted hex code in the input for user clarity, but keep the underlying color logic unchanged.
- Example utility:
  ```ts
  export function invertHexColor(hex: string): string {
    let c = hex.replace("#", "");
    if (c.length === 3) c = c[0]+c[0]+c[1]+c[1]+c[2]+c[2];
    if (c.length !== 6) return hex;
    let r = (255 - parseInt(c.slice(0, 2), 16)).toString(16).padStart(2, "0");
    let g = (255 - parseInt(c.slice(2, 4), 16)).toString(16).padStart(2, "0");
    let b = (255 - parseInt(c.slice(4, 6), 16)).toString(16).padStart(2, "0");
    return "#" + r + g + b;
  }
  ```
- Update the color input logic:
  ```tsx
  // In ColorInput.tsx
  const displayColor = theme === "dark" ? invertHexColor(color) : color;
  ```

### Relevant Files
- `packages/excalidraw/components/ColorPicker/ColorInput.tsx`
- `packages/excalidraw/components/ColorPicker/ColorPicker.tsx`
- `packages/common/src/colors.ts`

### Labels
- bug
- dark-theme
- enhancement

### Additional Context
This will improve user experience and reduce confusion when working with colors across themes.
author: SatyamVyas04

title: Scaling multi-selected items sometimes scales dimensionally, sometimes aspect-ratio

title: Background color option is enabled but non-functional for open-path lines
body:
**Description**
The user interface allows selecting a "Background" color for a simple, open-path line, but the selection has no visual effect. This can be confusing as it suggests the feature is broken, when it should likely be disabled for this type of element.

**Steps to Reproduce**

- Select the "Line" tool.

- Draw a simple, open line on the canvas (with only a start and an end point).

- With the line selected, observe the properties panel on the left.

- Click on the "Background" color option.

- Select any color from the palette.

**Expected Behavior**
The "Background" color option should be disabled (grayed out and non-clickable) when an open-path line is selected, as it does not have an area to fill.

**Actual Behavior**
The "Background" color option is enabled and clickable. When a color is selected, the UI indicates the color has been chosen, but there is no visual change to the line itself.

**Environment**

OS: Windows 10

Browser: Chrome 141.0.7390.66

Thank you for maintaining this fantastic tool!
author: Acciolyy

title: Input Validation: Invalid 4-character hex codes are processed inconsistently instead of being rejected
body:
**Description**
When a user enters a hexadecimal color code with an invalid length (e.g., 4 characters like #1234), the system does not reject it as invalid. Instead, it processes the input and converts it to an unexpected color.


**Steps to Reproduce**

- Select any shape tool (e.g., Rectangle, Line, Diamond).

- Draw a shape on the canvas.

- Test Case A (Stroke):
  a. With the shape selected, click on the "Stroke" color property.
  b. In the "Hex code" input field, type 1234 and press Enter.
  c. Observe that the stroke color changes to a grey, strange color.

- Test Case B (Background):
  a. With the same shape selected, click on the "Background" color property.
  b. In the "Hex code" input field, type 1234 and press Enter.
  c. Observe that the background color changes to a solid gray color.

**Expected Behavior**
The system should treat a 4-character hex code as invalid and reject the input in all cases. The color of the shape should not change, and the invalid code should not be saved or processed. (Note: 3-character shorthand codes like "#FFF" work as expected, but 4-character codes should be invalid).

**Actual Behavior**
The system accepts the 4-character code but processes it inconsistently:

1. For the Stroke, "#1234" results in a grey color. 
2. For the Background, the same "#1234" code results in a gray color too.
3. In both cases, the invalid input is processed instead of being rejected.

**Environment**

OS: Windows 10

Browser: Chrome 141.0.7390.66


**Thanks!**

title: UX: Remember last used style per element type, not globally
body:
I am going to propose a design change. Probably I am biased, because I mainly use Excalidraw to draw user interfaces. Maybe there are other user cases where this proposal is not a good one.

When I draw a wireframe usually I keep the same style for classes of elments. Say "arrows" are gray, "text" is black. Now when I change the text to blue for example, the next arrow I draw will be blue. If the last arrow was gray, I'd like the program to keep the new one gray, not change to blue.

Say it another way I'd like the program to remember last text stroke color instead of last stroke each time I start drawing a text element.
author: luc4leone

title: [UX Bug] Invalid Email Error Displays Raw JSON Object Instead of User-Friendly Message
body:
**Problem Description**

When attempting to use the "Passwordless sign-in" feature with an email address that contains **accented or special characters** (non-ASCII, such as 'á', 'é', 'õ', or 'ç'), the system fails to correctly display the validation error. Instead of showing a user-friendly message, the **raw JSON error object** is displayed directly in the form interface.

**Expected Behavior**

The system should display a clearly formatted, user-friendly error message to the end-user.

* *Example of Expected Message:* "Invalid email address. Please use only basic Latin characters (A-Z, 0-9) and standard symbols."

**Current Behavior (Bug)**

The system exposes the internal API/validation response as raw JSON, as seen in the attached screenshot.

* *Example of Current Display:*
    ```json
    [{"origin": "string", "code": "invalid_format", "format": "email", "pattern": "/^((?!\\.)...", "message": "Invalid email address", "path": ["email"]}]
    ```

**Steps to Reproduce**

1.  Navigate to the "Passwordless sign-in" page.
2.  In the email field, enter an address containing an accented character (e.g., `eádjig@example.com`).
3.  Attempt to submit the form (or trigger the field validation/blur).
4.  Observe that the raw JSON object is displayed as the validation feedback.

**Impact**

* **UX (User Experience):** The message is not understandable to the end-user, leading to confusion and frustration.
* **Security/Exposure:** The error exposes internal validation details, such as the regular expression (`"pattern"`) used by the system.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d0b27262-aadd-4570-9482-09dce930eedc)

title: [BUG] Text Element incorrectly associated with the original container after "Wrap text in a container"
body:
**Description:**

The Text Element written inside a Shape (Rectangle, Diamond, Ellipse) appears to retain a reference or association to the Original Container after being "wrapped" in a New Container using the "Wrap text in a container" action.

This causes the Text Element to be incorrectly associated with two containers simultaneously, leading to errors when manipulating the original object.

Note. The Shapes are: Rectangle, Diamond, or Ellipse.

**Steps to Reproduce:**

- Insert any shape (e.g., Rectangle).
- Write embedded text within the shape (the text is part of the shape, not a separate element).
- Select the shape using the Group Selection action.
- Right-click and select the Wrap text in a container option.
- Ungroup using the Ungroup Selection action.
- Result: There are two separate shapes/containers:
- New Container (small, associated with the text).
- Original Shape (the initial large element).

**The Problem:**

When moving or resizing the Original Shape, the following is observed:

- Movement Issue: When moving the Original Shape, the Text Element inside the New Container also moves with it incorrectly, indicating it still maintains the association to the original parent.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fc39252b-87c7-4f5b-ab75-1244b7876be5

- Resizing/Editing Issue: When changing the size of the Original Shape or trying to edit the text, the text element appears to interact erratically or visually swap between the Original Shape and the New Container.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4e2f7c17-5b75-439c-8272-646d38c11c9f


**Desired Behaviour:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dcb97579-48b8-4920-90ee-a3fe69de0fd5
author: Joakim31

title: Feature Request: Option to Zoom with Mouse Wheel Without Holding Ctrl
body:
**Description**
Currently, zooming in and out with the mouse requires holding the Ctrl key while scrolling.
It would be more user-friendly to include an optional setting that allows zooming directly with the mouse wheel — without the need to hold Ctrl.

**Rationale**
Allowing zoom by default with the mouse wheel would make navigation smoother and more intuitive, especially for designers who frequently move around the canvas.
This feature would enable users to quickly zoom in or out and explore different areas of the canvas simply by pointing the mouse and scrolling, offering a more fluid and precise control experience.

**Suggested Implementation**
Add a toggle in the settings (e.g., “Zoom with mouse wheel without Ctrl”) so users can choose their preferred behavior.

title: Feature Request: Advanced Laser Tool Options (Pointer Modes)
body:
At the moment, the laser tool provides a single behavior: when used, it draws a red trail that follows the cursor and fades away after a few seconds.
There are no additional options or modes available for different presentation styles or user preferences.

This proposal extends the laser tool with multiple selectable modes, adjustable line thickness, and an optional neon-style visual effect. These options are exposed via a Laser pointer submenu (navbar dropdown) in the main toolbar, so users can switch modes and adjust settings without leaving the flow.

---

### Proposed Laser Modes
####  1. Pointer Mode – Classic Laser Pointer
- Displays a single glowing dot that follows the cursor position, without leaving any trail or drawing.
- Functions like a real physical laser pointer, ideal for quick and precise highlighting.
- No drawings remain on the canvas after movement stops.

#### 2. Annotation Mode – Persistent Laser
- Works similarly to the persistent laser behavior proposed in [#9884](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/new#9884)
- Allows users to draw freely, keeping the strokes visible until manually cleared.
- Suitable for explanations or annotations that need to stay visible longer.
- Should include a “Clear all laser drawings” action for quick removal, or automatically clear all drawings when switching away from this mode.

#### 3. Hold-to-Draw Mode – Temporary Fade
- The stroke remains visible only while the mouse or touch is pressed.
- Once released, the line gradually fades out instead of disappearing instantly.
- Useful for quick, short-lived highlights during live discussions.

---

### Additional Options

#### Thickness Control
- Add a slider or numeric input to adjust the laser stroke width (for example, 1 px–10 px).
- Helps adapt the laser’s visibility to different display sizes or audience settings.

#### Neon Glow Effect
- Each laser line or dot could include a thin white inner line with a soft outer glow (box-shadow).
- Improves visibility on dark backgrounds and adds a modern, polished appearance similar to presentation tools like iPad or Keynote lasers.

*I would be interested in implementing this feature and can start working on it once the proposal is reviewed and approved.*

title: Feature Request: Add Lorem Ipsum Text Generator inside Excalidraw
body:
### 🧩 Feature Request: Add Lorem Ipsum Text Generator inside Excalidraw

**Description**
Add a built-in Lorem Ipsum text generator that allows users to quickly insert placeholder text directly onto the canvas.

For example:

* Typing `lorem10` generates 10 words of lorem text
* Typing `lorem50` generates 50 words, etc.

**Motivation / Use Case**
When creating mockups or UI wireframes, users often need placeholder text. Currently, they have to switch to external lorem generators, which breaks the design flow.

Integrating a simple generator inside Excalidraw would:

* Improve workflow speed
* Eliminate context-switching
* Help designers and developers prototype faster

**Proposed Implementation**

* Detect text inputs like `lorem<number>` and replace them with generated lorem ipsum text once the user presses *Enter* or *finishes editing*.
* Implementation can use a small internal function — no external dependency required.

**Example**
User types:

```
lorem20
```

It automatically expands to:

```
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua...
```


I’d be happy to work on this feature and submit a PR if the maintainers approve.

`www.mijnoverheid.nl/*`: Personal government services portal - don't allow subdomains as this requires login credentials, potential security risk.
`https://wetten.overheid.nl/*`: Official Dutch laws and regulations database.
`https://ondernemersplein.overheid.nl/*`: Government information portal for entrepreneurs and business owners.
`www.duo.nl/*`: Education financing information (DUO) - don't allow subdomains as there are pages requiring login credentials, potential security risk.
`www.uwv.nl/*`: Employee Insurance Agency information about unemployment and disability benefits - don't allow subdomains as there are pages requiring login credentials, potential security risk.
`www.svb.nl/*`: Social Insurance Bank information about state pension and child benefits - don't allow subdomains as there are pages requiring login credentials, potential security risk.
`www.kvk.nl/*`: Chamber of Commerce business information and registration - don't allow subdomains as there are pages requiring login credentials, potential security risk.
`www.ind.nl/*`: Immigration and Naturalisation Service official information and procedures.
`https://zoek.officielebekendmakingen.nl/*`: Official government publications and announcements database.
`www.justis.nl/*`: Ministry of Justice and Security services and information (VOG, permits).
`www.cbr.nl/*`: Central Office for Motor Vehicle Driver Testing information about licenses and exams.

This would allow people to embed important dutch information in their Excalidraws. I'm doing research and would greatly benefit from these URL's to be whitelisted. Alternatively, having a client-side whitelist and a way to allow/disallow websites would be a solution. Going from a whitelist to a blocklist would also be an option as mentioned here:
> @dwelle, @schoening  - Just to add my thoughts on this as well, it's a serious limitation in functionality compared to competitors (e.g., Miro, Lucid) that offer embedding of almost any webpage. I understand the concern about appropriate use but, ultimately, this really does limit the use of Excalidraw as a collaboration tool.
> 
> 
> 
> I'd even go so far as to suggest switching from an allow list to a block list could be a more appropriate way to deal with abuse if it happens. 

 _Originally posted by @mathewcsims in [#6917](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6917#issuecomment-2143553446)_

title: Auto-format for percent values
body:
I’ve noticed that the percent values such as zoom are not formatted according to the locale.

For instance, for Turkish, percentage sign is prepended to the number (`%100`). In most European languages, there is a non-breaking space after the number (`100 %`). However, in the app a standard format seems to be used. Some other languages use a completely different sign (Arabic, CJK languages etc.).

To remedy this, locale-aware percent formatting can be used. `Intl` has an option for this, it is very handy.

author: DataX8

title: Migrate from Yarn to Bun for improved performance
body:
## Summary

Migrate the Excalidraw monorepo from Yarn 1.22 to Bun 1.2.22 for improved performance and developer experience.

## Motivation

Had migrated my personal project to bun recently and saw great performance improvements. I migrated excalidraw locally and saw following results.

Benchmark | Yarn | Bun | Winner | Speedup
-- | -- | -- | -- | --
Fresh Install | 11-15s | 4.75s | 🏆 Bun | 3x faster
Cached Check | 2.03s | 0.23s | 🏆 Bun | 9x faster
Build Packages | 44.76s | 44.53s | ≈ Same | -
Script Startup | 0.5-1s | 0.11s | 🏆 Bun | 5-9x faster
Add Package | 2-3s | <1s | 🏆 Bun | 2-3x faster

## Proposed Changes

- [ ] Replace Yarn with Bun in all package.json files (8 workspaces)
- [ ] Update all GitHub Actions workflows (7 files) to use `oven-sh/setup-bun`
- [ ] Update Dockerfile to use `oven/bun:1` base image
- [ ] Update build scripts and git hooks

## Breaking Changes

None - drop-in replacement with zero breaking changes.

## Impact

- **CI/CD:** ~14s saved per run = ~4 hours/month
- **Developers:** ~8-16 min/day per developer saved
- **Team (5 devs):** ~16 hours/month productivity gain

title: Bug: Basic Shapes library shapes on Android lose resize frame after recent Excalidraw update
body:
### Environment
- OS: Android (Lenovo Tab M11, Android 15)
- App: Excalidraw (latest version — Android app and Obsidian plugin affected)
- Also occurs: Excalidraw web app when using mobile mode; does **not** occur if forcing desktop site
- Library: Basic Shapes (by pgilfernandez, imported from excalidraw-libraries repo)

### Summary
After a recent Excalidraw update, when inserting a shape from the Basic Shapes library on Android (Excalidraw standalone app or Obsidian plugin), the selection frame with resize handles is missing. Previously, shapes would show a frame/handles for resizing or other adjustments. The issue **does not occur** on the web if desktop mode is selected; the frame reappears. The problem affects platforms that do not have a desktop option (Android app, Obsidian plugin).

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open Excalidraw on Android (free-standing app, or as Obsidian plugin).
2. Import or use the Basic Shapes library (by pgilfernandez).
3. Drop any shape from the library onto the canvas.
4. Attempt to select or resize the shape.

### Expected behavior
Inserted shape shows a selection frame and resize handles, like other elements, and as it did before the recent update.

### Actual behavior
The shape has no selection frame and cannot be resized via handles. On the web version, the issue resolves by forcing desktop site mode; on Android/Obsidian, no workaround.

### Reproduction asset
- See above steps. (If needed, I can attach a minimal .excalidraw file or a screen recording in a comment.)

### Additional notes
- Device: Lenovo Tab M11, Android version 15
- No errors in Android app logs.
- Workaround available only by forcing desktop mode on web (not on Android app/Obsidian).
- Affects the Basic Shapes library by pgilfernandez (repo has issues disabled, hence reporting here).

Please tag @pgilfernandez for visibility.

---

Subscribed to issue for notifications.

title: Eraser deletes the entire element instead of erasing only a small portion of a line
body:
### Bug Description
When I use the eraser on a line or freehand stroke, Excalidraw removes the **entire element** instead of erasing only the small portion I drag over. This makes it impossible to erase just a segment of a line.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Draw a line or freehand stroke.
2. Select the eraser tool.
3. Try to erase a small part from the middle of the stroke.
4. The whole element gets deleted instead of just the erased part.

### Expected Behavior
Only the touched part of the line/stroke should be erased (partial erasing), and the rest of the element should remain.

### Actual Behavior
The full element is removed immediately even when erasing a tiny portion.

title: Missing Persian translations in fa-IR.json
body:
While auditing the Persian (fa-IR) localization file, I identified multiple keys with empty or null translation values, resulting in incomplete UI rendering for Persian users. These gaps directly impact the internationalization integrity, causing fallback failures and inconsistent UX across various components of the application. The affected keys include:

- polygon.breakPolygon
- polygon.convertToPolygon
- tab
- shapeSwitch
- library.search.inputPlaceholder
- library.search.heading
- library.search.noResults
- library.search.clearSearch
- search.frames
- search.texts
- toolBar.lasso
- toolBar.convertElementType

Additionally, there are other untranslated or partially translated entries discovered during a full audit of the locale file. I would like to contribute a patch to address these missing translations and ensure full coverage of the fa-IR locale.

title: Background-color picker becomes invisible when its swatch matches the canvas color
body: ## Description When editing a container in Excalidraw, I tried to change its background color. I couldn’t locate the background-color option in the left toolbar and initially thought it was missing. After checking closely, I realized the color swatch was actually there, but its fill color matched the canvas background, making it visually disappear. This causes confusion because the control appears absent even though it’s technically present. --- ## Steps to Reproduce * Select any shape on the canvas. * Press Cmd/Ctrl + / to open the Command Palette. * Choose “Change Background Color (Elements)” from the palette. * This opens the background-color tool (same as pressing E). * In the color palette, select Shade Level 2. * Shade Level 2 matches the toolbar’s background color, so the color swatch in the toolbar becomes camouflaged / low-visibility, making it appear as if the background-color control is missing. The swatch appears invisible / camouflaged, making it look like the background-color option is missing. --- ## Expected Behavior The background-color picker should remain visible regardless of the color value. A border, outline, or contrast indicator around the swatch would prevent it from disappearing. --- ## Actual Behavior The color picker swatch becomes camouflaged against the canvas background, making it appear as if the background-color option is missing. ## Screenshots Tooltip shown while hovering

title: Error: spawn xdg-open ENOENT when running yarn start on Headless Linux Server
body:
When attempting to run the Excalidraw development server using yarn start on a headless Ubuntu Server (which lacks a graphical environment and the xdg-open utility), the process throws an unhandled error and exits prematurely, preventing the server from staying active and accessible.

The server successfully initializes the Vite development environment and outputs the network addresses, but fails immediately after due to the attempt to automatically open a browser.

### **Steps to Reproduce**

i followed the steps from the documentation:
https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/introduction/development#local-installation


**Expected Behavior**

The development server should start successfully, print the Local and Network addresses, and continue running in the background, allowing access via the Network IP address from a remote browser.

**Actual Behavior**

The server starts, logs the successful Vite initialization, but then immediately throws an unhandled error and exits:

```
VITE v5.0.12 ready in 1015 ms

➜ Local: http://localhost:3000/
➜ Network: [http://192.168.57.2:3000/](http://192.168.57.2:3000/)
// ... Server outputs addresses
```

author: JackeyCloud

title: Renderer performance degrades due to full scene traversal on viewport updates
body:
While profiling rendering performance for large scenes, I noticed that the renderer performs a **full traversal of all non-deleted elements** on every viewport update (pan / zoom / resize).

Specifically, `Renderer.getRenderableElements` currently pulls all scene elements via:

```ts
scene.getNonDeletedElements()
```

and then applies `isElementInViewport` checks to determine visibility.
This results in **O(N)** work per render, regardless of how many elements actually intersect the viewport.

For scenes with thousands of elements, this causes noticeable lag during pan and zoom operations, even when only a small subset of elements is visible.

---

### Expected behavior

Rendering work should scale with the number of **viewport-relevant elements**, not total scene size.

---

### Current behavior

* Renderer always iterates over the full scene
* Visibility filtering happens after the full traversal
* Performance degrades linearly as scene size grows

---

### Scope

This issue affects:

* Panning and zooming large scenes
* Interactive rendering performance
* Any code path relying on `Renderer.getRenderableElements`

This is a **performance / architectural issue**, not a correctness bug.

---

### Additional context

The existing visibility logic (`isElementInViewport`) appears correct and should remain authoritative.
The main inefficiency comes from the renderer being tightly coupled to full scene traversal instead of querying viewport-based candidates.

---

### Motivation

Improving this would significantly benefit users working with large canvases, diagrams, or imported content, without changing rendering behavior or public APIs.

title: Grid mode shortcut too hard to reach
body:
I'm trying to prototype the early levels of my 2D game with Excalidraw, and I find myself toggling grid mode on and off pretty often. The grid mode shortcut `ctrl+'` currently is very hard to hit with the QWERTY layout because I need to reach all the way over to the right side of the keyboard with my left hand and use `right ctrl + '` or I have to move my hands off the mouse to hit it.

I saw that as the backtick key (\`) is currently not bound to anything in Excalidraw. Binding grid mode to "ctrl+`" would completely solve this issue at no cost to most QWERTY users (though I'm unsure of what the impacts are for international keyboard layouts).

Related issue: setting custom keyboard shortcuts in #3852

Thanks for making Excalidraw! The hand-drawn look makes drawing and creating easier to approach :)

title: Regression on examples/with-script-in-browser
body:
[Commit 8fb16669ab913b7618c6e55a4aba18275fabd1bb](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/commit/8fb16669ab913b7618c6e55a4aba18275fabd1bb) has introduced a regression on the `examples/with-script-in-browser` application, when running the application, there's an error that blocks the example application from loading.
```
 TypeError: can't access property "svg" of undefined
    <anonymous> renderElement.ts:372
 ```

## How to reproduce

- clone the project
- checkout to 8fb1666 (`git checkout 8fb1666`)
- Run the install and build scripts: `yarn clean-install && yarn rm:build && yarn start:example`
  - Example application at http://localhost:3001 doesn't work
- checkout to the previous commit to `8fb1666`, `f2600fe` ( `git checkout f2600fe`)
- Run the install and build scripts: `yarn clean-install && yarn rm:build && yarn start:example`
  - Example application at http://localhost:3001 works normally
author: kaique-lisboa

title: Feature Request: Add ability to change text orientation (horizontal/vertical)
body: **Goal** To allow users to rotate the text element (ExcalidrawTextElement) to a vertical orientation, in addition to the default horizontal orientation. Why is this needed? (Motivation) This feature will significantly enhance the ability to create complex diagrams and charts, proving especially useful in the following scenarios: Vertical Labels: For labeling vertical lines or axes in flowcharts, network diagrams, or graphs. Space Efficiency: To save horizontal space on the canvas when aligning multiple labels closely together. Proposed Implementation Details When a text element is selected, an option should be available in the properties panel to switch the text orientation: Data Structure: A new property (e.g., orientation: 'horizontal' | 'vertical') should be added to the ExcalidrawTextElement interface. User Interface (UI): Introduce a toggle button or a dropdown menu in the properties sidebar/panel dedicated to text orientation. Rendering: When the orientation is set to vertical, the text element should be rendered with a 90-degree rotation (or -90 degrees) within the canvas rendering logic. Constraint Check: The implementation must ensure that the bounding box calculation and the text editing functionality remain accurate and intuitive even when the text is rotated. Example / Mockup I am happy to help contribute to the implementation of this feature if it is approved.

title: Outdated document to createRoot
body:
In case someone runs into it:

https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/integration#browser

refers to `const root = ReactDOM.createRoot(excalidrawWrapper);` to create the root. This isn't the right way to do it.
Instead it is `const root = createRoot(excalidrawWrapper);` using `import { createRoot } from "https://esm.sh/react-dom@19.0.0/client";`

title: Action predicate method doesn't work
body:
The [types ](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7cc31ac64a9ec3f9ce5bc3a09cd3716c2eb07370/packages/excalidraw/actions/types.ts) describing the action do not specify what the fields are specifically responsible for. 
But after researching the code, I came to the conclusion that the "[predicate](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7cc31ac64a9ec3f9ce5bc3a09cd3716c2eb07370/packages/excalidraw/actions/types.ts#L185)" method is most likely responsible for the display condition of the action.
If that's really the case, then it's not working at all right now. The buttons are hidden via `display: none;`, but are still included in the DOM and the `predicate` method is never called.

title: Feature Request: Option to conduct surveys in collaboration mode
body: ### **Summary** Excalidraw currently lacks native survey or polling capabilities. This request proposes introducing a survey feature that allows surveys to be created, managed, and used during collaboration sessions to support quick decisions, structured input, and lightweight voting. ### **Frontend Requirements** - Sidebar integration (next to comments, presentation, search) for quick access - Toast notifications for key events (survey created, closed, reopened) ### **Functional Requirements** - Multiple answer options per survey - Survey timer, including automatic closing and optional reopening - Result display in percentages and absolute numbers - Single‑choice and multiple‑choice modes - Automatic or manual closing (by the organizer) - Delete surveys when needed - Multiple surveys running at the same time

title: Enable zoom in/out using mouse wheel while pressing the middle mouse button
body:
In the current version of Excalidraw, pressing the mouse middle button (scroll wheel) and scrolling up or down does not trigger any action. This feels like a missed opportunity, as many design and drawing tools use this interaction for zooming.

I propose adding a new feature where:

* **Pressing the middle mouse button + scrolling up** → Zoom in
* **Pressing the middle mouse button + scrolling down** → Zoom out

This would provide a more intuitive and efficient workflow, especially for users coming from other design tools.

### Current Behavior

* Pressing the mouse wheel and scrolling up or down does nothing.

### Expected Behavior

* Middle mouse button + scroll up should zoom in.
* Middle mouse button + scroll down should zoom out.

### Why this feature is useful

* Improves usability and workflow speed.
* Matches behavior found in many popular drawing/design applications.
* Does not interfere with existing shortcuts.
author: PATILYASHH

title: Enable `https://app.powerbi.com/reportEmbed` URLs for Web Embeds
body:
## Problem Statement

Excalidraw’s current Web Embed / iframe handling appears to reject or block `https://app.powerbi.com/reportEmbed` URLs (Power BI’s official embed endpoint). This prevents users from embedding Power BI reports directly into Excalidraw diagrams, even though these URLs are explicitly designed by Microsoft for safe iframe embedding.

As a result, users who rely on Power BI for dashboards, analytics, and reporting cannot integrate live, interactive reports into Excalidraw diagrams and workflows.

## Why This Matters

Supporting `app.powerbi.com/reportEmbed` would:

- Enable live, interactive BI dashboards inside Excalidraw diagrams
- Improve Excalidraw’s usefulness for architecture documentation, data workflows, and analytics-heavy use cases
- Align Excalidraw with modern documentation practices that combine diagrams with live data
- Leverage an official, iframe-safe embed endpoint provided by Microsoft

Power BI is widely used in enterprise, education, and data-driven organizations, where embedding dashboards alongside system and process diagrams is a common requirement.

## Technical Details

Power BI provides a dedicated embed endpoint that is specifically designed for iframe embedding and is already supported in many documentation and collaboration platforms.

At present, Excalidraw appears to block or reject this domain or URL pattern in its embed allowlist or validation logic.

## Proposed Change

- Allow `https://app.powerbi.com/reportEmbed` as a supported source in Excalidraw’s Web Embed / iframe system
- Preferably whitelist `app.powerbi.com` (or narrowly the `reportEmbed` path) while keeping the existing security model intact

## Security Considerations

This request does not propose allowing arbitrary iframes. It is limited to a single, well-known embed provider that already operates under strict CSP and embed policies. Power BI embeds are commonly accepted in platforms such as Confluence, Notion, and SharePoint.

If there are specific security constraints that prevent this, guidance on acceptable alternatives would be appreciated.

## User Impact

Without this support, users must rely on static screenshots or exports of reports, which quickly become outdated, or manage dashboards separately from Excalidraw diagrams.

With this support, Excalidraw can function as a unified workspace combining system diagrams with live, interactive data.

## Willingness to Help

If pointers to the relevant embed validation or allowlist logic are available, I am happy to assist with testing or validation.

title: Please change "All your data is stored locally in your browser". It's misleading.
body:
The happy-looking text "All your data is stored locally in your browser", which is prominently displayed in the UI, seems to be offering reassurance to the user that their data is stored automatically, and that manual saving is not required.

In fact, it should be interpreted as a **WARNING**. While saving *is* performed automatically, drawings are ONLY saved to browser storage, which can be cleared through a variety of actions, many of which are outside the control of the user.

Please change that text to something that cannot be misinterpreted, such as "Your data is saved automatically, but you should also explicitly save your work to a file regularly, since the auto-saves are stored in web browser storage, which is transitory and may be cleared without any warning if your browser closes, if you clear your browser cache, if your browser is updated, if the Docker container running Excalidraw stops, if the Docker host operating system is restarted, and so on."

title: Add support for opening .excalidraw.png and .excalidraw.svg files using the Excalidraw PWA
body:
Please add support for opening `.excalidraw.png` and `.excalidraw.svg` files using the Excalidraw PWA. These file formats are created by Excalidraw when the "Export image..." ➜ "Embed scene" option is enabled.

Since Excalidraw already has internal support for these, fixing this is simple: we just need to update the PWA manifest to accept these file-types.

**Impact:** By default, programs such as Finder refuse to open these files with Excalidraw because the mime-type is not included in the PWA manifest file. Even though Excalidraw nows how to interpret the file type, opening them is a hassle.

**Workaround:** It's currently possible to open these file types with Excalidraw's "Open" button, by dragging the file into the Excalidraw window, or by digging through the OS options (e.g. for macOS Finder or Windows File Explorer) to open the file using an unsupported app.

author: LowByteFox

title: Bug: 2 scenarios where arrow elements are not updated
body:
# Description
There appears to be two cases where arrow bindings do not update until the affected elements are manually moved:

## 1. Distribute action on elements with bound elements
- When distribution is applied to elements with arrow(s) bound to them, the arrows do not render in the correct position until the elements are moved.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15f01820-f249-445f-92f5-1dfae4eb25e6

## 2. When editing bound text exceeds its associated container's height
- When text bound to a container extends beyond the container's height, the associated arrow elements similarly fail to render correctly unless the container is moved.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6b4123f4-d802-46e1-8b13-033e3965fbd2

## Potential fix/areas of interest
For both cases the `updateBoundElements` method seems most applicable.

- The distribute action currently uses `newElementWith`. Historically, this was also used in the align action, but was later replaced with the more current combination of `mutateElement` and `updateBoundElements` methods. (see PR #8998)
- The Plan would be to follow similarly and replace the `newElementWith` to mirror the align implementation.
- For the bound text overflow case, I've located the relevant code path and plan to add `updateBoundElements` after `mutateElement` is applied.

@anviik and I discovered these while looking into a related issue with bound text. We're actively working on a PR to address both these scenarios

title: Feature Request: Global overhaul of mermaid-to-excalidraw styling (including Subgraph support)
body:
Hi Excalidraw team,

I am a developer using Excalidraw via the Obsidian plugin. I've noticed that the current conversion from Mermaid code to Excalidraw elements has a significant limitation regarding **styling**, particularly with **subgraphs**.

### The Problem

When using the native converter, custom styles applied to containers (subgraphs) are currently ignored. While `classDef` for individual nodes works well, the `style` directive for subgraphs is lost during the conversion.

Furthermore, the general mapping of styles from code to canvas is limited, creating "flat" diagrams that require extensive manual editing to leverage Excalidraw’s full potential.

### Use Case & Productivity

I frequently use **AI to generate complex Mermaid code** for educational and architectural purposes. Moving from AI-generated code to a visual canvas is a massive productivity boost, but only if the visual hierarchy and intent (colors, dashed lines, groupings) are preserved.

Improving the parser to handle global styling would allow users to create expressive, information-dense diagrams and mind maps significantly faster, without losing Mermaid's ability to convey meaning through visual styling.

### Reproducible Example

The background colors and border styles for `SG_RED` and `SG_BLUE` are lost when importing this code:

```
graph TD
    %% Node styles work fine
    classDef criticalNode fill:#ff5252,stroke:#b71c1c,color:#fff
    
    subgraph SG_RED ["CRITICAL MODULE"]
        Node01["Important Service"]:::criticalNode
    end

    subgraph SG_BLUE ["DASHED BORDER MODULE"]
        Node02["Feature Module"]
    end

    %% THESE SUBGRAPH STYLES ARE CURRENTLY IGNORED DURING CONVERSION:
    style SG_RED fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px
    style SG_BLUE fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 10 5

```

### Proposed Improvements

I propose enhancing the mapping logic globally to capture more attributes from the Mermaid parser:

* **Containers/Subgraphs**: Map `fill` to `backgroundColor` and ensure proper z-index layering.
* **Borders**: Map `stroke-dasharray` to `strokeStyle: "dashed"` and `stroke-width` to Excalidraw’s native stroke width.
* **Aesthetics**: Better integration with Excalidraw's styling capabilities, such as fill styles (solid vs. hachure) and opacity.

This enhancement would bridge the gap between structured code and the flexible, high-quality visual thinking that Excalidraw is known for.

Thank you for considering this improvement!

title: Request: Whitelist tana.pub for embedding
body:
## Description

I would like to request that `tana.pub` be whitelisted for embedding in Excalidraw.

## Use Case

[Tana](https://tana.inc/) is a knowledge management and note-taking tool that allows users to publish their work via `tana.pub`. Whitelisting this domain would enable users to embed their published Tana content directly into Excalidraw diagrams, creating a seamless workflow between these two powerful tools.

## Benefits

- Enhanced integration between Excalidraw's visual thinking capabilities and Tana's knowledge management features
- Better support for users who work with both infinite canvas (Excalidraw) and structured knowledge graphs (Tana)
- Improved productivity for users building visual representations of their knowledge bases

## Additional Context

Tana is a legitimate productivity tool with a growing user base, and `tana.pub` is their official public sharing domain.

Thank you for considering this request!

title: Korean locale (ko-KR) for Excalidraw Web UI
body:
### Description 
---

Hello Excalidraw team,
Thank you for building and maintaining Excalidraw — it’s a fantastic tool and I use it regularly.

I noticed that the Excalidraw web app (https://excalidraw.com/
) supports many languages, but Korean (한국어) is not currently available in the UI language options.

Korea is a market where English usage is relatively low, so an English-only interface can be a real barrier for many users—especially students, educators, and non-technical users who would otherwise benefit from Excalidraw.

###  Request
---

Would you consider adding Korean (ko / ko-KR) language support to the web app UI on https://excalidraw.com/?

### Why this matters
---

Lower the language barrier: A Korean UI would make the product more accessible to a wider audience in Korea.

Improve adoption: Reducing friction for first-time users can help increase usage and retention.

Consistency with existing i18n support: Since many locales are already supported, adding Korean would be a meaningful addition.

### Willingness to help
---

If there is an existing translation workflow (e.g., locale JSON files, Crowdin/Weblate, etc.), I’d be happy to contribute Korean translations or help review them.
Please let me know the preferred process.

Thank you for considering this request.

author: ChristinaZhang-zz

title: Path resolution for index.css fails by default when custom condition is not set
body:
Currently, path resolution for `index.css` only works if the build tool sets either the `development` or `production` custom condition. While most build tools do automatically do so and its use is recommended by Node.js, after all it is non-standard and cannot be expected from all tools. As the `default` fallback is not defined for `index.css`, path resolution fails for the build tools that do not set these conditions.

From the `package.json` exports entry for `index.css`:

```json
"./index.css": {
      "development": "./dist/dev/index.css",
      "production": "./dist/prod/index.css"
},
```

In the absence of the `development` or `production` conditions, path resolution falls back to the `default` condition, which is absent. Path resolution fails and throws an error.

An example from the build tool I am using which uses ESBuild under the hood:

```
[ERROR] Could not resolve "@excalidraw/excalidraw/index.css" [plugin es]

    src/tw-excalidraw/components/App.tsx:6:7:
      6 │ import '@excalidraw/excalidraw/index.css';
        ╵        ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

  You can mark the path "@excalidraw/excalidraw/index.css" as external to exclude it from the
  bundle, which will remove this error and leave the unresolved path in the bundle.
```

The reproduction is just to attempt to build Excalidraw under any build tool that does not set these conditions by default. The intended behaviour is that the path to `index.css` correctly resolves.

Notably, the JavaScript exports work, as `default` is defined. It is just the CSS that does not work:

```json
".": {
      "types": "./dist/types/excalidraw/index.d.ts",
      "development": "./dist/dev/index.js",
      "production": "./dist/prod/index.js",
      "default": "./dist/prod/index.js"
}
```

My proposed solution is to set a default condition for `index.css` as well.

```json
"./index.css": {
      "development": "./dist/dev/index.css",
      "production": "./dist/prod/index.css",
      "default": "./dist/prod/index.css"
},
```

From my testing, there does not seem to be negative impacts of doing so; it is already set for the JavaScript anyway, so this change would not be inconsistent.

title: [FEATURE REQUEST] - Auto-pan canvas while dragging selections (edge scrolling)
body:
## Description
When moving a selection, it would be really helpful if the canvas could **auto-pan when the cursor reaches the edge of the viewport** (edge scrolling).

**Example:** I have a drawing I want to move to a location that’s currently off-screen to the right. I’d like to select it, start dragging, and then when I drag toward/into the right edge of the window, the view should pan right so I can continue dragging into that area.

## Current workarounds
- Zoom out until both the source and destination are visible, then drag.
- Cut/copy the selection, pan to the destination, then paste.
author: AdamLinCodes

title: [FEATURE REQUEST] - Right-click hold to pan (temporary Hand/drag mode)
body:
## Description
It would be great if **right-click + hold** could temporarily enter **drag/pan (Hand) mode** to move around the canvas.

Right now:
- **Left-click** behaves like select mode (select items, box-select, resize, move selections, etc.).
- To **pan** the canvas, I need to switch to Hand mode (press `H` or click the Hand tool in the toolbar), then switch back.

I find the mode-switching a bit unintuitive and slow for frequent navigation.

## Proposed behavior
- **Right-click tap**: keep existing behavior (open the context menu for actions like paste, etc.).
- **Right-click hold + drag**: temporarily pan the canvas (like Hand mode).
- **On release**: return to the previous tool automatically (typically select).

**Note:** This behaviour is very common in other diagramming apps such LucidChart

title: Add Escape-to-cancel for in-progress pointer drawing (before element is created)
body:
### Problem

  When a user accidentally starts drawing (for example, wrong tool selected or misclick on canvas), there is currently no way to cancel mid-creation.
  They must finish creating the element first and then delete it.

  This adds friction and breaks expected drawing flow, especially during fast sketching sessions.

  ### Why this is needed

  Most modern whiteboard/design tools allow Esc to cancel an in-progress draw action.
  Users expect this behavior as a standard interaction pattern. Without it, Excalidraw feels less forgiving and slower for quick edits.

  ### Current behavior

  - User starts pointer-based creation.
  - Pressing Esc does not cancel that in-progress creation.
  - User has to complete the shape/stroke, then undo/delete.

  ### Expected behavior

  - While an element is being created via pointer interaction, pressing Esc should immediately cancel creation.
  - No partial/temporary element should remain in the scene.
  - Interaction state and pointer listeners should be cleaned up safely.

  ### Scope

  Apply cancel behavior to pointer-based creation flows (e.g. drag-create shapes, drag arrows, free draw).

  ### Important compatibility note

  For multi-point linear creation mode (e.g. multi-point arrow/line), existing Esc behavior to finish creation should remain unchanged.

  ### Acceptance criteria

  1. Pressing Esc during drag-create of a shape cancels creation and leaves no new element.
  2. Pressing Esc during free draw cancels current stroke creation and leaves no new element.
  3. Pointer interaction state is reset cleanly (no stuck selection/ghost state).
  4. Multi-point arrow/line mode still uses Esc to finish creation (no regression).
  5. Automated tests cover these cases.

title: Undo button does not revert "open file" operation
body:
**Description**
When users open a file while already having some content on the canvas, the content in the file will overwrite anything existing. If the users click the undo button to undo the “open file” operation and want to restore the original work, the undo button does not remove the content from the file. It only restores the original content while preserving things from the newly opened file, so that both remain on the canvas. If the users continue to click the undo button, it only undoes the previous changes to the original content. The content from the file remains on the canvas, and the users must manually erase its content piece by piece with the eraser to remove it from the canvas. This does not meet user expectations and can be extremely time-consuming.

**Reproduce Steps**
1. Draw more than one stroke on the canvas.
2. Click the first button in the column bar on the left to open a previously saved file from Excalidraw.
3. After opening the file, keep clicking the undo button in the lower left corner of the screen to observe the order in which the content on the canvas is cleared.

title: [DOCKER] Build failures: Node version mismatch (`marked@16` requires Node >=20) and PWA precache size limit exceeded
body:
**Description**  
Running `docker compose up`/`docker compose build` can fail in two consecutive build steps on current `master`.

## Environment
- Docker Compose v2
- Build target: `Dockerfile` build stage
- Monorepo uses Yarn 1 workspace install

## Steps to reproduce
1. Clone the repo.
2. Run:
   - `docker compose build --progress=plain --no-cache`
   - or `docker compose up`

## Actual behavior
Build fails during dependency install and/or app build:

1. Install step failure:
- `marked@16.4.2: The engine "node" is incompatible with this module. Expected version ">= 20". Got "18.20.8"`

2. Build step failure:
- Workbox/Vite PWA error:
- `maximumFileSizeToCacheInBytes` default/too low for generated `assets/index-*.js` (~2.52 MB)
- `yarn build:app:docker` exits with code 1

## Expected behavior
Docker image builds successfully with `docker compose build` and container starts with `docker compose up`.

## Notes
- `package.json` currently declares `engines.node >=18`, but dependency resolution now requires Node 20+.
- Current PWA precache file-size limit is below the generated main bundle size in production Docker builds.

## Proposed fixes
1. Use Node 20+ in Docker build stage.
2. Align `engines.node` with actual requirement (Node 20+).
3. Increase Workbox precache limit (or reduce main bundle size / split chunks) so `build:app:docker` is stable.
author: rafaself

title: [Language] Portuguese (`pt-BR` / `pt-PT`) not shown in language selector due to completion threshold filter
body:
**Description**
Portuguese locales exist in the codebase (`pt-BR.json`, `pt-PT.json`), but neither appears in the app language selector.

The selector uses `languages` from `packages/excalidraw/i18n.ts`, where locales are filtered by translation completion:

- `COMPLETION_THRESHOLD = 85`
- `percentages[lang.code] >= COMPLETION_THRESHOLD`

Current values in `packages/excalidraw/locales/percentages.json` are:

- `pt-BR: 84`
- `pt-PT: 84`

So both locales are filtered out before rendering.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Start Excalidraw app in development mode.
2. Open the main menu.
3. Open the language dropdown.
4. Search for Portuguese options.

**Actual behavior**
- `Português Brasileiro` (`pt-BR`) and `Português` (`pt-PT`) are not present.

**Expected behavior**
- Portuguese should be visible in the language selector (or there should be explicit product decision/docs explaining why it is hidden).

**Likely cause**
- Threshold/filter logic in `packages/excalidraw/i18n.ts` excludes locales below 85%, and both Portuguese variants are currently at 84%.

**Possible fixes**
**1. Complete missing translation keys in `pt-BR` and `pt-PT` so coverage reaches `>=85`, then regenerate `percentages.json`.**
2. Lower `COMPLETION_THRESHOLD` from `85` to `84`.
3. Add an allowlist/exception for strategic locales if desired.

title: [BUG] Eyedropper applies wrong color in dark theme
body:
## Summary
In dark theme, the eyedropper can preview the hovered color correctly, but the color that gets applied to element/background may differ (notably black/white-like swaps).

***Video 1 - Reproducing the bug***

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8edd356a-6f49-4371-a670-81d59b74a074

## User Impact
- Picking a color from canvas does not reliably apply the same visual color.
- Most noticeable with very dark/light colors.
- Reduces trust in eyedropper behavior.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open Excalidraw.
2. Switch to dark theme.
3. Open a color picker (e.g. Background/Stroke).
4. Activate eyedropper.
5. Hover over a high-contrast color (especially near black/white tones).
6. Click to apply.
7. Compare hovered visual color vs final applied visual color.

## Expected Behavior
- The applied color should match what was visually sampled under cursor.

## Actual Behavior
- In dark theme, applied color can differ from sampled visual color.

## Root Cause
Dark theme rendering uses a color transform (`applyDarkModeFilter`) for display. Eyedropper sampled already-transformed pixels from the canvas and persisted them directly. When rendering again in dark mode, color transformation was effectively applied twice from a visual standpoint, causing mismatch.

## Fix Implemented
- Added reverse transformation helper to undo dark-mode filter for sampled colors before persisting:
  - `removeDarkModeFilter(...)` in [packages/common/src/colors.ts](packages/common/src/colors.ts)
- Updated eyedropper apply path to use reverse transform only when app theme is dark:
  - [packages/excalidraw/components/EyeDropper.tsx](packages/excalidraw/components/EyeDropper.tsx)
- Kept preview behavior aligned with actual sampled pixel under cursor.

## Regression Tests Added
- Reverse-filter behavior tests:
  - [packages/common/src/colors.test.ts](packages/common/src/colors.test.ts)
- Eyedropper integration tests (including dark-theme apply flow):
  - [packages/excalidraw/tests/eyeDropper.test.tsx](packages/excalidraw/tests/eyeDropper.test.tsx)

## Validation
Executed in Docker (Node 20):
- Targeted tests passed.
- Full app test suite passed:
  - `97` test files passed
  - `1210` tests passed (`46` skipped, `1` todo)

## Notes
- Existing unrelated warnings/log noise from pre-existing tests were not modified as requested.
author: rafaself

title: SVG files with .excalidraw extension produce cryptic "Error: invalid file"
body:
## Description

When a file with `.excalidraw` extension contains SVG content instead of JSON (e.g., from an SVG export that was saved with the wrong extension), excalidraw.com shows the unhelpful error "Error: invalid file" instead of diagnosing the actual problem.

This happens because `normalizeFile()` in `blob.ts` sets the MIME type to `application/vnd.excalidraw+json` based solely on the `.excalidraw` extension, which causes `parseFileContents()` to skip the SVG decoding path and attempt to JSON-parse the XML content.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a drawing on excalidraw.com
2. Export to SVG (with or without "Embed scene")
3. Save/rename the file with `.excalidraw` extension (instead of `.svg` or `.excalidraw.svg`)
4. Try to open the file on excalidraw.com

## Expected Behavior

Excalidraw should either:
1. Detect that the file content is SVG despite the `.excalidraw` extension and attempt SVG decoding
2. Show a helpful error message like: "This file appears to be an SVG image, not an Excalidraw scene file. If this SVG was exported from Excalidraw, try renaming it to `.excalidraw.svg` and ensure 'Embed scene' was enabled during export."

## Actual Behavior

The generic "Error: invalid file" is shown with no indication of what's wrong.

## Root Cause

The loading pipeline in `packages/excalidraw/data/blob.ts`:

1. `normalizeFile()` (line ~474): sees `.excalidraw` extension → sets MIME type to `MIME_TYPES.excalidraw` (`application/vnd.excalidraw+json`)
2. `parseFileContents()` (line ~33): checks `blob.type === MIME_TYPES.png` and `blob.type === MIME_TYPES.svg` — neither matches `MIME_TYPES.excalidraw`, so it reads content as raw text
3. `loadSceneOrLibraryFromBlob()` (line ~148): `JSON.parse()` fails on SVG/XML content
4. The catch at line ~188 throws `new Error("Error: invalid file")`

The SVG content detection in `parseFileContents()` only triggers when `blob.type === MIME_TYPES.svg` (`image/svg+xml`), which is never true for files with `.excalidraw` extension.

## Suggested Fix

In `parseFileContents()` or `loadSceneOrLibraryFromBlob()`, if JSON parsing fails and the content starts with `<?xml` or `<svg`, try the SVG decoding path (`decodeSvgBase64Payload`) as a fallback. If that also fails (no embedded scene data), show a descriptive error message.

Alternatively, `normalizeFile()` could check the first few bytes of the file content (like it already does for images with `getActualMimeTypeFromImage()`) to detect SVG content regardless of extension.

## Environment

- excalidraw.com (as of March 2026)
- All browsers
- Related issues: #8444, #6751, #10105, #10370

title: Add .cursorrules file to improve Cursor IDE experience for contributors
body:
## Proposal: Add a `.cursorrules` file for Cursor IDE users

Hi\! I noticed Excalidraw does not have a `.cursorrules` file. A lot of contributors (and folks building on top of Excalidraw) use [Cursor IDE](https://cursor.sh), and a well-crafted `.cursorrules` file helps the AI assistant understand your codebase conventions — reducing friction for new contributors significantly.

### What `.cursorrules` does

The `.cursorrules` file tells Cursor's AI assistant about your project's:
- Code style and naming conventions
- Component architecture patterns
- TypeScript usage patterns
- Testing approach
- Common pitfalls to avoid

This means anyone using Cursor to contribute gets AI completions that actually match your codebase conventions out of the box, rather than generic suggestions.

### What I'd suggest for Excalidraw

Given Excalidraw's React + TypeScript stack, a `.cursorrules` file could cover:
- Functional components with strict TypeScript (no `any`)
- The canvas/element model patterns specific to Excalidraw
- State management conventions
- Testing with Vitest
- Performance-sensitive rendering patterns

I maintain an open-source collection of `.cursorrules` files at https://github.com/survivorforge/cursor-rules — the React + TypeScript rules there could serve as a solid starting point, though ideally you would customize it to Excalidraw's specific conventions.

### Options

1. **I can submit a PR** with a `.cursorrules` file tailored to Excalidraw's patterns (I'd study the codebase first)
2. **You can adapt** the [react rules from our collection](https://github.com/survivorforge/cursor-rules/tree/main/rules/react) yourself
3. **No action needed** if you prefer not to add it — totally fine\!

This is a free community contribution with no strings attached. Happy to do the work if it's useful to the project.

title: Add “Full Screen” Button for Excalidraw on Android (Chrome – Tablets & Mobile)
body:
**Description**

When using Excalidraw on **Android devices with Google Chrome**, especially on **tablets**, the drawing area is limited by the browser UI and surrounding interface elements. This significantly reduces the usable workspace for drawing and note-taking.

A **dedicated full-screen button** inside Excalidraw would greatly improve the experience by allowing users to maximize the canvas and hide browser/interface elements.

This feature would be particularly useful for:

* Android tablets used for **teaching, note-taking, and diagrams**
* Mobile users who want a **larger drawing canvas**
* Users working with a **stylus or finger input**

**Proposed Feature**

Add a **“Full Screen” button** in the Excalidraw interface that:

* Uses the **Fullscreen API** to expand the canvas to the entire screen.
* Hides browser UI elements where possible.
* Works on **Android Chrome (mobile and tablet)**.
* Allows users to **toggle full screen on/off easily**.

**Possible Implementation**

Use the browser Fullscreen API:

```javascript
document.documentElement.requestFullscreen();
```

And allow exiting with:

```javascript
document.exitFullscreen();
```

The button could be placed near existing canvas controls (zoom, view options, etc.).

**Expected Benefits**

* Much larger drawing space on tablets and phones
* Better usability for **education and diagramming**
* Improved experience for **stylus users**
* Cleaner interface during presentations or explanations

**Additional Context**

Excalidraw is increasingly used on **Android tablets in educational contexts**, where maximizing screen space is important. A simple full-screen toggle would make the tool significantly more comfortable to use on mobile devices.

title: ndroid Chrome: “Save to…” can’t overwrite the currently opened .excalidraw file (existing file greyed out, forced to rename)
body:
### Summary
On Android (OxygenOS 15) using Chrome `145.0.7632.159`, Excalidraw cannot save back to the same `.excalidraw` file after opening it. When invoking **Save to…** again, the original file is shown **greyed out** in the picker and Excalidraw forces choosing a new filename.

This happens even when the file is stored in the **Downloads** directory. On the same device/location, **draw.io** can overwrite the same file, so this looks like an Excalidraw handle/save-flow bug/compat issue.

### Environment
- OS: Android (OxygenOS 15)
- Browser: Chrome `145.0.7632.159`
- Excalidraw version: <!-- fill in from Excalidraw main menu (bottom) -->
- Running as: <!-- browser tab or installed PWA? -->

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open https://excalidraw.com on Android Chrome.
2. Create a drawing.
3. Use **Main menu → Save to…** and save as `test.excalidraw` into **Downloads**.
4. Use **Main menu → Open** and open `test.excalidraw` from **Downloads**.
5. Make any change.
6. Use **Main menu → Save to…** again and navigate to **Downloads**.

### Actual result
- The existing `test.excalidraw` file is **greyed out** in the save picker.
- Excalidraw cannot overwrite it and forces saving under a **new name**.

### Expected result
- After opening an `.excalidraw` file, Excalidraw should retain a writable file handle and allow saving changes back to the **same file** (overwrite), without forcing a new filename.

### Notes / comparison
- Reproducible even in **Downloads** (not only on SD card).
- On the same phone and directory, **draw.io** can overwrite the same filename successfully.
- This suggests Excalidraw may not be obtaining/persisting the `FileSystemFileHandle` on Android Chrome (falls back to “Save as” behavior).

title: [Feature Request] REST API for Excalidraw+ — enable AI agents, CI/CD, and embedding workflows
body:
## Problem

There's no programmatic way to interact with Excalidraw+ diagrams. The only option is the web UI, which makes it impossible to integrate Excalidraw+ into automated workflows.

## Use cases

**AI agent workflows:** AI coding assistants (Claude, Copilot, etc.) can already generate `.excalidraw` JSON programmatically. With a REST API, they could create and update diagrams directly in Excalidraw+ — keeping architecture diagrams, flowcharts, and system maps up to date as code evolves.

**Docs embedding:** With stable API-backed URLs for diagrams, we could embed live Excalidraw diagrams in markdown docs, READMEs, wikis, and internal knowledge bases that stay in sync with the source of truth.

**CI/CD integration:** Auto-generate or update diagrams as part of build pipelines — e.g., regenerate an architecture diagram when services change.

## What we'd need

- **CRUD endpoints** for diagrams (create, read, update, delete)
- **Collection management** (list, add to, remove from collections)
- **API key authentication**
- **Stable URLs** for embedding (ideally with SVG/PNG render endpoints)

## Context

The `@excalidraw/excalidraw` npm package and the official [excalidraw-mcp](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw-mcp) server show Excalidraw is already thinking about AI/developer workflows. A REST API for the hosted service would be the missing piece that ties it all together.

Related: #9119 (Members Administration API)

2. rotate the curved arrow to any non-zero angle
3. Deselect the arrow
4. zoom in and hover over the visually rendered path

title:
Mermaid import:
tags rendered as literal text instead of line breaks
body:
Hey there, I noticed that when importing Mermaid diagrams that use `
` tags for multi-line node labels, the `
` shows up as literal text in the rendered diagram instead of being converted to actual line breaks. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Paste or enter a Mermaid diagram using `
` in node labels: ``` graph TD A["User Registration
Process"] --> B["Validate Input
Data"] B --> C{"Is Data
Valid?"} C -->|Yes| D["Create User
Account"] ``` 2. The resulting Excalidraw elements show the raw `
` string in the text (e.g., `User Registration
Process`) instead of rendering it as two lines. ### Expected behavior The `
` tag should be converted to a newline so the text displays as: ``` User Registration Process ``` ### What's happening The `@excalidraw/mermaid-to-excalidraw` library returns skeleton elements where `text` and `label.text` still contain the literal `
` string. Excalidraw doesn't sanitize these before converting them to final elements, so the tags end up visible in the diagram. This affects both the clipboard paste path (`App.tsx`) and the Text-to-Diagram dialog (`TTDDialog/common.ts`). ### Environment Reproducible on latest `master` across browsers.

author: ricezs

title: Feature Request: Selection UI customization API (padding, hover state, CSS variables)
body:
# Feature Request: Selection UI customization API

## Summary

When embedding Excalidraw as a library, there's currently no way to customize the selection/transform UI rendering. We're building a design editor using Excalidraw as the canvas engine, and the hardcoded selection styling makes it difficult to match our target UX.

## Requested capabilities

### 1. Configurable selection padding

The selection bounding box is drawn with a fixed padding offset around the element bounds (currently hardcoded as `vl * 2` in the rendering code). For frame elements and other container types, we need zero padding — the selection outline should sit exactly on the element edge, not outside it.

**Proposed API** — a per-element-type or global override:
```ts
// Option A: appState-level default
appState.selectionPadding?: number // default: current value

// Option B: per-element override via callback
<Excalidraw
  getSelectionPadding={(element) => element.type === "frame" ? 0 : undefined}
/>
```

### 2. Hover state for elements (selection outline without handles)

There's no built-in way to show a hover preview on elements — the blue outline that appears when the cursor is over an element, before clicking to select. In design tools like Figma, hovering shows the selection outline without resize handles or rotation handle. Currently embedders have to implement this as a separate HTML overlay, which creates visual inconsistency with the native canvas-drawn selection.

**Proposed API:**
```ts
// Expose a hover state in appState
appState.hoveredElementId?: string

// Or a callback
<Excalidraw
  onPointerUpdate={({ pointer }) => {
    // Already exists, but no visual feedback for hover
  }}
  hoveredElementId={elementId} // renders selection outline without handles
/>
```

### 3. CSS variable for selection padding

Similar to `--color-selection` (which already works and is great), a `--selection-padding` CSS variable would allow embedders to control the gap without needing API changes.

## Use case

Building a design editor with Excalidraw as the canvas engine, using native `type: "frame"` elements for artboards. The current selection UI gaps make it visually inconsistent between a custom hover overlay (which sits on the element edge) and the native selection (which has padding). Design tools like Figma, Sketch, and others render the selection outline flush against the element bounds for frames.

## Current workarounds

- **Selection color**: `--color-selection` CSS variable works perfectly for overriding the default purple.
- **Selection padding**: No workaround available. Cannot override the hardcoded padding.
- **Hover state**: Rendering a separate HTML `<div>` overlay using `sceneCoordsToViewportCoords` to approximate the position, but it's a different rendering pipeline (HTML vs canvas) and will never be pixel-perfect with the native selection.

## References

- Selection rendering: `renderSelectionElement()` in `renderer/renderElement`
- Padding constant: used as `(t.padding ?? vl * 2) / o.zoom.value` in the selection box renderer
- Zero padding is already implemented for image elements (cropping mode) via `isImageElement()` check — extending this pattern to other element types would be straightforward

title: feat: (with impl) Ability to stack shapes to show multiple copies -- great for system design interviews
body: ### Problem When creating system design diagrams, a common pattern is showing that a service is distributed/replicated across multiple nodes -- typically drawn as stacked offset copies of the same shape: The way this is currently done requires manually duplicating the shape, repositioning copies, removing text from each copy, and grouping everything together. It's tedious and error-prone and honestly a big time waste ### Proposed Solution with Impl A single action (`Ctrl/Cmd+J` or new toolbar button) that: - creates 2 offset copies behind shape (rectangle, rhombus or circle) - stripes text from the copies -- only the front element keeps the text - groups them together so they move as one unit - preserves all styles - double clicking the stack opens the text editor in the front most shape, regardless of where you click within overlapping area -- this makes sure you can't add text to copies behind - doesn't break any arrows pointing from it or towards it ### Use case This is most common in system design to show scale ### Implementation PR will with implementation and new tests: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/10969 ### Full Video with Audio **See the full video here --** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5wxA1oRrtoU ### Short video: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4500a208-3dab-4135-976c-71225b64b441
